Hooks.Name: calcview
Hooks.Description: Called on a HUD when you are linked to it. Return a table with origin, angles and fov fields to change your camera view.

Parameters.Name: player
Parameters.Description: The local player.

Parameters.Name: origin
Parameters.Description: The default location of the camera.

Parameters.Name: angles
Parameters.Description: The default angles of the camera.

Parameters.Name: fov
Parameters.Description: The default field of view of the camera.

ReturnValues.Description: A table describing the view to render from.

Hooks.Name: canexitvehicle
Hooks.Description:
Called when a player is attempting to leave an engine vehicle (e.g. a chair). 
This hook is executed only if the player has granted the CONTROL_VEHICLE_INPUT permission and the vehicle belongs to the instance owner.

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player that is attempting to leave the vehicle.

Parameters.Name: vehicle
Parameters.Description: The vehicle the player is currently sat in.

ReturnValues.Description: False to block the exit.

Hooks.Name: chatboxclosed
Hooks.Description:
Called on a HUD when you are linked to it and the chatbox has been closed. 
This requires the READ_CHATBOX_STATE permission.

Hooks.Name: chatboxopened
Hooks.Description:
Called on a HUD when you are linked to it and the chatbox has been opened. 
This requires the READ_CHATBOX_STATE permission.

Parameters.Name: mode
Parameters.Description: The mode the chatbox was opened with.

Hooks.Name: chattextchanged
Hooks.Description:
Called on a HUD when you are linked to it and the text in the chatbox input has changed. 
This requires the READ_CHATBOX_STATE permission.

Parameters.Name: text
Parameters.Description: The contents of the chatbox input.

Hooks.Name: checkfiledownload
Hooks.Description:
Called when a file is requested by a client from an asynchronous include. 
Return false to deny the download. If no hook callback returns a value, the download will be allowed.

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player attempting to download the file.

Parameters.Name: fileName
Parameters.Description: The name of the file being downloaded (as a full path).

ReturnValues.Description: True to allow the download, false to deny.

Hooks.Name: checktricorderoverride
Hooks.Description: Called when a player is attempting to use their tricorder/scanner on the Starfall entity with this hook.

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player attempting to use their tricorder/scanner.

Parameters.Name: tricorder
Parameters.Description: The tricorder/scanner being used to override.

Hooks.Name: dtvarchanged
Hooks.Description: Called when a DTVar value is changed (if change detection was enabled by net.listenForDTVarChanges() or net.listenForAllDTVarChanges()).

Parameters.Name: type
Parameters.Description: The type of DTVar that has changed.

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: The index under the given type of the DTVar that has changed.

Parameters.Name: oldValue
Parameters.Description: The previous value for the DTVar.

Parameters.Name: newValue
Parameters.Description: The new value for the DTVar.

Hooks.Name: entityremoved
Hooks.Description: Called when an entity is removed.

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: The entity removed.

Hooks.Name: entitytakedamage
Hooks.Description:
Called when an entity takes damage through the server's damage system. 
NOTE: This hook is sampled over time, and will not report every damage event. To track exact damage amounts, periodically poll the health of a given entity.

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: The entity that took damage.

Parameters.Name: inflictor
Parameters.Description: The weapon that caused the damage.

Parameters.Name: attacker
Parameters.Description: The owner of the weapon that caused the damage.

Parameters.Name: amount
Parameters.Description: The amount of damage taken.

Parameters.Name: pos
Parameters.Description: The position at which the damage occurred (in world space).

Hooks.Name: hudlink
Hooks.Description: Called when a player links to your HUD (clientside only).

Parameters.Name: player
Parameters.Description: The player linking.

Hooks.Name: hudshoulddraw
Hooks.Description:
Called on a HUD when you are linked to it. 
Return a value to deny the given HUD element from drawing. 
See: https://wiki.facepunch.com/gmod/HUD_Element_List 
In addition to the above, the following extra elements are defined: 
* LuaHudHealth 
* LuaHudAmmo 
* LuaHudDeathNotice

Parameters.Name: element
Parameters.Description: The string name of the HUD element requesting permission to draw.

Hooks.Name: hudunlink
Hooks.Description: Called when a player unlinks from your HUD (clientside only).

Parameters.Name: player
Parameters.Description: The player being unlinked.

Hooks.Name: input
Hooks.Description: Called when an input on a wired SF chip is written to

Parameters.Name: input
Parameters.Description: The input name

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: The value of the input

Hooks.Name: inputmouseapply
Hooks.Description: Called when mouse input is applied to the local player's view.

Parameters.Name: x
Parameters.Description: The amount of mouse movement along the x-axis this frame.

Parameters.Name: y
Parameters.Description: The amount of mouse movement along the y-axis this frame.

Parameters.Name: viewAngles
Parameters.Description: The view angles of the local player.

Hooks.Name: keypress
Hooks.Description: Called when a player presses an input button.

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player pressing the button.

Parameters.Name: key
Parameters.Description: The IN_ button.

Hooks.Name: keyrelease
Hooks.Description: Called when a player releases an input button.

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player releasing the button.

Parameters.Name: key
Parameters.Description: The IN_ button.

Hooks.Name: net
Hooks.Description:
Called when a network message is received. 
Note that network messages are stateful, and thus once read, data cannot be retrieved again. Having multiple callbacks for this hook will not work unless each is reading a separate part of the incoming message.

Parameters.Name: length
Parameters.Description: The length of the network message in bits.

Parameters.Name: player
Parameters.Description: The player that sent the network message (only on the server side).

Hooks.Name: networkentitycreated
Hooks.Description: Called when a networked entity has been created.

Parameters.Name: entity
Parameters.Description: The entity that has been created.

Hooks.Name: notifyshouldtransmit
Hooks.Description: Called when an entity enters or leaves the local player's PVS.

Parameters.Name: entity
Parameters.Description: The entity whose transmission state has changed.

Parameters.Name: shouldTransmit
Parameters.Description: Whether the entity is transmitting or not.

Hooks.Name: oncontextmenuclose
Hooks.Description: Called when the GMod context menu is closed (-menu_context keybind).

Hooks.Name: oncontextmenuopen
Hooks.Description: Called when the GMod context menu is opened (+menu_context keybind).

Hooks.Name: onentitycreated
Hooks.Description: Called when an entity is created.

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: The entity created.

Hooks.Name: onentityscanstarted
Hooks.Description:
Called when the local player starts scanning an entity with a scanning weapon. 
Note that this may be called multiple times with different entities as the player switches targets before "onentityscanstopped" is called. 
To detect scanning on players other than the instance owner, a linked HUD is required.

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player that is initiating the scan.

Parameters.Name: scanner
Parameters.Description: The scanner weapon being used to scan the entity.

Parameters.Name: target
Parameters.Description: The target entity that is being scanned.

Hooks.Name: onentityscanstopped
Hooks.Description:
Called when the local player stops scanning an entity with a scanning weapon. 
Note that this is called only when the player no longer has an active scanning target. If the player changes target, only "onentityscanstarted" will be called. 
To detect scanning on players other than the instance owner, a linked HUD is required.

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player that was scanning.

Parameters.Name: scanner
Parameters.Description: The scanner weapon that was being used to scan an entity.

Hooks.Name: onremove
Hooks.Description: Called when the chip is being removed or updated with new code.

Parameters.Name: reason
Parameters.Description: The reason for the instance shutdown (one of "UPDATING", "RESTARTING", "TERMINATING" or "REMOVING").

Hooks.Name: onscreensizechanged
Hooks.Description:
Called when the game's screen resolution has changed. 
Calling render.getScreenRes() will return the new size.

Parameters.Name: oldWidth
Parameters.Description: The old screen width in pixels.

Parameters.Name: oldHeight
Parameters.Description: The old screen height in pixels.

Hooks.Name: permissiongranted
Hooks.Description: Runs when a permission has been granted to this instance.

Parameters.Name: permission
Parameters.Description: The permission that has been granted.

Parameters.Name: player
Parameters.Description: The player that granted the permission. This is always ents.player() on the client.

Hooks.Name: permissionrevoked
Hooks.Description: Runs when a permission has been revoked from this instance.

Parameters.Name: permission
Parameters.Description: The permission that has been revoked.

Parameters.Name: player
Parameters.Description: The player that revoked the permission. This is always ents.player() on the client.

Hooks.Name: playerattempteduse
Hooks.Description:
Called when a player attempts to use an entity. 
This is called before prop protection checks, so the player may not be allowed to use the entity. Use the "playeruse" hook for successful use events.

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player that pressed their use key.

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: The entity that the player is attempting to use.

Hooks.Name: playerauthed
Hooks.Description: Called when a player's Steam ID has been confirmed.

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player that has been authed.

Parameters.Name: steamID
Parameters.Description: Their Steam ID.

Parameters.Name: uniqueID
Parameters.Description: Don't use it, it's not really unique thanks to Garry.

Hooks.Name: playerbindpress
Hooks.Description: Called when a player runs a command. Return true to block the command (only works for the chip's owner).

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player using the command (the local player).

Parameters.Name: bind
Parameters.Description: The command run, e.g "+attack"

Parameters.Name: pressed
Parameters.Description: Boolean value whether the bind was activated or deactivated.

Hooks.Name: playerbuttondown
Hooks.Description: Called when a player presses down any button.

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player pressing the button.

Parameters.Name: key
Parameters.Description: The KEY_ or MOUSE_ button.

Hooks.Name: playerbuttonup
Hooks.Description: Called when a player releases any button.

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player releasing the button.

Parameters.Name: key
Parameters.Description: The KEY_ or MOUSE_ button.

Hooks.Name: playerdeath
Hooks.Description: Called when a player dies.

Parameters.Name: victim
Parameters.Description: The player that died.

Parameters.Name: inflictor
Parameters.Description: The entity that caused the death.

Parameters.Name: attacker
Parameters.Description: The player that killed them.

Hooks.Name: playerdisconnected
Hooks.Description: Called when a player disconnects.

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player that left.

Hooks.Name: playerenteredvehicle
Hooks.Description: Called when a player enters an engine vehicle (e.g. a chair).

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player that entered the vehicle.

Parameters.Name: vehicle
Parameters.Description: The vehicle that was entered.

Parameters.Name: seatIndex
Parameters.Description: The index of the seat that was entered (starting from 0).

Hooks.Name: playerinitialspawn
Hooks.Description: Called when a player spawns for the first time.

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player that has spawned for the first time.

Hooks.Name: playerleavevehicle
Hooks.Description: Called when a player leaves an engine vehicle (e.g. a chair), after any exit animation is completed.

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player that left the vehicle.

Parameters.Name: vehicle
Parameters.Description: The vehicle that was left.

Hooks.Name: playerspawn
Hooks.Description: Called when a player spawns.

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player that has spawned.

Hooks.Name: playeruse
Hooks.Description: Called when a player successfully uses an entity after passing prop protection checks.

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player that pressed their use key.

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: The entity that is being used.

Hooks.Name: postduplicationpaste
Hooks.Description: Called when the duplication the chip is a part of has been fully restored.

Parameters.Name: createdEntities
Parameters.Description: A table keyed by entity index (at the point of saving), where each entity corresponds to an entity in the dupe.

Hooks.Name: postentitypaste
Hooks.Description:
Called when the chip has been restored. 
Note that other entities in the duplication may not have been fully restored yet. To wait until everything has been restored, use the "postduplicationpaste" hook.

Parameters.Name: data
Parameters.Description: The table of data you saved with PreEntityCopy.

Parameters.Name: createdEntities
Parameters.Description: A table keyed by entity index (at the point of saving), where each entity corresponds to an entity in the dupe.

Hooks.Name: preentitycopy
Hooks.Description:
Called when the chip is being copied. 
Note: don't try to store anything other than basic numbers, booleans and strings. To store an entity, store its entity index, then use the created entities lookup in PostEntityPaste using the saved index.

Parameters.Name: data
Parameters.Description: Add values to this table to be saved.

Hooks.Name: readcell
Hooks.Description: Called when a high speed device reads from a wired SF chip

Parameters.Name: address
Parameters.Description: The address requested

Hooks.Name: render
Hooks.Description:
Called when a frame is requested to be drawn. You may want to unhook from this if you don't need 
to render anything for a bit

Hooks.Name: renderoverlay
Hooks.Description:
Called on HUDs and emitters when they need to render their overlay (i.e. when the local player is looking at the chip). 
Adding a hook for this event will hide the default overlay. If you just want to disable the overlay from drawing, use starfall.showOverlay( false ).

Parameters.Name: overlayData
Parameters.Description: Metadata used to render the default overlay.

Hooks.Name: starfall_used
Hooks.Description: Called when a player presses use on the screen entity this instance is running on.

Parameters.Name: activator
Parameters.Description: The player that pressed use on the screen.

Parameters.Name: repeaterEntity
Parameters.Description: The repater screen entity that use was pressed on, if it was not pressed on the original screen.

Hooks.Name: think
Hooks.Description: Called once per game tick on the server and once per frame on the client.

Hooks.Name: tick
Hooks.Description: Called once per game tick on both the client and the server.

Hooks.Name: tricorderoverride
Hooks.Description: Called when a player successfully uses their tricorder/scanner on the Starfall entity with this hook.

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player that was successful in using their tricorder/scanner on your entity.

Parameters.Name: tricorder
Parameters.Description: The tricorder/scanner being used to override.

Hooks.Name: writecell
Hooks.Description: Called when a high speed device writes to a wired SF chip

Parameters.Name: address
Parameters.Description: The address written to

Parameters.Name: data
Parameters.Description: The data being written

Libraries.Name: Color
Libraries.Description: Colour helper functions.

Functions.Name: fromHSV
Functions.Description: Creates a colour object from the given HSV values

Parameters.Name: h
Parameters.Description: Hue, 0 - 1

Parameters.Name: s
Parameters.Description: Saturation, 0 - 1

Parameters.Name: v
Parameters.Description: Value, 0 - 1

Parameters.Name: a
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Alpha value, defaults to 255.

ReturnValues.Description: A new colour table with values converted into RGB from HSV.

Functions.Name: fromHex
Functions.Description: Creates a colour from a single hexadecimal value, either a string or a number.

Parameters.Name: hex
Parameters.Description: Either a string of the form "#FFFFFF", or a number of the form 0xFFFFFF

Parameters.Name: alpha
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Alpha value, defaults to 255.

ReturnValues.Description: A new colour table matching the hex value.

Libraries.Name: base64
Libraries.Description: Base 64 encoding library.

Functions.Name: decode
Functions.Description: Decodes the given string from base 64.

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to be decoded.

ReturnValues.Description: The decoded string.

Functions.Name: encode
Functions.Description: Encodes the given string into base 64.

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to be encoded.

ReturnValues.Description: The encoded string.

Libraries.Name: bit
Libraries.Description: LuaJIT's bit library. All inputs and results are expressed as signed 32-bit integers.

Functions.Name: arshift
Functions.Description: Returns the arithmetic right-shift of the given value.

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: The value to shift.

Parameters.Name: amount
Parameters.Description: The amount to shift by (between 0 and 31).

Functions.Name: band
Functions.Description: Returns the bitwise-and of the given values.

Parameters.Description: The values to combine using the bitwise-and operator.

Functions.Name: bnot
Functions.Description: Returns the the given value with every bit flipped.

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: The value to apply the bitwise-not operator to.

Functions.Name: bor
Functions.Description: Returns the bitwise-or of the given values.

Parameters.Description: The values to combine using the bitwise-or operator.

Functions.Name: bswap
Functions.Description:
Returns the given argument with its byte order swapped. 
This can be used to convert from big-endian to little-endian and vice-versa.

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: The value to swap the bytes of.

Functions.Name: bxor
Functions.Description: Returns the bitwise-xor of the given values.

Parameters.Description: The values to combine using the bitwise-xor operator.

Functions.Name: lshift
Functions.Description: Returns the logical left shift of the given value.

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: The value to shift.

Parameters.Name: amount
Parameters.Description: The amount to shift by (between 0 and 31).

Functions.Name: rol
Functions.Description:
Returns the bitwise left rotation of the given value. 
Unlike shifts, rotations insert bits that are shifted out of one side back into the other side.

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: The value to rotate.

Parameters.Name: amount
Parameters.Description: The amount to rotate by (between 0 and 31).

Functions.Name: ror
Functions.Description:
Returns the bitwise right rotation of the given value. 
Unlike shifts, rotations insert bits that are shifted out of one side back into the other side.

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: The value to rotate.

Parameters.Name: amount
Parameters.Description: The amount to rotate by (between 0 and 31).

Functions.Name: rshift
Functions.Description: Returns the logical right shift of the given value.

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: The value to shift.

Parameters.Name: amount
Parameters.Description: The amount to shift by (between 0 and 31).

Functions.Name: tobit
Functions.Description:
Returns the given value normalised into the numeric range used in bitwise operations (i.e. 32-bit signed integer range). 
Note that all bitwise operations normalise their inputs automatically, so this is usually unnecessary.

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: The value to normalise.

Functions.Name: tohex
Functions.Description: Returns the hexadecimal representation of the given value.

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: The value to represent as a hexadecimal string.

Parameters.Name: numDigits
Parameters.Description: The number of hexadecimal digits to return in the given string (default = 8). Negative values upper-case the output.

Libraries.Name: builtin
Libraries.Description: Built in values. These can be found in the global table.

Functions.Name: Angle
Functions.Description: Creates a new angle object. Omitting the components will default them to 0.

Parameters.Name: p
Parameters.Description: Initial value of the pitch component, or an angle to copy.

Parameters.Name: y
Parameters.Description: Initial value of the yaw component.

Parameters.Name: r
Parameters.Description: Initial value of the roll component.

ReturnValues.Description: A new angle object with the given initial components.

Functions.Name: CRC
Functions.Description: Returns a CRC32 value of the given string.

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to compute the CRC32 value for.

ReturnValues.Description: The CRC32 value as a string.

Functions.Name: Color
Functions.Description: Provides a new colour object. All values should be between 0 and 255.

Parameters.Name: r
Parameters.Description: Initial red value.

Parameters.Name: g
Parameters.Description: Initial green value.

Parameters.Name: b
Parameters.Description: Initial blue value.

Parameters.Name: a
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Initial alpha value, defaults to 255.

ReturnValues.Description: A new colour object with r, g, b, a fields.

Functions.Name: GetTypeDef
Functions.Description: Returns a table which you can add new methods for the given type to.

Parameters.Name: name
Parameters.Description: The name of the type you want to extend, e.g "Entity".

ReturnValues.Description: If the type exists, then a table which you can add methods to. Otherwise nil.

Functions.Name: HSVToColor
Functions.Description: Returns a colour object from the given HSV values

Parameters.Name: h
Parameters.Description: Hue, 0 - 1

Parameters.Name: s
Parameters.Description: Saturation, 0 - 1

Parameters.Name: v
Parameters.Description: Value, 0 - 1

Parameters.Name: a
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Alpha value, defaults to 255.

ReturnValues.Description: A new Color object with values converted into RGB from HSV.

Functions.Name: IsValid
Functions.Description: Returns true if the passed in value is not nil, has an "isValid" method and that method returns true.

Parameters.Name: object
Parameters.Description: The object to check for validity.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the object is "valid", false otherwise.

Functions.Name: Material
Functions.Description:
Returns a new IMaterial from the given path. 
Note that materials with a shader that is not UnlitGeneric will not render correctly in 2D contexts like screens and 
HUDs. For such materials, use render.getTextureID( path ) to get an unlit version.
Functions.Usage:
-- Load a PNG material, setting the "noclamp" and "smooth" parameters. 
local WaveMaterial = Material( "vgui/wave.png", "noclamp smooth" )

Parameters.Name: path
Parameters.Description: The path to the material (excluding materials/).

Parameters.Name: option
Parameters.Description:
If a PNG file, then any combination (separated by spaces) of: 
- vertexlitgeneric 
- unlitgeneric 
- nocull 
- alphatest 
- mips 
- noclamp 
- smooth

ReturnValues.Description: New IMaterial, or nil if the given path is invalid.

Functions.Name: Matrix
Functions.Description: Creates a new VMatrix.

Parameters.Name: data
Parameters.Description: A 4x4 table of values to initialise the matrix with, or a VMatrix to copy.

ReturnValues.Description: A new VMatrix object with the given initial values, or otherwise the identity matrix.

Functions.Name: Mesh
Functions.Description: Creates a new IMesh object to hold static mesh data.

Parameters.Name: materialHint
Parameters.Description: (Optional) An IMaterial that the mesh will be used with. Provides a hint for the mesh's internal format.

ReturnValues.Description: New IMesh.

Functions.Name: Quaternion
Functions.Description: Creates a quaternion from the given components.

Parameters.Name: real
Parameters.Description: The real part.

Parameters.Name: i
Parameters.Description: The i component.

Parameters.Name: j
Parameters.Description: The j component.

Parameters.Name: k
Parameters.Description: The k component.

ReturnValues.Description: A quaternion with the given components.

Functions.Name: Vector
Functions.Description: Creates a new vector object. Omitting the components will default them to 0.

Parameters.Name: x
Parameters.Description: Initial value of the x component, or a vector to copy.

Parameters.Name: y
Parameters.Description: Initial value of the y component.

Parameters.Name: z
Parameters.Description: Initial value of the z component.

ReturnValues.Description: A new vector object with the given initial components.

Functions.Name: Vector2
Functions.Description: Creates a new 2D vector.

Parameters.Name: x
Parameters.Description: The x coordinate to initialise the vector with if a number, or the vector to copy values from.

Parameters.Name: y
Parameters.Description: The y coordinate to initialise the vector with (ignored if X is a vector).

ReturnValues.Description: A new 2D vector with the given initial components, or (0, 0) if no components are given.

Functions.Name: assert
Functions.Description: Throws an error if the condition parameter is not truthy. Returns the condition parameter otherwise.

Parameters.Name: condition
Parameters.Description: Condition that evaluates to true on success, and false-y on failure.

Parameters.Name: msg
Parameters.Description: Error value to throw if the condition value is false-y.

ReturnValues.Description: The condition value provided, if it is not false-y.

Functions.Name: compileString
Functions.Description: Compiles the given string and returns it as a function, or nil and a string if there is a syntax error.

Parameters.Name: code
Parameters.Description: Lua code string to compile.

Parameters.Name: name
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Name to assign to the chunk.

Parameters.Name: env
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Environment to assign to the chunk.

ReturnValues.Description: Compiled function, or nil if compilation failed.
An error string if there's a syntax error.

Functions.Name: cpuAverage
Functions.Description:
Gets the average CPU time for the current instance, used for the average CPU time quota. 
This value represents a moving average over previous frames/ticks and is not updated in real time.

Parameters.Name: addTimeSeconds
Parameters.Description: If provided, the returned time will assume this many additional seconds will be used up this tick/frame (e.g. pass cpuTime() to predict the average including this frame/tick).

ReturnValues.Description: Current average CPU time in seconds.

Functions.Name: cpuGlobalTime
Functions.Description: Returns the global CPU time usage across all chips on the server in seconds. This is used to calculate the global CPU quota.

Parameters.Name: currentInstanceAverageTimeSeconds
Parameters.Description: If provided, the returned time will assume the current instance has this many seconds as its average CPU time.

Functions.Name: cpuMax
Functions.Description: Gets the CPU time hard quota. If cpuGlobalTime(), cpuAverage() or cpuTime() exceed this value, the instance may be halted.

ReturnValues.Description: Quota in seconds.

Functions.Name: cpuTime
Functions.Description: Gets the amount of time spent running this frame/tick. This value is updated in real time.

ReturnValues.Description: Current execution CPU time in seconds since the last frame/tick.

Functions.Name: doAll
Functions.Description:
Runs every file you included. 
Be careful with files that only belong on the server/client, it won't discriminate. 
Note that this does not load asynchronously included scripts that have not yet been downloaded. Those must be loaded with dofile().

ReturnValues.Description: A table of file name => returned value for every script executed.

Functions.Name: dofile
Functions.Description:
Runs an --@include'd file and returns the result. 
If the file was included asynchronously this will first download the file and yield until it's available. Thus for asynchronous includes, this must be called within a coroutine, the main file, or a file that is loaded by the main file.

Parameters.Name: file
Parameters.Description: The filename to execute, must have been included.

Functions.Name: error
Functions.Description: Throws an error, halting execution. If the current line is not executing within pcall/xpcall, this will terminate the running instance.

Parameters.Name: message
Parameters.Description: The error message/object. This can be any value, but is normally a string.

Parameters.Name: level
Parameters.Description: The level at which the error should originate from, where 1 is the current level, 2 is the caller and so on.

Functions.Name: getLibraries
Functions.Description: Gets a list of all libraries

ReturnValues.Description: A table containing the names of all available libraries.

Functions.Name: getScripts
Functions.Description:
Returns every included script file in a table of filepath -> function pairs. 
Call a function to run the file in the same way as dofile().

ReturnValues.Description: Table of filepath -> function pairs.

Functions.Name: getfenv
Functions.Description: Gets the current function environment.

ReturnValues.Description: Environment table of the current function.

Functions.Name: getmetatable
Functions.Description: Retrieves the metatable of the given table, or the value of the __metatable field on its metatable if the metatable is locked.

Parameters.Name: table
Parameters.Description: Table to get the metatable of.

ReturnValues.Description: The metatable assigned to the given table if not locked, the __metatable field on the table's metatable if locked, or nil if the table has no metatable.

Functions.Name: hint
Functions.Description: Shows a gamemode hint to the chip owner.

Parameters.Name: message
Parameters.Description: The string message to display.

Parameters.Name: type
Parameters.Description: The type of message, one of NOTIFY_GENERIC, NOTIFY_ERROR or NOTIFY_HINT.

Parameters.Name: duration
Parameters.Description: How long to show the message for.

Functions.Name: ipairs
Functions.Description: Provides a sequential iterator for tables.

Parameters.Name: table
Parameters.Description: Table to iterate in order starting at index 1.

Functions.Name: load
Functions.Description:
Compiles the given string and returns it as a function, or nil and a string if there is a syntax error. 
This function can also accept a mode parameter to be compatible with Lua 5.2+, but only "t" or "bt" are accepted ("bt" is treated as "t" as bytecode loading is not permitted).

Parameters.Name: code

Runs an --@include'd script and caches the result.

Functions.Name: xpcall
Functions.Description: Runs the given function and calls the given callback function on error.
Functions.Usage:
local function ErrorHandler( Error ) 
	-- Here the stack is yet to unwind, so the traceback points at the exact line the error occurred. 
	-- The value returned here will be output as the second returned value from xpcall. 
	return debug.traceback( Error ) 
end 
local function MyAwesomeFunc( ... ) 
	print( "Thanks for", ... ) 
end 
local Success, Err = xpcall( MyAwesomeFunc, ErrorHandler, "cake", "cookies", "milk" ) 
if not Success then 
	-- Err will include an accurate traceback here due to returning debug.traceback() in the error handler. 
	print( "Failed with error:", Err ) 
end

Parameters.Name: func
Parameters.Description: Function to run.

Parameters.Name: callback
Parameters.Description: Function to run on error. It's passed the error and executed before the stack unwinds, allowing for accurate tracebacks. The returned value is used as the final error result.
Optional arguments to pass to the provided function when calling.

ReturnValues.Description: true if execution succeeded without error, false otherwise.
If execution failed, the error message. Otherwise, all returned values from the function.

Libraries.Name: coroutine
Libraries.Description: Coroutine library. Allows for co-operative threading.

Functions.Name: create
Functions.Description: Creates and returns a coroutine thread object from the given function.
Functions.Usage:
-- Create a new thread that does some work. 
local Thread = coroutine.create( function( Message ) 
	print( "Caller says:", Message ) 
	local Reply = coroutine.yield( "Hello!" ) 
	print( "Caller says:", Reply ) 
	return "Goodbye!" 
end ) 
local Success, Message = coroutine.resume( Thread, "Hello thread!" ) 
assert( Success ) 
print( "Coroutine says:", Message ) 
Success, Message = coroutine.resume( Thread, "Goodbye thread!" ) 
assert( Success ) 
print( "Coroutine says:", Message ) 
-- Coroutine is now dead as its function has returned.

Parameters.Name: func
Parameters.Description: Function to run inside the coroutine.

ReturnValues.Description: Coroutine object representing the given function.

Functions.Name: resume
Functions.Description: Resumes a given coroutine.
Functions.Usage:
-- Pass some data to a thread and retrieve its next yielded response. 
local Success, Data = coroutine.resume( Thread, { Message = "Hello thread!" } ) 
-- This data will be taken from the next coroutine.yield() or return statement in the thread. 
print( Data )

Parameters.Name: thread
Parameters.Description: Coroutine to resume.
The parameters to pass to the coroutine (as the initial arguments, or to be returned by coroutine.yield()).

ReturnValues.Description: True if the coroutine executed without error, false if an error was caught.
If the coroutine errored, the error that was thrown. Otherwise, all values passed to coroutine.yield() or returned by the coroutine's function.

Functions.Name: running
Functions.Description: Returns the current running coroutine, or nil if none is running.
Functions.Usage:
-- Within a coroutine body, it's possible to get the running thread. 
coroutine.wrap( function() 
	local Thread = coroutine.running() 
	print( coroutine.status( Thread ) ) -- "running" 
end )() 
-- Outside of a coroutine, there is no running thread. 
print( coroutine.running() ) -- nil

Functions.Name: status
Functions.Description: Gets the status of a given coroutine. Either 'suspended', 'running', 'normal' or 'dead'.
Functions.Usage:
-- New coroutines are suspended. 
local Thread = coroutine.create( function() end ) 
print( coroutine.status( Thread ) ) -- "suspended"

Parameters.Name: thread
Parameters.Description: Coroutine to check.

ReturnValues.Description: The status of the given coroutine.

Functions.Name: wait
Functions.Description:
Yields the current coroutine and will continue to yield when called until the given time has elapsed (relative to time.curTime()). 
This does not resume the coroutine for you after the given time. You must keep resuming it until the time has elapsed (e.g. in a think hook or repeating timer).
Functions.Usage:
-- Every second, print the next number in the sequence. 
local Thread = coroutine.create( function() 
	local i = 1 
	while true do 
		coroutine.wait( 1 ) 
		print( i ) 
		i = i + 1 
	end 
end ) 
-- Poll the coroutine to allow it to advance time. 
hook.add( "think", "coroutine_counter", function() coroutine.resume( Thread ) end )

Parameters.Name: durationInSeconds
Parameters.Description: The time in seconds to yield for.

Functions.Name: wrap
Functions.Description:
Returns a function that, when invoked, resumes the coroutine representing the given function, passing the provided arguments. 
The returned values from the wrapped function represent the values that were yielded from the coroutine, or returned at the end.
Functions.Usage:
-- Generate an iterator over powers of 2 using a coroutine-wrapped function. 
-- Each invocation in a generic for will resume the coroutine. 
local function PowersOf2( Start, End ) 
	return coroutine.wrap( function() 
		for i = Start, End do 
			coroutine.yield( bit.lshift( 1, i ) ) 
		end 
	end ) 
end 
-- This will print every power of 2 between 2 ^ 1 and 2 ^ 10 (inclusive). 
for PowerOf2 in PowersOf2( 1, 10 ) do 
	print( PowerOf2 ) 
end

Parameters.Name: func
Parameters.Description: Function to run inside the coroutine.

ReturnValues.Description: Function that resumes the coroutine when invoked, returning any yielded/returned values.

Functions.Name: yield
Functions.Description:
Yields the current coroutine, suspending it in place ready for the next resume. 
Suspended coroutines will not execute again until they are resumed externally.
Functions.Usage:
-- Yield some data to the caller, and use the data passed through coroutine.resume() afterwards. 
local Data = coroutine.yield( { Message = "Hello caller!" } ) 
-- This next line will not execute until the caller resumes the thread again. 
print( Data )

Parameters.Description: The values to yield to the caller, which will be returned by coroutine.resume() (or from calling a coroutine-wrapped function).

ReturnValues.Description: The values passed to coroutine.resume() or a coroutine-wrapped function after this yield.

Libraries.Name: debug
Libraries.Description: Debug library.

Functions.Name: isCalledBy
Functions.Description: Indicates whether the given function is the caller at the given stack level.

Parameters.Name: level
Parameters.Description: The stack level above the current level to check (1 = current level).

Parameters.Name: func
Parameters.Description: The function to look for.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the function at the given stack level is equal to the given function.

Functions.Name: isCalledByAnyOf
Functions.Description: Indicates whether any of the given functions are the caller at the given stack level.

Parameters.Name: level
Parameters.Description: The stack level above the current level to check (1 = current level).

Parameters.Name: funcs
Parameters.Description: The functions to look for (a lookup table with function keys).

ReturnValues.Description: True if the function at the given stack level is contained in the lookup (using rawget).

Functions.Name: traceback
Functions.Description: Provides the current stack traceback.
Functions.Usage:
-- Get a traceback starting at the current line 
debug.traceback() 
 
-- Get a traceback with a message starting at the caller. 
debug.traceback( "Some error happened", 2 ) 
 
-- Get a traceback from a coroutine. 
debug.traceback( Thread ) 
 
-- Get a traceback from a coroutine at a given level (0 = yield, error or return). 
debug.traceback( Thread, "Some error happened", 2 )

Parameters.Name: thread
Parameters.Description: (Optional) The coroutine to get the traceback from.

Parameters.Name: message
Parameters.Description: (Optional) The message to include on the first line of the traceback (e.g. a caught error).

Parameters.Name: stackLevel
Parameters.Description: (Optional) The stack level to start the traceback from (1 = where debug.traceback() is called or where a coroutine last yielded/died).

ReturnValues.Description: A string representing the current call stack, or the given message if it is not a string.

Libraries.Name: fastlz
Libraries.Description:
FastLZ library. 
Provides compression and decompression using the LZMA method.

Functions.Name: compress
Functions.Description:
Compresses the given string into an LZMA1 (FORMAT_ALONE) binary sequence. 
Note that for very small strings, the output of this function may be larger than the original string.

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string value to be compressed.

ReturnValues.Description: The compressed string.

Functions.Name: decompress
Functions.Description:
Decompresses the given string. 
If compressing data outside of GMod to pass into this function, use the LZMA1 format (FORMAT_ALONE), and ensure that the size of the decompressed payload is provided starting at the 6th byte as an 8 byte little-endian integer.

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string value to be decompressed (compressed with fastlz.compress()).

Parameters.Name: maxSize
Parameters.Description: An optional size limit in bytes after which decompression will be stopped.

ReturnValues.Description: The decompressed string.

Functions.Description: Finds all entities intersecting the given ray.

Parameters.Name: startPos
Parameters.Description: The starting position of the ray.

Parameters.Name: endPos
Parameters.Description: The end position of the ray.

Parameters.Name: mins
Parameters.Description: (Optional) If provided, sets the mins corner of an axis-aligned box that will be used to test entities along the ray.

Parameters.Name: maxs
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Sets the maxs corner of the axis-aligned box to test entities with (assuming mins was provided).

Parameters.Name: filter
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Function to filter results, passed each entity that was found. Return true to include the entity, false to skip it.

ReturnValues.Description: An array of found entities.

Functions.Name: byClass
Functions.Description:
Finds entities by class name. 
The provided class name can contain wildcards ('*') to match zero or more characters.
Functions.Usage:
-- Find all entities whose class name starts with "prop_" 
local Props = find.byClass( "prop_*" ) 
-- Find all entities whose class name is exactly "prop_physics" 
local PhysicsProps = find.byClass( "prop_physics" )

Parameters.Name: class
Parameters.Description: The class name, optionally with wildcards.

Parameters.Name: filter
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Function to filter results, passed each entity that was found. Return true to include the entity, false to skip it.

ReturnValues.Description: An array of found entities.

Functions.Name: byIndex
Functions.Description: Finds an entity by its index.

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: The entity index.

ReturnValues.Description: The matching entity if it exists, or nil otherwise.

Functions.Name: byModel
Functions.Description:
Finds entities by their model path. 
The provided model path can contain wildcards ('*') to match zero or more characters.
Functions.Usage:
-- Find all entities whose model path starts with "models/props_borealis" 
local Ents = find.byModel( "models/props_borealis/*" ) 
-- Find all entities whose model is exactly "models/props_borealis/bluebarrel001.mdl" 
local Barrels = find.byModel( "models/props_borealis/bluebarrel001.mdl" )

Parameters.Name: modelPath
Parameters.Description: The model file to search for, optionally with wildcards.

Parameters.Name: filter
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Function to filter results, passed each entity that was found. Return true to include the entity, false to skip it.

ReturnValues.Description: An array of found entities.

Functions.Name: inBox
Functions.Description:
Finds entities contained within an axis-aligned box in world space. 
Note that any entity that the box intersects is returned, even if that entity is not fully within the box.

Parameters.Name: mins
Parameters.Description: Minimum extents of the box to search within (world space).

Parameters.Name: maxs
Parameters.Description: Maximum extents of the box to search within (world space).

Parameters.Name: filter
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Function to filter results, passed each entity that was found. Return true to include the entity, false to skip it.

ReturnValues.Description: An array of found entities.

Functions.Name: inCone

Parameters.Name: cosAngle

Parameters.Name: filter

Libraries.Name: holograms
Libraries.Description: Library for creating and manipulating physics-less models AKA "Holograms".

Functions.Name: addHalo
Functions.Description:
Adds a halo to all the passed in holograms. A hologram can only have a single halo at a time. 
Halo rendering requires the permission DRAW_HALOS.
Functions.Usage:
-- Adds a very blurry yellow halo around the first hologram in holograms.getAll() 
-- Note that halos are persistent until you call Hologram:removeHalo(). 
-- Note also that additive should really be always true, otherwise you'll get an odd looking halo. 
local Holos = holograms.getAll() 
holograms.addHalo( Holos[ 1 ], Color( 255, 255, 0 ), 10, 10, 1, true, false ) 
 
-- Alternatively, you can also add halos to multiple holograms at once. 
-- This will add a sharp red halo to all holograms the chip has spawned. 
local HaloParameters = holograms.addHalo( Holos, Color( 255, 0, 0 ), 1, 1, 1, true, false ) 
-- After 1 second, make the halos draw through walls, and increase the blur. 
timer.simple( 1, function() 
	HaloParameters.IgnoreZ = true 
	HaloParameters.BlurX = 5 
	HaloParameters.BlurY = 5 
end )

Parameters.Name: holos
Parameters.Description: A single hologram or an array of holograms to have a halo added to.

Parameters.Name: colour
Parameters.Description: The colour of the halo.

Parameters.Name: blurX
Parameters.Description: (Optional, default 2) The amount to blur horizontally, clamped between 0 and 10.

Parameters.Name: blurY
Parameters.Description: (Optional, default 2) The amount to blur vertically, clamped between 0 and 10.

Parameters.Name: drawPasses
Parameters.Description: (Optional, default 1) The amount of passes to make when drawing the halo. A higher value reduces blur. Clamped between 1 and 3.

Parameters.Name: additive
Parameters.Description: (Optional, default true) Whether the halo should be additively rendered.

Parameters.Name: ignoreZ
Parameters.Description: (Optional, default false) Whether the halo should be occluded by objects in the way or not.

ReturnValues.Description:
A table with the following editable fields (case-insensitive): 
- Color 
- BlurX 
- BlurY 
- DrawPasses 
- Additive 
- IgnoreZ 
Editing any of these values will update the halo for all the holograms passed in originally.

Functions.Name: canSpawn
Functions.Description: Returns true if the number of holograms spawned by the instance's owner is less then the max.

ReturnValues.Description: True if you can spawn holograms, false otherwise.

Functions.Name: create

ReturnValues.Description: The hologram entity.

Returns a table containing all hologram entities belonging to the current instance. 
This is shared so you can get all holograms on the client too, though the results may differ depending on the local player's networked state and location in the world relative to each hologram.

ReturnValues.Description: An array of all holograms spawned for the current instance (and are visible to the local player if called on the client).

Functions.Name: getGlobalCount
Functions.Description: Gets the number of spawned holograms across all the instance owner's chips.

ReturnValues.Description: The total number of holograms spawned by the instance's owner.

Functions.Name: getInstanceCount

ReturnValues.Description: Number of spawned holograms under this instance.

Functions.Name: getMaxHologramSize

Libraries.Name: http
Libraries.Description: HTTP library, allows sending HTTP requests.

Functions.Name: canRequest
Functions.Description: Returns whether a request can be made at the moment, taking into account permissions and the rate limit.

Functions.Name: cooldownTime
Functions.Description: Returns how long (in seconds) until the next request is allowed.

Functions.Name: fetch
Functions.Description:
Performs a GET request. 
Requires the SEND_HTTP_REQUESTS permission to be granted when used on the client side.

Parameters.Name: url
Parameters.Description: The URL to request.

Parameters.Name: onSuccess
Parameters.Description: Function to run on success. It's passed the response body, a table of header values, and the response code.

Parameters.Name: onFailed
Parameters.Description: Function to run on failure. It's passed an error message explaining the failure.

Parameters.Name: headers
Parameters.Description: An optional table of headers to include in the request (keys and values must be strings).

ReturnValues.Description: True if the request fired, false otherwise.
If the request did not fire, the reason why.

Functions.Name: maxRequestsPerSecond
Functions.Description: Returns the maximum number of HTTP requests that are allowed per second.

Functions.Name: nextRequest
Functions.Description: Returns the timestamp at which you can next run a request, relative to time.sysTime().

Functions.Name: post
Functions.Description:
Performs a form-encoded POST request. 
Requires the SEND_HTTP_REQUESTS permission to be granted when used on the client.

Parameters.Name: url
Parameters.Description: The URL to request.

Parameters.Name: params
Parameters.Description: The table of parameters to send as the (form-encoded) body of the POST request.

Parameters.Name: onSuccess
Parameters.Description: The function to run on success. It's passed the response body, a table of header values, and the response code.

Parameters.Name: onFailed
Parameters.Description: Function to run on failure. It's passed an error message explaining the failure.

Parameters.Name: headers
Parameters.Description: An optional table of headers to include in the request (keys and values must be strings).

ReturnValues.Description: True if the request fired, false otherwise.
If the request did not fire, the reason why.

Functions.Name: sendRequest
Functions.Description:
Sends an HTTP request. Unlike fetch() and post(), this allows defining all parameters of a request, including the body and method. 
Requires the SEND_HTTP_REQUESTS permission to be granted when used on the client.

Parameters.Name: request
Parameters.Description: The request to send.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the request fired, false otherwise.
If the request did not fire, the reason why.

Libraries.Name: input
Libraries.Description: Input library, for dealing with keyboard/mouse input on the client.

Functions.Name: getAnalogValue
Functions.Description:
Returns an integer value indicating the current position of an analog input axis. 
For a joystick axis, this returns a value between -32768 (pushed up/left) and 32767 (pushed down/right). 
For the mouse wheel, this returns a positive integer for increments of scrolling up, and a negative integer for increments of scrolling down. 
For the other mouse axis, this reflects the x/y co-ordinate of the mouse on the screen i.e. 0, 0, is top-left, and values increase up to the screen width/height for bottom-right.

Parameters.Name: axis
Parameters.Description: The axis to retrieve the value for (one of input.ANALOG_*).

The mode to open the file with.

The multiplier and exponent represent the given value as follows: value = mult * 2 ^ exponent.

Libraries.Name: mesh
Libraries.Description: Mesh library to facilitate drawing dynamic meshes.

Functions.Name: advanceVertex
Functions.Description: Advances the current mesh to the next vertex, committing any written data for the current vertex.

Functions.Name: generate
Functions.Description:
Generates a mesh, either writing to the given mesh object, or otherwise drawing the result directly. 
Writing to an IMesh can be done outside a rendering hook. 
Note that you cannot yield in the callback as mesh generation must occur synchronously in a single frame.
Functions.Usage:
-- Generates a 64x64 white rectangle at (0, 0) 
local function GenerateMesh() 
	mesh.writeQuadFromPlane( Vector( 32, 32, 0 ), Vector( 0, 0, -1 ), 64, 64 ) 
end 
-- In a render hook, call mesh.generate to draw the mesh dynamically. 
-- Alternatively, pass an IMesh to store the mesh data to be drawn later. 
hook.add( "render", "mesh", function() 
	render.clear( 0, 0, 0, 255 ) 
	mesh.generate( mesh.MATERIAL_QUADS, 1, GenerateMesh ) 
end )

Parameters.Name: primitiveType
Parameters.Description: The type of primitives to draw (mesh.MATERIAL_*).

Parameters.Name: primitiveCount
Parameters.Description: The number of primitives that will be drawn.

Parameters.Name: generatorFunc
Parameters.Description: A function that, when called, should write the mesh.

Parameters.Name: meshInstance
Parameters.Description: An optional IMesh object to write the mesh values to.

Functions.Name: writeColor
Functions.Description: Sets the colour of the current vertex.

Parameters.Name: r
Parameters.Description: The red value (0-255).

Parameters.Name: g
Parameters.Description: The green value (0-255).

Parameters.Name: b
Parameters.Description: The blue value (0-255).

Parameters.Name: a
Parameters.Description: The alpha value (0-255).

Functions.Name: writeNormal
Functions.Description: Sets the normal of the current vertex (used for lighting).

Parameters.Name: normal
Parameters.Description: The normal vector to write.

Functions.Name: writePosition
Functions.Description: Sets the position of the current vertex. Every vertex must have a unique position.

Parameters.Name: pos
Parameters.Description: The position to write.

Functions.Name: writeQuadFromPlane
Functions.Description: Writes 4 vertices to the mesh based on the given plane and size, all with the given colour.

Parameters.Name: position
Parameters.Description: The centre point for the quad.

Parameters.Name: normal
Parameters.Description: The plane's normal.

Parameters.Name: width
Parameters.Description: The width of the quad.

Parameters.Name: height
Parameters.Description: The height of the quad.

Parameters.Name: color
Parameters.Description: (Optional) The colour to apply to each vertex in the quad (default is white).

Functions.Name: writeQuadFromVertices
Functions.Description: Writes 4 vertices to the mesh, all with the given colour.

Parameters.Name: vertex1
Parameters.Description: The top-left corner.

Parameters.Name: vertex2
Parameters.Description: The top-right corner.

Parameters.Name: vertex3
Parameters.Description: The bottom-right corner.

Parameters.Name: vertex4
Parameters.Description: The bottom-left corner.

Parameters.Name: color
Parameters.Description: (Optional) The colour to apply to each vertex in the quad (default is white).

Functions.Name: writeTexCoord
Functions.Description: Sets the texture co-ordinates of the current vertex.

Parameters.Name: stage
Parameters.Description: The stage to write the co-ordinates to. Stage 0 is the main texture, stages 1 and 2 are used for lightmaps.

Parameters.Name: u
Parameters.Description: The u co-ordinate.

Parameters.Name: v
Parameters.Description: The v co-ordinate.

Functions.Name: writeUserData
Functions.Description: Sets the user data for the current vertex. This is used to define the tangent.

Parameters.Name: tangentX
Parameters.Description: The x component of the tangent vector.

Parameters.Name: tangentY
Parameters.Description: The y component of the tangent vector.

Parameters.Name: tangentZ
Parameters.Description: The z component of the tangent vector.

Parameters.Name: tangentHandedness
Parameters.Description: The handedness of the tangent vector (either -1 or 1).

Libraries.Name: net
Libraries.Description: Networking library. More reliable and flexible than usermessages. Allows for networking server to client or client to server.

Functions.Name: broadcast
Functions.Description: Broadcasts the currently stored network message to every player.

Functions.Name: bytesLeft
Functions.Description: Returns the number of bytes left in the current net message. Only works inside the net hook.

ReturnValues.Description: The number of bytes remaining to read in the current net message.
The number of bits remaining to read in the current net message.

Functions.Name: bytesWritten
Functions.Description: Returns the number of bytes currently written.

ReturnValues.Description: The number of bytes written to the current message.
The number of bits written to the current message.

Functions.Name: canSend
Functions.Description:
Indicates whether a net message can be sent at this time. 
If the target is omitted on the server, then this will return false if there is at least one player that cannot have a message sent to them at this time.

Parameters.Name: target
Parameters.Description: The specific player to check (server only).

ReturnValues.Description: True if a net message can be sent, false otherwise.

Functions.Name: getDTVar
Functions.Description:
Retrieves a DataTable value from the chip. 
Use net.setDTVar() on the server to set them.

Parameters.Name: type
Parameters.Description: Type of the DTVar. One of "Angle", "Bool", "Entity", "Float", "Int", "String", or "Vector".

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: An integer between 1 and 32 (1 and 4 for strings).

Parameters.Name: chip
Parameters.Description: Optional chip entity that you own to get the value from.

Functions.Name: getMaxDTVarIndex
Functions.Description:
Returns the maximum index available for the given DTVar type, or 0 if the given type is invalid. 
Some entity types can have less slots available.

Parameters.Name: type
Parameters.Description: The type of DTVar to get the maximum index of.

Parameters.Name: chip
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Chip entity that you own to get the maximum index from. Default is the instance's chip.

Functions.Name: listenForAllDTVarChanges
Functions.Description:
Enables change detection for all DataTable values on the chip. 
Changes will be broadcast via. the "dtvarchanged" hook, passing the type, index, old value and new value. 
This can increase CPU usage, so should only be used if all DataTable values are used and need change detection. 
Use net.listenForDTVarChanges() to specify more precise change detection.

Functions.Name: listenForDTVarChanges
Functions.Description:
Enables change detection for the given DataTable value. 
Changes will be broadcast via. the "dtvarchanged" hook, passing the type, index, old value and new value. 
If an index is not provided, all indices for the given type will have change detection enabled.

Parameters.Name: type
Parameters.Description: Type of the DTVar. One of "Angle", "Bool", "Entity", "Float", "Int", "String", or "Vector".

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: (Optional) An integer between 1 and 32 (1 and 4 for strings).

Functions.Name: quotaMax
Functions.Description: Returns the max amount of bytes/s allowed.

ReturnValues.Description: The maximum amount of bytes/s allowed.

Functions.Name: quotaUsed
Functions.Description:
Returns the amount of bytes in the net quota currently used. 
Note that this does not include the message currently being written, as the quota usage depends on the players the message will be sent to. 
If called on the server with no target specified, this returns the average quota used across all players.

Parameters.Name: target
Parameters.Description: The player to get the current quota usage against (server only).

ReturnValues.Description: The amount of bytes currently used.

Functions.Name: readAngle
Functions.Description: Reads an angle from the net message.

Parameters.Name: lowPrecision
Parameters.Description: Should be the same as the value used for net.writeAngle().

ReturnValues.Description: An angle from the net message.

Functions.Name: readBit
Functions.Description: Reads a bit from the net message.

ReturnValues.Description: A bit value from the net message (either 1 or 0).

Functions.Name: readBool
Functions.Description: Reads a boolean from the net message.

ReturnValues.Description: A boolean value from the net message.

Functions.Name: readData
Functions.Description: Reads binary data from the net message.

Parameters.Description: length

ReturnValues.Description: A binary string from the net message with the given length.

Functions.Name: readDouble
Functions.Description: Reads a double precision float from the net message.

ReturnValues.Description: A double precision float value from the net message.

Functions.Name: readEntity
Functions.Description: Reads an entity from the net message.

ReturnValues.Description: An entity from the net message.

Functions.Name: readFloat
Functions.Description: Reads a float from the net message.

Parameters.Name: double
Parameters.Description: Boolean flag to read a double precision float.

ReturnValues.Description: A float value from the net message.

Functions.Name: readInt
Functions.Description: Reads an integer from the net message.

Parameters.Name: bits
Parameters.Description: Number of bits the integer was sent with. Must be between 1 and 32.

Parameters.Name: unsigned
Parameters.Description: Boolean flag to read an unsigned integer.

ReturnValues.Description: An integer value from the net message, consuming the specified number of bits.

Functions.Name: readMatrix
Functions.Description: Reads a VMatrix from the net message.

ReturnValues.Description: A matrix from the net message.

Functions.Name: readNormal
Functions.Description: Reads a normalised vector from the net message.

Functions.Name: readString
Functions.Description: Reads a string from the net message.

Functions.Name: readTable
Functions.Description: Reads a table from the net message.

ReturnValues.Description: A table from the net message.

Functions.Name: readType
Functions.Description:
Reads a value from the net message of the given type enum. 
Usually the type would be sent as part of net.writeType()

Parameters.Name: type
Parameters.Description: (Optional) The type ID to read. If omitted, the type ID is read from the net message.

Functions.Name: readUInt
Functions.Description: Reads an unsigned integer from the net message.

Parameters.Name: bits
Parameters.Description: Number of bits the integer was sent with. Must be between 1 and 32.

ReturnValues.Description: An unsigned integer value from the net message, consuming the specified number of bits.

Parameters.Name: lowPrecision

ReturnValues.Description: A vector from the net message.

Functions.Name: send
Functions.Description: Sends the currently stored net message to the given player. Passing nil is the same as net.broadcast().

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player to send the message to. Passing nil sends to all.

Functions.Name: sendOmit
Functions.Description: Sends the currently stored network message to every player except those you pass in.

Parameters.Name: plys
Parameters.Description: A player or table of players to not send to.

Functions.Name: sendPAS
Functions.Description:
Sends the currently stored network message to every player who has the given position in their PAS 
(Potentially Audible Set)

Parameters.Name: pos
Parameters.Description: The position to check hearing for.

Functions.Name: sendPVS
Functions.Description:
Sends the currently stored network message to every player who has the given position in their PVS 
(Potentially Visible Set).

Parameters.Name: pos
Parameters.Description: The position to check visibility for.

Functions.Name: sendToServer
Functions.Description: Sends the currently stored network message to the server.

Functions.Name: setDTVar
Functions.Description:
Sets a DataTable value on the given chip entity. 
DataTable values are networked automatically to all clients whenever their value changes. 
Each chip has a limited number of values available per type. The index controls which value to set under a given type. 
If you omit the last argument, the value will be set on the chip executing this function. 
For entities, use ents.NULL_ENTITY to reset the value.

Parameters.Name: type
Parameters.Description: Type of the DTVar. One of "Angle", "Bool", "Entity", "Float", "Int", "String", or "Vector".

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: An integer between 1 and 32 (1 and 4 for strings).

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: The value to set.

Parameters.Name: chip
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Chip entity that you own to set the value on.

Functions.Name: start
Functions.Description: Starts a net message. All net.write functions called after this and before a send or broadcast will be sent in one net message.

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Starfall entity to send the message to instead of the current instance's entity. Can only send to entities you own.

Parameters.Name: unreliable
Parameters.Description: (Optional) If set to true, the message will not ensure the other end receives it and thus not wait before sending a new message.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the message started successfully, false otherwise.
The reason the message did not start successfully, if the first returned value was false.

Functions.Name: writeAngle
Functions.Description: Writes an angle to the net message.

Parameters.Name: ang
Parameters.Description: Angle to write.

Parameters.Name: lowPrecision
Parameters.Description: If true, the written data will be smaller at the cost of precision.

Functions.Name: writeBit
Functions.Description: Writes a bit to the current net message.

Parameters.Name: bit
Parameters.Description: Boolean true or false as the bit to write.

Functions.Name: writeBool
Functions.Description: Writes a boolean to the current message.

Parameters.Name: bool
Parameters.Description: Boolean value to write.

Functions.Name: writeData
Functions.Description: Writes binary data to the net message.

Parameters.Name: data
Parameters.Description: The data to send (a string).

Parameters.Name: length
Parameters.Description: The length, in bytes, of the data.

Functions.Name: writeDouble
Functions.Description: Writes a double precision float to the current net message.

Parameters.Name: double
Parameters.Description: Double precision float to write.

Functions.Name: writeEntity
Functions.Description: Writes an entity to the net message.

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: Entity to write.

Functions.Name: writeFloat
Functions.Description: Writes a float to the current net message.

Parameters.Name: float
Parameters.Description: Floating point number to write.

Parameters.Name: double
Parameters.Description: Boolean flag to use double precision instead of single.

Functions.Name: writeInt
Functions.Description: Writes an integer to the current net message.

Parameters.Name: int
Parameters.Description: Integer to write.

Parameters.Name: bits
Parameters.Description: Number of bits the integer uses. Must be between 1 and 32.

Parameters.Name: unsigned
Parameters.Description: Boolean to send the integer as an unsigned integer. Unsigned integers have a larger range but can't be negative.

Functions.Name: writeMatrix
Functions.Description: Writes a matrix to the net message.

Parameters.Name: matrix
Parameters.Description: The VMatrix to write.

Functions.Name: writeNormal
Functions.Description: Writes a normalised vector to the net message.

Parameters.Name: normal
Parameters.Description: Normalised vector to write.

Functions.Name: writeString
Functions.Description:
Writes a string to the net message. Some special characters may not be sent correctly. 
Use net.writeData for that.

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: String to write.

Functions.Name: writeTable
Functions.Description:
Writes an entire table with any arbitrary structure to the net message. 
NOTE: This writes 8 bit key type, key, 8 bit value type, value for every key-value pair. Usually it's much more efficient to write data in a pre-defined structure, use this as a last resort only.

Parameters.Name: table
Parameters.Description: Table to write.

Functions.Name: writeType
Functions.Description:
Writes the value's type, then its value to the net message. 
Note that this means an extra byte for the type is sent, so use only if necessary. Most net messages should have a pre-defined structure and not need to use this function. 
This is especially expensive if writing an arbitrary table, as it will use net.writeTable() internally.

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: The value to write.

Functions.Name: writeUInt
Functions.Description: Writes an unsigned integer to the current net message.

Parameters.Name: int
Parameters.Description: Integer to write.

Parameters.Name: bits
Parameters.Description: Number of bits the integer uses. Must be between 1 and 32.

Functions.Name: writeVector
Functions.Description: Writes a vector to the net message.

Parameters.Name: vec
Parameters.Description: Vector to write.

Parameters.Name: lowPrecision
Parameters.Description: If true, the written data will be smaller at the cost of precision.
