Libraries.Description: NPC library for altering NPC behaviour.

Functions.Name: getUsableWeaponClassNames
Functions.Description:
Returns a list of weapon class names that can be given to an NPC. 
Note that this does not check if the instance owner has permission to use each class, thus giving a class returned here may fail.

Fields.Name: WEAPON_PROFICIENCY_POOR
Fields.Description: Poor weapon proficiency (i.e. will miss most of the time).

Fields.Name: WEAPON_PROFICIENCY_AVERAGE
Fields.Description: Average weapon proficiency (i.e. will miss roughly half the time).

Fields.Name: WEAPON_PROFICIENCY_GOOD
Fields.Description: Good weapon proficiency (i.e. will sometimes miss).

Fields.Name: WEAPON_PROFICIENCY_VERY_GOOD
Fields.Description: Very good weapon proficiency (i.e. will rarely miss).

Fields.Name: WEAPON_PROFICIENCY_PERFECT
Fields.Description: Perfect weapon proficiency (i.e. will almost never miss).

Fields.Name: D_HT
Fields.Description: Represents a hate relationship (to be used with NPC:setRelationship()).

Fields.Name: D_FR
Fields.Description: Represents a fear relationship (to be used with NPC:setRelationship()).

Fields.Name: D_LI
Fields.Description: Represents a like relationship (to be used with NPC:setRelationship()).

Fields.Name: D_NU
Fields.Description: Represents a neutral relationship (to be used with NPC:setRelationship()).

Libraries.Name: os
Libraries.Description: GLua's os library.

Functions.Name: clock
Functions.Description:
Returns the current CPU clock time. 
Unlike other time measurements, this increments only when actual CPU work is performed, but has relatively low precision.

Functions.Name: date
Functions.Description: Returns the date corresponding to the given timestamp, formatted using a specified format. See PIL for the supported formatting options.

Parameters.Name: format
Parameters.Description: The format to use to output the date ("*t" outputs a Date structure).

Parameters.Name: time
Parameters.Description: An optional timestamp to use (default = os.time()).

Functions.Name: difftime
Functions.Description: Returns the difference between the two given timestamps.

Parameters.Name: endTime
Parameters.Description: The timestamp to subtract from.

Parameters.Name: startTime
Parameters.Description: The timestamp to subtract.

Functions.Name: time
Functions.Description: Returns the current UNIX timestamp in seconds.

Parameters.Name: date
Parameters.Description: An optional date to get the timestamp for.

Libraries.Name: permissions
Libraries.Description:
Permissions library. 
Provides an interface to check for access to certain priveleged functions.

Functions.Name: canPerformAction
Functions.Description:
Returns whether your instance has been granted permission to perform the given action. 
Note that this will throw an error if you have not registered the permission first.

Parameters.Name: action
Parameters.Description: The permission action to check access for.

Parameters.Name: target
Parameters.Description: (Optional) The target player to evaluate the permission against. This is only applicable on the server.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the instance has been granted this permission, false otherwise.

Functions.Name: register
Functions.Description:
Registers a permission request for this instance. You must register permissions in order to be granted them. 
All valid permissions are fields on the permissions library table.
Functions.Usage:
-- Request access to draw halos and play sounds from the internet. 
permissions.register( { permissions.DRAW_HALOS, permissions.PLAY_URL_SOUNDS } )

Parameters.Name: rights
Parameters.Description: The permission(s) to register (a single value or a list of values).

Fields.Name: REMOVE_ENTITY
Fields.Description: Permission to remove entities belonging to the owner.

Fields.Name: SET_PLAYER_POSITION
Fields.Description: Permission to set the owner's position.

Fields.Name: CONTROL_VEHICLE_INPUT
Fields.Description: Permission to control input (e.g. exiting) while in a vehicle owned by the chip owner.

Fields.Name: PLAY_CLIENT_SOUNDS
Fields.Description: Permission to play sounds on the client.

Fields.Name: DRAW_HALOS
Fields.Description: Permission to draw halos on the client.

Fields.Name: PLAY_URL_SOUNDS
Fields.Description: Permission to play sounds from the internet.

Fields.Name: USE_WEB_BROWSER
Fields.Description: Permission to use the game's web browser.

Fields.Name: SEND_HTTP_REQUESTS
Fields.Description: Permission to make HTTP requests on the client (on the chip's owner only).

Fields.Name: READ_CHATBOX_STATE
Fields.Description: Permission to read the chatbox state, including typed text.

Fields.Name: DRAW_MESH_HOLOGRAMS
Fields.Description: Permission to draw meshes using holograms.

Fields.Name: USE_RENDER_TARGET_MATERIALS_WITH_HOLOGRAMS
Fields.Description: Permission to apply render target textures as a hologram's material.

Libraries.Name: profiler
Libraries.Description: Profiler library for recording execution time.

Functions.Name: pop
Functions.Description: Pops a profiler state.

Functions.Name: push
Functions.Description: Pushes a new profiler state. Execution time will be measured from this point until popping.

Parameters.Name: name
Parameters.Description: The name of the new state.

Functions.Name: swap
Functions.Description:
Performs a pop and a push simultaneously, without discarding the starting time of the last state. 
Thus, the new state starts from the old state's ending time and the old state stops here.

Parameters.Name: name
Parameters.Description: The name of the new state.

Libraries.Name: propprotection
Libraries.Description: Prop protection library.

Functions.Name: isAllowedForEntity
Functions.Description: Returns true if the given action is permitted against the given entity for the owner of this instance, false otherwise.

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: The entity to query access against.

Parameters.Name: accessType
Parameters.Description: The type of access to check (one of propprotection.ACCESS_*).

Functions.Name: isAllowedForPlayer
Functions.Description: Returns true if the given action is permitted against all entities owned by the given player for the owner of this instance, false otherwise.

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player to query access against.

Parameters.Name: accessType
Parameters.Description: The type of access to check (one of propprotection.ACCESS_*).

Functions.Name: isGrantedForEntityToPlayer
Functions.Description: Returns true if the given player can perform the given action against the given entity (owned by the owner of this instance), false otherwise.

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: The entity to query access against (must be owned by the instance owner).

Parameters.Name: accessType
Parameters.Description: The type of access to check (one of propprotection.ACCESS_*).

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player to check for granted access.

Functions.Name: isGrantedToPlayer
Functions.Description: Returns true if the given action is permitted for the given player against all entities owned by the player that owns this instance, false otherwise.

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player to check for granted access.

Parameters.Name: accessType
Parameters.Description: The type of access to check (one of propprotection.ACCESS_*).

Fields.Name: ACCESS_DUPLICATOR
Fields.Description: Permission to use the duplicator tool with an entity.

Fields.Name: ACCESS_GRAVITY_GUN
Fields.Description: Permission to use the gravity gun with an entity.

Fields.Name: ACCESS_PHYSGUN
Fields.Description: Permission to use the physgun with an entity.

Fields.Name: ACCESS_TOOLGUN
Fields.Description: Permission to use the toolgun with an entity (excluding the duplicator tool).

Fields.Name: ACCESS_USE
Fields.Description: Permission to use an entity (pressing the use key).

Libraries.Name: props
Libraries.Description: Prop spawning library.

Functions.Name: canCreate
Functions.Description: Returns whether a prop can be created.

Functions.Name: create
Functions.Description:
Creates a prop_physics entity with the given model. 
Can optionally place it at a given position, and set its angles. 
This requires the SPAWN_ENTITY permission when running in restricted mode (e.g. executed from the shared area).

Parameters.Name: model
Parameters.Description: The model to give the prop.

Parameters.Name: pos
Parameters.Description: Optional starting position.

Parameters.Name: ang
Parameters.Description: Optional starting angles.

Parameters.Name: dontUndo
Parameters.Description: If true, then the prop will not be added to your undo list.

ReturnValues.Description: The created prop entity, or nil if creation failed.
If creation failed, the reason why.

Functions.Name: createVehicleSeat
Functions.Description:
Creates a vehicle seat with the given model. 
Can optionally place it at a given position, and set its angles. 
This requires the SPAWN_ENTITY permission when running in restricted mode (e.g. executed from the shared area).

Parameters.Name: model
Parameters.Description: The model to give the vehicle (must be a valid seat model, see the vehicles tab of the spawnmenu).

Parameters.Name: pos
Parameters.Description: Optional starting position.

Parameters.Name: ang
Parameters.Description: Optional starting angles.

Parameters.Name: dontUndo
Parameters.Description: If true, then the vehicle will not be added to your undo list.

ReturnValues.Description: The created vehicle entity, or nil if creation failed.
If creation failed, the reason why.

Functions.Name: deleteOnShutdown
Functions.Description: Sets whether props/vehicle seats should be deleted when the instance shuts down (either from error or chip removal).

Parameters.Name: shouldDelete
Parameters.Description: Whether all props/vehicle seats spawned by this instance be deleted on shutdown.

Functions.Name: getAll
Functions.Description: Returns a table containing every prop/vehicle seat you have spawned with props.create()/props.createVehicleSeat().

Functions.Name: removeAll
Functions.Description: Removes every prop/vehicle seat you have created with props.create()/props.createVehicleSeat().

Libraries.Name: quaternion
Libraries.Description: Quaternion library.

Functions.Name: New
Functions.Description: Creates a new Quaternion given a variety of inputs

Parameters.Description:
A series of arguments which lead to valid generation of a quaternion. 
Acceptable inputs are: 
- A single number for the real part. 
- 4 numbers for the real and 3 imaginary parts. 
- A vector which populates the imaginary parts. 
- An angle object. 
- A number and a vector to populate the real and imaginary parts. 
- 2 vectors to populate with the angle of the cross product between them. 
- An entity which populates using the entity's angles.

ReturnValues.Description: A new quaternion whose values depend on the provided input.

Functions.Name: abs
Functions.Description: Returns the absolute value of q.

Parameters.Name: q
Parameters.Description: The quaternion to get the absolute value of.

ReturnValues.Description: The absolute value of the given quaternion.

Functions.Name: conj
Functions.Description: Returns the conjugate of q.

Parameters.Name: q
Parameters.Description: The quaternion to get the conjugate of.

ReturnValues.Description: The conjugate of the given quaternion.

Functions.Name: exp
Functions.Description: Returns Euler's constant e raised to the power q.

Parameters.Name: q
Parameters.Description: The quaternion to raise e to the power of.

ReturnValues.Description: e raised to the power of the given quaternion.

Functions.Name: fromAngles
Functions.Description: Creates a quaternion representing the given angles.

Parameters.Name: angles
Parameters.Description: The angles to represent.

ReturnValues.Description: A quaternion representing the given angles.

Functions.Name: fromAxis
Functions.Description: Creates a quaternion representing the angles expressed by the given basis vectors.

Parameters.Name: forward
Parameters.Description: The forward direction of the basis.

Parameters.Name: up
Parameters.Description: The up direction of the basis.

ReturnValues.Description: A quaternion representing the world angles of the basis' forward direction, accounting for roll.

Functions.Name: fromComponents
Functions.Description: Creates a quaternion from the given components.

Parameters.Name: real
Parameters.Description: The real part.

Parameters.Name: i
Parameters.Description: The i component.

Parameters.Name: j
Parameters.Description: The j component.

Parameters.Name: k
Parameters.Description: The k component.

ReturnValues.Description: A quaternion with the given components.

Functions.Name: fromEntity
Functions.Description: Creates a quaternion representing the angles of the given entity.

Parameters.Name: entity
Parameters.Description: The entity whose angles should be used.

ReturnValues.Description: A quaternion representing the angles of the given entity.

Functions.Name: fromNumber
Functions.Description: Creates a quaternion with real part equal to the given number.

Parameters.Name: real
Parameters.Description: The real part.

ReturnValues.Description: A quaternion whose real part is equal to the given value, with no imaginary parts.

Functions.Name: fromNumberAndVector
Functions.Description: Creates a quaternion whose real part is the given number, and whose imaginary parts are taken from the given vector.

Parameters.Name: real
Parameters.Description: The real part.

Parameters.Name: imaginaryParts
Parameters.Description: The imaginary parts as a Vector.

ReturnValues.Description: A quaternion whose real part is the given number, and imginary parts are taken from the given vector (i, j, k).

Functions.Name: fromVector
Functions.Description: Creates a quaternion with imaginary parts set from the given vector.

Parameters.Name: imaginaryParts
Parameters.Description: The imaginary parts as a Vector.

ReturnValues.Description: A quaternion whose real part is 0, and imginary parts are taken from the given vector (i, j, k).

Functions.Name: inv
Functions.Description: Returns the inverse of q.

Parameters.Name: q
Parameters.Description: The quaternion to get the inverse of.

ReturnValues.Description: The inverse of the given quaternion.

Functions.Name: log
Functions.Description: Returns the natural logarithm of q.

Parameters.Name: q
Parameters.Description: The quaternion to apply the natural logarithm to.

ReturnValues.Description: The natural logarithm of the given quaternion.

Functions.Name: qMod
Functions.Description: Changes quaternion q so that the represented rotation is by an angle between 0 and 180 degrees.

Parameters.Name: q
Parameters.Description: The quaternion to normalise.

ReturnValues.Description: A new quaternion with a normalised angle.

Functions.Name: qRotation
Functions.Description:
Constructs a quaternion from the rotation vector rotVec. 
Vector direction is axis of rotation, magnitude is angle in degrees.

Parameters.Name: rotVec
Parameters.Description: The rotation vector to be represented as a quaternion.

ReturnValues.Description: A quaternion that, when applied to a vector, rotates it by the given direction and magnitude in degrees.

Functions.Name: qRotationAxis
Functions.Description: Returns a quaternion for rotation about the given axis by the given angle.

Parameters.Name: axis
Parameters.Description: The axis of rotation.

Parameters.Name: ang
Parameters.Description: The magntidue of rotation (in degrees).

ReturnValues.Description: A quaternion that, when applied to a vector, rotates it around the given axis by the given angle in degrees.

Functions.Name: qi
Functions.Description: Returns Quaternion n * i

Parameters.Name: n
Parameters.Description: The value of the i component.

ReturnValues.Description: A quaternion whose i component is n and all others are 0.

Functions.Name: qj
Functions.Description: Returns Quaternion n * j

Parameters.Name: n
Parameters.Description: The value of the j component.

ReturnValues.Description: A quaternion whose j component is n and all others are 0.

Functions.Name: qk
Functions.Description: Returns Quaternion n * k

Parameters.Name: n
Parameters.Description: The value of the k component.

ReturnValues.Description: A quaternion whose k component is n and all others are 0.

Functions.Name: rotationAngle
Functions.Description: Returns the angle of rotation in degrees.

Parameters.Name: q
Parameters.Description: The quaternion to extract the rotation angle from.

ReturnValues.Description: The angle of rotation represented by the given quaternion, in degrees.

Functions.Name: rotationAxis
Functions.Description: Returns the axis of rotation.

Parameters.Name: q
Parameters.Description: The quaternion to extract the rotation axis from.

ReturnValues.Description: The axis of rotation.

Functions.Name: rotationVector
Functions.Description: Returns the rotation vector represented by the given quaternion, where the magnitude is the angle of rotation in degress.

Parameters.Name: q
Parameters.Description: The quaternion to extract the rotation vector from.

ReturnValues.Description: A vector representing the rotation vector of the given quaternion.

Functions.Name: slerp
Functions.Description: Performs spherical interpolation between q0 and q1. Returns q0 for t = 0, q1 for t = 1.

Parameters.Name: q0
Parameters.Description: The starting quaternion.

Parameters.Name: q1
Parameters.Description: The target quaternion.

Parameters.Name: t
Parameters.Description: The fraction between the 2 quaternions (between 0 and 1).

ReturnValues.Description: A quaternion that is between q0 and q1, spherically interpolated based on the value of t.

Functions.Name: vec
Functions.Description: Converts q to a vector by dropping the real component.

Parameters.Name: q
Parameters.Description: The quaternion to extract the imaginary parts from.

ReturnValues.Description: A vector whose components are the i, j, k components of the quaternion.

Libraries.Name: render
Libraries.Description: Render library. Screens are 512x512 units. Most functions require that you be in the rendering hook to call, otherwise an error is thrown. +x is right, +y is down

Functions.Name: addBeam
Functions.Description:
Adds a beam control point to the ongoing multi-segment beam rendering operation, if called within a beam generator function. 
Attempting to call this outside of a beam generator function, or to add more control points than were declared will result in an error being thrown.

Parameters.Name: pos
Parameters.Description: The position of the control point.

Parameters.Name: width
Parameters.Description: The width to interpolate to/from on this control point.

Parameters.Name: textureEnd
Parameters.Description: The texture co-ordinate to apply at this control point.

Parameters.Name: color
Parameters.Description: The colour to apply at this control point.

Functions.Name: capturePixels
Functions.Description:
Dumps the current render target and allows the pixels to be accessed by render.readPixel. 
Allowed render contexts: 2D (render targets only).

Functions.Name: clear
Functions.Description:
Clears the surface. 
Only works on screens and inside render target drawing operations.

Parameters.Name: r
Parameters.Description: Red value to clear to (0-255, default = 0).

Parameters.Name: g
Parameters.Description: Green value to clear to (0-255, default = 0).

Parameters.Name: b
Parameters.Description: Blue value to clear to (0-255, default = 0).

Parameters.Name: a
Parameters.Description: Alpha value to clear to (0-255, default = 255).

Parameters.Name: clearDepth
Parameters.Description: Whether to clear the depth buffer (default = false).

Parameters.Name: clearStencil
Parameters.Description: Whether to clear the stencil buffer (default = false).

Functions.Name: clearBuffersObeyStencil
Functions.Description:
Clears the surface, obeying any stencil checks. 
Only works on screens and inside render target drawing operations.

Parameters.Name: r
Parameters.Description: Red value to clear to (0-255, default = 0).

Parameters.Name: g
Parameters.Description: Green value to clear to (0-255, default = 0).

Parameters.Name: b
Parameters.Description: Blue value to clear to (0-255, default = 0).

Parameters.Name: a
Parameters.Description: Alpha value to clear to (0-255, default = 255).

Parameters.Name: clearDepth
Parameters.Description: Whether to clear the depth buffer (default = false).

Functions.Name: clearDepth
Functions.Description:
Clears the depth buffer without altering the colour or stencil buffers. 
Only works on screens and inside render target drawing operations.

Functions.Name: clearStencil
Functions.Description:
Clears the stencil buffer, resetting every pixel to zero. 
Allowed render contexts: all contexts except hologram render callbacks.

Functions.Name: clearStencilBufferRectangle
Functions.Description:
Sets the stencil values for a rectangle in screen space to the given value. 
Allowed render contexts: 2D (or cameras pushed on top of a 2D context).

Parameters.Name: x
Parameters.Description: The x co-ordinate in screen space of the top-left corner of the rectangle.

Parameters.Name: y
Parameters.Description: The y co-ordinate in screen space of the top-left corner of the rectangle.

Parameters.Name: endX
Parameters.Description: The x co-ordinate in screen space of the bottom-right corner of the rectangle.

Parameters.Name: endY
Parameters.Description: The y co-ordinate in screen space of the bottom-right corner of the rectangle.

Parameters.Name: stencilValue
Parameters.Description: The new stencil value to set for every matching pixel in the stencil buffer.

Functions.Name: computeDynamicLighting
Functions.Description:
Computes the dynamic light colour of the surface at the given position and with the given normal. 
This returns a vector with values between 0 and 1 indicating the colour intensity.

Parameters.Name: pos
Parameters.Description: The position to compute the lighting at.

Parameters.Name: normal
Parameters.Description: The normal of the surface to compute the lighting for.

ReturnValues.Description: The dynamic light colour as a vector.

Functions.Name: computeLighting
Functions.Description:
Computes the (static) light colour of the surface at the given position and with the given normal. 
This returns a vector with values between 0 and 1 indicating the colour intensity.

Parameters.Name: pos
Parameters.Description: The position to compute the lighting at.

Parameters.Name: normal
Parameters.Description: The normal of the surface to compute the lighting for.

ReturnValues.Description: The light colour as a vector.

Functions.Name: computeTextureCoords
Functions.Description:
Computes corrected texture co-ordinates for the given texture/material. 
When using drawTexturedRectUV, the engine internally alters the texture co-ordinates. 
This function accounts for that alteration and ensures the rendering operation uses the intended values.
Functions.Usage:
local texture = render.getTextureID( "some/material" ) 
-- Compute the corrected texture co-ordinates to tile the texture in a 2x2 grid. 
local u0, v0, u1, v1 = render.computeTextureCoords( texture, 0, 0, 2, 2 ) 
hook( "render", "example", function() 
	render.clear() 
	render.setColor( 255, 255, 255 ) 
	render.setTexture( texture ) 
	render.drawTexturedRectUV( 0, 0, 512, 512, u0, v0, u1, v1 ) 
end )

Parameters.Name: texture
Parameters.Description: The texture ID or IMaterial to compute corrected co-ordinates for.

Parameters.Name: u
Parameters.Description: The intended u co-ordinate.

Parameters.Name: v
Parameters.Description: The intended v co-ordinate.

Parameters.Name: endU
Parameters.Description: The intended end u co-ordinate.

Parameters.Name: endV
Parameters.Description: The intended end v co-ordinate.

ReturnValues.Description: The corrected u co-ordinate.
The corrected v co-ordinate.
The corrected end u co-ordinate.
The corrected end v co-ordinate.

Functions.Name: createClientModel
Functions.Description: Creates an invisible client side model. Call Model:drawModel() to draw it in a 3D camera.

Parameters.Name: model
Parameters.Description: The model path to use.

Parameters.Name: renderGroup
Parameters.Description: One of the render.RENDERGROUP_* enums.

ReturnValues.Description: The created client side model, or nil if no further models can be created.

Functions.Name: createFont
Functions.Description:
Creates a font. Cannot be used in a rendering hook, use it outside. Returns the font's unique name. 
This only needs to be called once. After that, use the returned name to reference the font. 
If using a table of parameters, only the first argument is used. The rest are ignored. 
Creating a font is a costly process, only create the fonts you absolutely need.

Parameters.Name: font
Parameters.Description: Base font name to use OR a table containing font parameters (see the Font structure).

Parameters.Name: size
Parameters.Description: The size of the font in pixels (between 4 and 255, default is 13).

Parameters.Name: weight
Parameters.Description: The weight of the font (how thick it is, minimum of 100, default is 500).

Parameters.Name: antialias
Parameters.Description: Whether to anti-alias the text, which smooths/blurs the edges (default is true).

Parameters.Name: additive
Parameters.Description: Whether the font should render additively or not (default is false).

Parameters.Name: shadow
Parameters.Description: Whether to add a shadow to the font (default is false).

Parameters.Name: outline
Parameters.Description: Whether to add an outline to the font (default is false).

Parameters.Name: blur
Parameters.Description: The strength of blurring to apply (between 0 and 10, default is 0).

ReturnValues.Description: The name of the font that was created. Use this in later rendering functions.

Functions.Name: cursorPos
Functions.Description: Gets the co-ordinates of where the given player is aiming at on the screen (in screen space).

Parameters.Name: ply
Parameters.Description: The player to get the cursor position of.

Parameters.Name: screen
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Screen entity to get the cursor position for (either a repeater, or another screen).

ReturnValues.Description: The x co-ordinate of the player's aim position on the screen, or nil if the player's view does not intersect the screen.
The y co-ordinate of the player's aim position on the screen, or nil if the player's view does not intersect the screen.

Functions.Name: cutOutStencil
Functions.Description:
Call this after render.startStencil() to switch to drawing what you want to have the stencil cut out of.

Functions.Name: drawBeam

Draws a single-segment beam in 3D space.

Parameters.Name: start

Parameters.Name: end
Parameters.Description: The ending position of the beam.

Parameters.Name: width

Parameters.Name: texStart

Draws a rotated box in 3D space.

Parameters.Name: pos

Parameters.Name: ang
Parameters.Description: The angles of the box.

Parameters.Name: mins

Parameters.Name: maxs

Parameters.Name: color
Parameters.Description: The colour of the box.

Functions.Name: drawCircle
Functions.Description:
Draws a circle outline. 
For widths > 1, this function can be costly. Consider implementing it yourself with a vertex caching system to get better performance. 
Allowed render contexts: 2D, 3D2D.

Parameters.Name: x
Parameters.Description: Center x co-ordinate

Parameters.Name: y
Parameters.Description: Center y co-ordinate

Parameters.Name: rx
Parameters.Description: X Radius

Parameters.Name: ry
Parameters.Description: Y Radius

Parameters.Name: width
Parameters.Description: The width of the outline (default = 1). Widths > 1 are much more costly to draw.

Functions.Name: drawFilledCircle
Functions.Description:
Draws a filled circle. 
Allowed render contexts: 2D, 3D2D.

Parameters.Name: x
Parameters.Description: Center x co-ordinate

Parameters.Name: y
Parameters.Description: Center y co-ordinate

Parameters.Name: rx
Parameters.Description: X Radius

Parameters.Name: ry
Parameters.Description: Y Radius

Functions.Name: drawLine
Functions.Description:
Draws a line in 2D space. 
Allowed render contexts: 2D, 3D2D.

Parameters.Name: x1
Parameters.Description: X start co-ordinate

Parameters.Name: y1
Parameters.Description: Y start co-ordinate

Parameters.Name: x2
Parameters.Description: X end co-ordinate

Parameters.Name: y2

Functions.Name: drawLine3D
Functions.Description:
Draws a line in 3D space. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera, 3D world (emitter/holograms).

Parameters.Name: start
Parameters.Description: The starting position of the line.

Parameters.Name: end
Parameters.Description: The ending position of the line.

Parameters.Name: color
Parameters.Description: The colour of the line.

Parameters.Name: writeZ
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Whether to render the line using the depth buffer (default = false). This value is always true when rendering in the world (e.g. with an emitter or hologram).

Functions.Name: drawMultiSegmentBeam
Functions.Description:
Draws a multi-segment beam in 3D space using the given generator function to add beam segments. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera, 3D world (emitter/holograms).
Functions.Usage:
local Ent = ents.self() 
-- Define a generator function outside the render hook, taking parameters. 
local function GenerateExampleBeamSegments( Colour ) 
	-- Inside the generator function, add every declared beam control point. 
	-- This example draws 3 segments (1 -> 2, 2 -> 3 and 3 -> 4). 
	render.addBeam( Ent:toWorld( Vector( 0, 0, 0 ) ), 16, 1, Colour ) 
	render.addBeam( Ent:toWorld( Vector( 0, -32, 0 ) ), 16, 0, Colour ) 
	render.addBeam( Ent:toWorld( Vector( 0, 0, -32 ) ), 16, 1, Colour ) 
	render.addBeam( Ent:toWorld( Vector( 0, -32, -32 ) ), 16, 0, Colour ) 
end 
-- Pick an appropriate material to render the beam with. 
local BeamMaterial = Material( "trails/physbeam" ) 
local White = Color( 255, 255, 255 ) 
hook( "render", "example", function() 
	-- Pass the generator function to drawMultiSegmentBeam to render the beam segments. 
	-- Additional arguments can be passed to the generator to make re-using functions easier. 
	render.setMaterial( BeamMaterial ) 
	render.drawMultiSegmentBeam( 4, GenerateExampleBeamSegments, White ) 
end )

Parameters.Name: numControlPoints
Parameters.Description: The number of control points that will be used to draw beam segments (up to 128, n beam segments require n + 1 control points). Exceeding this value will result in an error. At least 2 control points are required to render a beam.

Parameters.Name: generatorFunc
Parameters.Description: A function that will add beam segments using render.addBeam(). This function cannot yield.
Arguments to pass to the generator function.

Functions.Name: drawPoly

Parameters.Description: Array of vertices.

Functions.Name: drawRawText
Functions.Description:
Draws the given text using whatever colour was set previously by setColor(), the position set by 
setTextPos() and the font set by setFont(). 
Unlike drawText, this does not handle alignment, new lines or tabs for you. However, it 
will be significantly more performant. 
After drawing, the text position will be updated to be after the width of the drawn text, so you 
do not need to update it manually unless you wish to render elsewhere. 
Allowed render contexts: 2D, 3D2D.

Parameters.Name: text
Parameters.Description: The text to draw.

Functions.Name: enableAlpha
Functions.Description: Sets whether to write alpha when rendering.

Parameters.Name: shouldWrite
Parameters.Description: Whether to write to the alpha channel.

Functions.Name: enableClipping
Functions.Description:
Enables/disables clipping. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera.

Parameters.Name: enable
Parameters.Description: Sets whether clipping is enabled. You should disable it after.

Functions.Name: enableColor
Functions.Description:
Sets whether to write colour values. 
Use this to render to the stencil/depth buffer without it being visible. 
NOTE: The engine does not render in 2D if the current colour's alpha is 0, even if colour writing is disabled. You must have a non-zero alpha value set to render to the stencil/depth buffer.
Functions.Usage:
render.startStencil() 
-- Turn off writing colour values. 
render.enableColor( false ) 
-- Render your bounds here 
-- Turn back on writing colour values. 
render.enableColor( true ) 
render.drawStencil() 
-- Draw things constrained to the stencil 
render.endStencil()

Parameters.Name: shouldWrite
Parameters.Description: Whether to write colour values or not.

Functions.Name: enableDepth
Functions.Description:
Sets whether to write to the depth buffer. 
Note that "enabling" depth writing here defers to the current rendering context's depth writing mode, which may not 
actually be enabled. Use render.overrideDepthEnable() to force depth writing.

Parameters.Name: shouldWrite
Parameters.Description: Whether to write to the depth buffer.

Functions.Name: enableScissorRect
Functions.Description:
Enables a scissor rect, which will prevent anything drawing outside the provided bounds. 
If a render matrix has been previously pushed, it will be applied to the given co-ordinate values to produce a screen-space scissor rectangle. 
Note that scissor rectanges are axis-aligned, and thus only translation and scaling make sense. 
For more complex use cases, see render.startStencil()/render.drawStencil()/render.cutOutStencil(). 
Allowed render contexts: 2D (or cameras pushed on top of a 2D context).

Parameters.Name: x
Parameters.Description: The x co-ordinate of the rect's top-left corner.

Parameters.Name: y
Parameters.Description: The y co-ordinate of the rect's top-left corner.

Parameters.Name: endX
Parameters.Description: The x co-ordinate of the rect's bottom-right corner.

Parameters.Name: endY
Parameters.Description: The y co-ordinate of the rect's bottom-right corner.

ReturnValues.Description: The screen-space x co-ordinate of the rect's top-left corner, after applying the current render matrix.
The screen-space y co-ordinate of the rect's top-left corner, after applying the current render matrix.
The screen-space x co-ordinate of the rect's bottom-right corner, after applying the current render matrix.
The screen-space y co-ordinate of the rect's bottom-right corner, after applying the current render matrix.

Functions.Name: end3D
Functions.Description: Ends a 3D camera. You must do this to start a new one, and you should end every camera you start.

Functions.Name: end3D2D
Functions.Description: Ends a 3D2D camera. You must do this in order to start a new one, and you should end every camera you start.

Functions.Name: endStencil
Functions.Description: Ends the stencil operation. Call this after drawStencil() to stop restricting drawing operations.

Functions.Name: frameNumber
Functions.Description: Returns the current frame number.

Functions.Name: get3DMaterialFrame
Functions.Description: Returns the current frame index of the base texture being rendered by the last set 3D material.

Functions.Name: get3DRenderBounds
Functions.Description:
Returns the minimum and maximum extents within which the current 3D rendering is restricted to, if any. 
Rendering is restricted when drawing in the world, such as in an emitter's render hook or a hologram render callback.

ReturnValues.Description: The x co-ordinate of the minimum corner of the render bounds.
The y co-ordinate of the minimum corner of the render bounds.
The z co-ordinate of the minimum corner of the render bounds.
The x co-ordinate of the maximum corner of the render bounds.
The y co-ordinate of the maximum corner of the render bounds.
The z co-ordinate of the maximum corner of the render bounds.

Functions.Name: getAmbientLightColor
Functions.Description: Returns the ambient light colour as a vector.

Functions.Name: getBlend
Functions.Description:
Returns the current blend factor (effectively an alpha multiplier) that will be applied to subsequent 3D rendering operations. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera, 3D world (emitter/holograms).

ReturnValues.Description: Alpha value between 0 and 1.

Functions.Name: getCameraStack
Functions.Description:
Returns the current camera stack. Each element is one of the render.CAM_* constants indicating the type of camera that was pushed. 
The most recently pushed camera is at the end of the array.

Functions.Description: Returns the number of clientside models you have currently in use across all instances.

Functions.Description: Returns a table of all currently active client side models for the current instance.

ReturnValues.Description: The current red value (between 0 and 255).
The current green value (between 0 and 255).
The current blue value (between 0 and 255).
The current alpha value (between 0 and 255).

Returns the current colour modulation that will be applied to subsequent 3D rendering operations.

ReturnValues.Description: Red modulation (between 0 and 1)
Green modulation (between 0 and 1)
Blue modulation (between 0 and 1)

Functions.Description:
Returns the configured size of the Starfall screen. 
Use render.getScreenRes() to get the resolution of the current rendering context.

ReturnValues.Description: The configured width of the screen in pixels.
The configured height of the screen in pixels.

Functions.Name: getIsWidescreen

Functions.Description:
Returns a material that can be used to write colour data without gamma correction. 
This is intended to be used within render targets to write exact colour data.

Functions.Name: getMatrix
Functions.Description: Returns the current render matrix (the resulting matrix from the last pushed matrix after multiplication).

Functions.Name: getMaxClientModels
Functions.Description: Returns the maximum number of clientside models you can use.

Functions.Name: getNum3DMaterialFrames
Functions.Description: Returns the number of animated texture frames that are available for the current 3D material's base texture.

Functions.Name: getNumTextureFrames
Functions.Description: Returns the number of animated texture frames that are available for the current 2D material's base texture.

Functions.Name: getScreenInfo
Functions.Description:
Returns information about the screen, such as dimensions and rotation. 
Note: this does a table copy so move it out of your draw hook.
Functions.Deprecated: Use screen.getParameters() which returns a more usable format.

ReturnValues.Description: A table describing the screen.

Functions.Name: getScreenPos
Functions.Description: Returns the screen surface's world position and angle

ReturnValues.Description: The screen position
The screen angle

Functions.Name: getScreenRes
Functions.Description:
Returns the screen resolution in the current rendering context. 
For HUDs/emitters, this returns the local player's screen resolution. 
For screens or inside render targets, when called during rendering this returns the size of the render target. 
To get the configured resolution of a Starfall screen, use render.getConfiguredScreenRes().

ReturnValues.Description: Width of the current rendering context in pixels.
Height of the current rendering context in pixels.

Functions.Name: getTextSize
Functions.Description: Gets the size of the given text displayed with the given font in pixels.

Parameters.Description: Font name returned by createFont

Parameters.Name: text
Parameters.Description: Text string to check the size of

ReturnValues.Description: Width of the text.
Height of the text.

Functions.Description: Returns the current frame index of the base texture being rendered by the last set 2D material.

Functions.Name: getTextureID
Functions.Description:
Looks up a texture ID by file name. The returned ID can be passed to render.setTexture(). 
When passed a PNG file path, the resulting material is equivalent to Material( texture, "noclamp" ). 
For other materials, the returned ID will correspond to a material that can be used in a 2D rendering context using 
the "UnlitGeneric" shader. 
For materials that already use the "UnlitGeneric" shader, use the Material() constructor instead of this function to preserve the original material parameters.

Parameters.Name: texture
Parameters.Description: Texture file path.

ReturnValues.Description: The ID of the texture, or nil if no such texture exists.

Sets whether to ignore the depth buffer.

Functions.Name: isHUD

Functions.Description: Returns whether a stencil is currently being used.

ReturnValues.Description: True if a stencil is currently in use, false otherwise.

Functions.Name: overrideBlend
Functions.Description: Enables/disables overriding the current blending function.

Parameters.Name: enable
Parameters.Description: Whether to enable the override.

Parameters.Name: sourceBlend
Parameters.Description: The blending factor for the source colour values (one of render.BLEND_*).

Parameters.Name: destBlend
Parameters.Description: The blending factor for the destination colour values (one of render.BLEND_*).

Parameters.Name: blendFunc
Parameters.Description: The blending function to use for colour (one of render.BLENDFUNC_*).

Parameters.Name: sourceBlendAlpha
Parameters.Description: The blending factor for the source alpha value (one of render.BLEND_*).

Parameters.Name: destBlendAlpha
Parameters.Description: The blending factor for the destination alpha value (one of render.BLEND_*).

Parameters.Name: blendFuncAlpha
Parameters.Description: The blending function to use for alpha (one of render.BLENDFUNC_*).

Functions.Name: overrideDepthEnable
Functions.Description:
Allows overriding the default depth write behaviour. 
This can be used to make opaque rendered elements in 3D2D properly occlude each other.
Functions.Usage:
-- Force depth writing to be enabled (must be done outside the 3D2D block). 
render.overrideDepthEnable( true, true ) 
 
-- First render a red rectangle. 
render.start3D2D( Pos, Ang, 0.1 ) 
    render.setColor( 255, 0, 0 ) 
    render.drawRect( 0, 0, 256, 256 ) 
render.end3D2D() 
 
-- Then render a yellow rectange behind it, note how this rectangle is occluded. 
-- If you comment out the overrideDepthEnable above, this will draw over the top of the red rectangle. 
render.start3D2D( Pos - Dir * 5, Ang, 0.1 ) 
    render.setColor( 255, 255, 0 ) 
    render.drawRect( 0, 0, 256, 256 ) 
render.end3D2D() 
 
-- Stop overriding depth writing. 
render.overrideDepthEnable( false )

Parameters.Name: enableOverride
Parameters.Description: Whether to override depth writing behaviour.

Parameters.Name: shouldWrite
Parameters.Description: Whether depth writing should be enabled. Has no effect if the override is not enabled.

Functions.Name: popCustomClipPlane
Functions.Description:
Pops a clip plane from the clipping plane stack. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera.

Functions.Name: popFilterMag
Functions.Description: Pops a magnification texture filter from the texture filter stack.

Functions.Name: popFilterMin
Functions.Description: Pops a minification texture filter from the texture filter stack.

Functions.Name: popMatrix
Functions.Description:
Pops a matrix from the current matrix stack. 
Note that 3D2D cameras have their own separate matrix stack, thus attempting to pop a matrix 
pushed before starting a 3D2D camera will result in an error, and any matrices not popped at the 
end of a 3D2D camera will be popped automatically.

Functions.Name: pushCustomClipPlane
Functions.Description:
Pushes a clip plane onto the clipping plane stack. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera.

Parameters.Name: normal
Parameters.Description: The normal vector of the plane.

Parameters.Name: distance
Parameters.Description: The distance of the plane from the origin along the given normal vector.

Functions.Name: pushFilterMag
Functions.Description:
Pushes a magnification texture filter onto the texture filter stack. This applies when a texture is being rendered 
at a larger size than the texture itself.

Parameters.Name: filterType
Parameters.Description: (Optional) The filter to apply (one of render.TEXFILTER_*, default = render.TEXFILTER_ANISOTROPIC).

Functions.Name: pushFilterMin
Functions.Description:
Pushes a minification texture filter onto the texture filter stack. This applies when a texture is being rendered at 
a smaller size than the texture itself.

Parameters.Name: filterType
Parameters.Description: (Optional) The filter to apply (one of render.TEXFILTER_*, default = render.TEXFILTER_ANISOTROPIC).

Functions.Name: pushMatrix
Functions.Description:
Pushes a matrix onto the matrix stack, pre-multiplying by the previously pushed matrix if present. 
Note that 3D2D cameras have their own separate matrix stack, thus attempting to pop a matrix 
pushed before starting a 3D2D camera will result in an error, and any matrices not popped at the 
end of a 3D2D camera will be popped automatically.

Parameters.Name: mat
Parameters.Description: The matrix

Functions.Description:
Sets the colour to draw any models with following this point.

Parameters.Name: r
Parameters.Description: Red value between 0 and 1.

Parameters.Name: g
Parameters.Description: Green value between 0 and 1.

Parameters.Name: b

Functions.Description: Sets an override material for upcoming 3D space rendering operations. This can be used to override the material on clientside models and holograms.

Parameters.Description: The material to use, or nil to remove any override material.

Functions.Description:
Sets model lighting from one of 6 directions (see the BOX_ enums).

Parameters.Name: r
Parameters.Description: The red colour value to use (between 0 and 1, default = 1).

Functions.Description: Sets the stencil comparison function, which determines which pixels are rendered.

Parameters.Name: stencilComparisonFunction
Parameters.Description: The comparison function to use (one of render.STENCILCOMPARISONFUNCTION_*).

Functions.Name: setStencilEnable
Functions.Description:
Sets whether stencil testing and writing is enabled or not. 
This is a lower-level interface into the stencil buffer. For most stencil use cases, use render.startStencil() instead. 
Allowed render contexts: all contexts except hologram render callbacks.

Parameters.Name: enable
Parameters.Description: Whether to enable stencil testing and writing.

Functions.Name: setStencilFailOperation
Functions.Description: Sets the stencil operation to execute when a stencil test fails for a pixel.

Parameters.Name: stencilOperation
Parameters.Description: The operation to use (one of render.STENCILOPERATION_*).

Functions.Name: setStencilPassOperation
Functions.Description: Sets the stencil operation to execute when a stencil test passes for a pixel.

Parameters.Name: stencilOperation
Parameters.Description: The operation to use (one of render.STENCILOPERATION_*).

Functions.Name: setStencilReferenceValue
Functions.Description: Sets the stencil reference value to use when comparing pixel values in the stencil buffer.

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: The reference value (between 0 and 255).

Functions.Name: setStencilTestMask
Functions.Description: Sets the stencil test mask. All stencil values read from the stencil buffer will be combined with this mask using a bitwise-and operation before they are used in comparison functions.

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: The mask to set (a single byte, 0-255). Setting to 255 disables masking.

Functions.Name: setStencilWriteMask
Functions.Description: Sets the stencil write mask. All stencil values written to the stencil buffer will be combined with this mask using a bitwise-and operation before they are committed.

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: The mask to set (a single byte, 0-255). Setting to 255 disables masking.

Functions.Name: setStencilZFailOperation
Functions.Description: Sets the stencil operation to execute when a stencil test fails for a pixel due to it being occluded by a previously written pixel in the depth buffer.

Parameters.Name: stencilOperation
Parameters.Description: The operation to use (one of render.STENCILOPERATION_*).

Functions.Name: setTextPos

Functions.Description: Sets the texture for upcoming 2D rendering.

Parameters.Name: texture
Parameters.Description: Either a texture ID, an IMaterial, a render target using the UnlitGenric shader, or nil to reset the texture.

Functions.Name: setTextureFrame
Functions.Description: Sets the base texture frame index to use for upcoming render operations using the current 2D material.

Parameters.Name: frame
Parameters.Description: The 0-based frame index to use (cannot exceed render.getNumTextureFrames()).

Functions.Name: start3D
Functions.Description:
Starts a 3D camera context. Used to render 3D models on a 2D surface. 
Calling with no arguments will replicate the current player's view. 
See render.start3DWithParameters for a more advanced version of this function that can handle orthographic projections.

Parameters.Name: pos
Parameters.Description: The position in the new 3D world to position the camera.

Parameters.Name: ang
Parameters.Description: The angles of the camera.

Parameters.Name: fov
Parameters.Description: The field of view of the camera.

Parameters.Name: x
Parameters.Description: The X position on the 2D surface to draw the 3D world.

Parameters.Name: y
Parameters.Description: The Y position on the 2D surface to draw the 3D world.

Parameters.Name: w
Parameters.Description: The width to take up on the 2D surface.

Parameters.Name: h
Parameters.Description: The height to take up on the 2D surface.

Parameters.Name: nearZ
Parameters.Description: The distance from the camera to start the near clipping plane.

Parameters.Name: farZ
Parameters.Description: The distance from the camera to start the far clipping plane.

Functions.Name: start3D2D
Functions.Description:
Starts a 3D2D camera. Rendering inside this will be drawn in the world at the given location. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera, 3D world (emitter/holograms).

Parameters.Name: pos
Parameters.Description: Position of the top left corner of the 3D2D camera.

Parameters.Name: ang
Parameters.Description: Angles of the 3D2D camera.

Parameters.Name: scale
Parameters.Description: Scale multiplier to apply to all rendering within the camera.

Functions.Name: start3DWithParameters
Functions.Description:
Starts a 3D camera using a parameters table describing the camera. 
This can be used for more advanced 3D rendering, such as orthographic projections.

Parameters.Name: cameraParameters
Parameters.Description: The parameters that describe the camera to start.

Functions.Name: startStencil
Functions.Description:
Starts a stencil operation, clearing any previous stencil values. 
Anything drawn after this and before calling render.drawStencil() will be used to restrict drawing. 
Anything drawn after this and before calling render.cutOutStencil() will be cut out of what's drawn after. 
For simple 2D box clipping, consider using render.enableScissorRect() instead. 
Allowed render contexts: all contexts except hologram render callbacks.
Functions.Usage:
-- Start the stencil, and draw a box to contain our content. 
render.startStencil() 
render.setColor( 255, 255, 255 ) 
render.drawRect( 128, 128, 256, 256 ) 
-- Now draw text inside the box. 
render.drawStencil() 
render.setColor( 0, 0, 0 ) 
render.drawText( Font, 256, 256, "Hello World!", render.TEXT_ALIGN_CENTER ) 
-- Finally, end the stencil. 
render.endStencil()

Functions.Name: suppressEngineLighting
Functions.Description:
Sets whether to supress the standard lighting. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera.

Parameters.Name: suppressEngineLighting
Parameters.Description: Whether to suppress the standard lighting.

Functions.Name: toScreen
Functions.Description:
Returns the location on the screen of a world space position as a table with x, y and boolean visible values. 
This only works on HUDs.
Functions.Deprecated: Use Vector:ToScreen() to get the same result.

Parameters.Name: pos
Parameters.Description: World-space position vector you want to know the onscreen location of.

Functions.Name: vertex
Functions.Description: Creates a vertex for use with polygons. This just creates a table; it doesn't really do anything special
Functions.Deprecated: This is literally creating a table. You don't need to use it.

Parameters.Description: x
y
u
v

ReturnValues.Description: { x = x or 0, y = y or 0, u = u or 0, v = v or 0 }

Fields.Name: STUDIO_RENDER
Fields.Description: This flag is always set when rendering entities, indicating that the entity should render.

Fields.Name: STUDIO_TWOPASS
Fields.Description:
Indicates that the current render is for an entity that will draw both opaque and translucent renderables (i.e. using RENDERGROUP_BOTH). 
This flag is present on both passes.

Fields.Name: STUDIO_TRANSPARENCY
Fields.Description: Indicates that the current render is for the translucent render pass. Use this flag to differentiate between the opaque and translucent pass when using RENDERGROUP_BOTH.

Fields.Name: RENDERGROUP_BOTH
Fields.Description: Marks the entity as rendering in both the opaque and translucent passes. For holograms, this results in the render callback being invoked twice per frame (once in each pass).

Fields.Name: RENDERGROUP_OPAQUE
Fields.Description: Marks the entity as rendering in just the opaque pass (i.e. the entity is completely opaque).

Fields.Name: RENDERGROUP_TRANSLUCENT
Fields.Description: Marks the entity as rendering in just the translucent pass (i.e. the entity is completely translucent).

Fields.Name: MATERIAL_LIGHT_DISABLE
Fields.Description: Disables the light at the given index.

Fields.Name: MATERIAL_LIGHT_POINT
Fields.Description: A light that emits from a single point in all directions.

Fields.Name: MATERIAL_LIGHT_DIRECTIONAL
Fields.Description: A light that emits from a point in a specific direction. Unlike a spot light, this does not have multiple cones.

Fields.Name: MATERIAL_LIGHT_SPOT
Fields.Description: A spotlight comprising of 2 light cones.

Fields.Name: CAM_3D2D
Fields.Description: Constant for a pushed 3D2D camera context.

Fields.Name: CAM_3D
Fields.Description: Constant for a pushed 3D camera context.

Fields.Name: TEXFILTER_POINT
Fields.Description: Applies point texture filtering, making rendering pixelated.

Fields.Name: TEXFILTER_LINEAR
Fields.Description: Applies linear texture filtering, applying a basic linear interpolation to each pixel.

Fields.Name: TEXFILTER_ANISOTROPIC
Fields.Description:
Applies anisotropic texture filtering, making textures appear smooth while retaining detail when rendered at an 
angle.

Fields.Name: STENCILOPERATION_KEEP
Fields.Description: Stencil operation to retain the current stencil value for a pixel.

Fields.Name: STENCILOPERATION_ZERO
Fields.Description: Stencil operation to set the stencil value for a pixel to zero.

Fields.Name: STENCILOPERATION_REPLACE
Fields.Description: Stencil operation to replace the stencil value for a pixel with the current reference value.

Fields.Name: STENCILOPERATION_INCRSAT
Fields.Description: Stencil operation to increment the stencil value for a pixel, stopping at 255.

Fields.Name: STENCILOPERATION_DECRSAT
Fields.Description: Stencil operation to decrement the stencil value for a pixel, stopping at 0.

Fields.Name: STENCILOPERATION_INVERT
Fields.Description: Stencil operation to invert the bits in the stencil value for a pixel.

Fields.Name: STENCILOPERATION_INCR
Fields.Description: Stencil operation to increment the stencil value for a pixel, wrapping back to 0 on overflow.

Fields.Name: STENCILOPERATION_DECR
Fields.Description: Stencil operation to decrement the stencil value for a pixel, wrapping back to 255 on overflow.

Fields.Name: STENCILCOMPARISONFUNCTION_NEVER
Fields.Description: Stencil comparison function that never passes.

Fields.Name: STENCILCOMPARISONFUNCTION_LESS
Fields.Description: Stencil comparison function that passes if the reference value is less than a pixel's value.

Fields.Name: STENCILCOMPARISONFUNCTION_EQUAL
Fields.Description: Stencil comparison function that passes if the reference value is equal to a pixel's value.

Fields.Name: STENCILCOMPARISONFUNCTION_LESSEQUAL
Fields.Description: Stencil comparison function that passes if the reference value is less than or equal to a pixel's value.

Fields.Name: STENCILCOMPARISONFUNCTION_GREATER
Fields.Description: Stencil comparison function that passes if the reference value is greater than a pixel's value.

Fields.Name: STENCILCOMPARISONFUNCTION_NOTEQUAL
Fields.Description: Stencil comparison function that passes if the reference value is not equal to a pixel's value.

Fields.Name: STENCILCOMPARISONFUNCTION_GREATEREQUAL
Fields.Description: Stencil comparison function that passes if the reference value is greater than or equal to a pixel's value.

Fields.Name: STENCILCOMPARISONFUNCTION_ALWAYS
Fields.Description: Stencil comparison function that always passes.

Fields.Name: BLEND_DST_COLOR
Fields.Description: Multiplies the given value by the colour value that was previously written.

Fields.Name: BLEND_ONE_MINUS_DST_COLOR
Fields.Description: Multiplies the given value by the colour value that was previously written subtracted from 1.

Fields.Name: BLEND_SRC_ALPHA
Fields.Description: Multiplies the given value by the alpha value being written.

Fields.Name: BLEND_ONE_MINUS_SRC_ALPHA
Fields.Description: Multiplies the given value by the alpha value being written subtracted from 1.

Fields.Name: BLEND_DST_ALPHA
Fields.Description: Multiplies the given value by the alpha value that was previously written.

Fields.Name: BLEND_ONE_MINUS_DST_ALPHA
Fields.Description: Multiplies the given value by the alpha value that was previously written subtracted from 1.

Fields.Name: BLEND_SRC_ALPHA_SATURATE
Fields.Description: Multiplies by the minimum of the source alpha, and (1 - destination alpha).

Fields.Name: BLEND_SRC_COLOR
Fields.Description: Multiplies the given value by the colour value that is being written.

Fields.Name: BLEND_ONE_MINUS_SRC_COLOR
Fields.Description: Multiplies the given value by the colour value that is being written subtracted from 1.

Fields.Name: BLENDFUNC_ADD
Fields.Description: Takes the source and destination values, and adds them together (source + destination).

Fields.Name: BLENDFUNC_SUBTRACT
Fields.Description: Subtracts the destination value from the source value (source - destination).

Fields.Name: BLENDFUNC_REVERSE_SUBTRACT
Fields.Description: Subtracts the source value from the destination value (destination - source).

Libraries.Name: rendertarget
Libraries.Description: Render target library. Use it to create a render target texture object to draw to.

Functions.Name: create
Functions.Description: Requests a new render target. These are limited to a fixed amount per player.

Parameters.Name: highRes
Parameters.Description: When no W/H is given: if true, the RT is the "highres" resolution. If false it is the standard screen size.

Parameters.Name: w
Parameters.Description: (Optional) This sets a custom width. Must be a power of 2, or it will error.

Parameters.Name: h
Parameters.Description: (Optional) This sets a custom height. Must be a power of 2, or it will error.

ReturnValues.Description: The render target, or nil if no further render targets can be created.

Functions.Name: createWithSize
Functions.Description: Requests a new render target with the given dimensions. These are limited to a fixed amount per player.

Parameters.Name: width
Parameters.Description: The width of the texture. Must be a power of 2.

Parameters.Name: height
Parameters.Description: The height of the texture. Must be a power of 2.

ReturnValues.Description: The render target, or nil if no further render targets can be created.

Functions.Name: getMaxMemoryMiB
Functions.Description:
Returns the maximum amount of graphics memory that can be allocated for render targets. 
This applies at the player level, and is shared with screens.

Functions.Name: getMaxRTCount
Functions.Description: Returns the maximum number of RTs that may be created. This applies at the player level.

Functions.Name: getMaxTextureSize
Functions.Description: Returns the maximum size along a given axis that a render target can be.

Functions.Name: getMinTextureSize
Functions.Description: Returns the minimum size along a given axis that a render target can be.

Functions.Name: getRemainingMemoryMiB
Functions.Description:
Returns the remaining amount of graphics memory (in MiB) that can be allocated for render targets for the instance owner. 
Note that this is shared with screens.

Functions.Name: getRemainingRTCount
Functions.Description:
Returns the remaining number of RTs that may be created for the instance owner. 
Note that this does not account for memory constraints. See rendertarget.getRemainingMemoryMiB() for the memory quota.

Fields.Name: MATERIAL_FLAG_VERTEXCOLOR
Fields.Description: Flag used to apply vertex-specific colours when rendering.

Fields.Name: MATERIAL_FLAG_VERTEXALPHA
Fields.Description: Flag used to apply vertex-specific alpha values when rendering.

Fields.Name: MATERIAL_FLAG_SELFILLUM
Fields.Description:
Flag used to apply self-illumination using the alpha channel when rendering. This applies only to the VertexLitGeneric shader. 
Cannot be used with MATERIAL_FLAG_ALPHATEST or MATERIAL_FLAG_TRANSLUCENT.

Fields.Name: MATERIAL_FLAG_ADDITIVE
Fields.Description: Flag used to render the material additively.

Fields.Name: MATERIAL_FLAG_ALPHATEST
Fields.Description:
Flag used to apply alpha testing when rendering. 
Alpha testing is a simple transparency check, either fully opaque, or completely hidden, based on the alpha value. 
Cannot be used with MATERIAL_FLAG_SELFILLUM or MATERIAL_FLAG_TRANSLUCENT.

Fields.Name: MATERIAL_FLAG_NOCULL
Fields.Description: Flag used to disable backface culling when rendering.

Fields.Name: MATERIAL_FLAG_TRANSLUCENT
Fields.Description:
Flag used to apply translucency when rendering based on the alpha channel. 
Cannot be used with MATERIAL_FLAG_SELFILLUM or MATERIAL_FLAG_ALPHATEST.

Fields.Name: MATERIAL_FLAG_HALFLAMBERT
Fields.Description: Flag used to apply half-lambert shading when rendering. This applies only to the VertexLitGeneric shader.

Fields.Name: SHADER_VERTEX_LIT_GENERIC
Fields.Description: Shader used to apply lighting to the render target. This is applicable only to render targets used for 3D rendering.

Fields.Name: SHADER_UNLIT_GENERIC
Fields.Description: Shader used to ignore lighting on the render target. This is required for 2D rendering.

Libraries.Name: sounds
Libraries.Description:
Sound functions. Plays and manipulates sounds, optionally 
attaching them to an entity.

Functions.Name: create
Functions.Description:
Creates a sound and attaches it to an entity. You need to do sound:play() before the sound will play however. 
Requires the PLAY_CLIENT_SOUNDS permission when used on the client side.

Parameters.Name: entity
Parameters.Description: Entity playing the sound

Parameters.Name: path
Parameters.Description: Filepath to the sound file.

ReturnValues.Description:
The sound object. Keep this around to ensure it isn't GC'd (and thus stopped) 
before it is done playing.

Functions.Name: duration
Functions.Description:
Returns the duration of the sound in seconds. Only works on .wav files, 
and there are other issues as well.

Parameters.Name: path
Parameters.Description: The file path to get the duration for.

ReturnValues.Description: The duration of the sound (in seconds).

Functions.Name: emitEntity
Functions.Description:
Plays a sound from an entity. Quick alternative to sounds.create if you don't need the extra bit of control. 
Requires the PLAY_CLIENT_SOUNDS permission when used on the client side.

Parameters.Name: entity
Parameters.Description: The entity to emit the sound from.

Parameters.Name: path
Parameters.Description: The name of the sound to emit (either a file name, or a sound script name).

Parameters.Name: soundLevel
Parameters.Description: (Optional) The sound level (in decibels), between 10 and 511.

Parameters.Name: pitch
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Pitch percent, from 0 to 255.

Functions.Name: emitWorld
Functions.Description: Plays a sound from a fixed point in the world.

Parameters.Name: origin
Parameters.Description: The position in the world to emit the sound from.

Parameters.Name: path
Parameters.Description: Filepath to the sound file.

Parameters.Name: soundLevel
Parameters.Description: (Optional) The sound level (in decibels), between 10 and 511.

Parameters.Name: pitch
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Pitch percent, from 0 to 255.

Functions.Name: exists
Functions.Description: Checks if the given sound path exists.

Parameters.Name: path
Parameters.Description: The path to check.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the given sound path exists, false otherwise.

Functions.Name: playFile
Functions.Description:
Plays a file from the given file path using IGModAudioChannel. 
This has a few distinct advantages over traditional sounds, including FFT data, playback rate adjusting, time seeking and more. 
Requires the PLAY_CLIENT_SOUNDS permission.

Parameters.Name: path
Parameters.Description: The filepath to the sound you want to play.

Parameters.Name: flags
Parameters.Description: Space separated flags. Any of: "3d", "mono", "noplay", "noblock".

Parameters.Name: callback
Parameters.Description: Function to run when the audio channel is ready to play. Receives the channel, and if an error occurred, an error ID and error name.

Functions.Name: playURL
Functions.Description: Plays a sound from the given URL. Requires the PLAY_URL_SOUNDS permission.

Parameters.Name: url
Parameters.Description: The URL pointing at a valid sound file or stream.

Parameters.Name: flags
Parameters.Description: Space separated flags. Any of: "3d", "mono", "noplay", "noblock".

Parameters.Name: callback
Parameters.Description: Function to run when the audio channel is ready to play. Receives the channel, and if an error occurred, an error ID and error name.

Functions.Name: stop
Functions.Description:
Stops a sound started by emitEntity() on the given entity. Note that this only works for sound scripts. 
Requires the PLAY_CLIENT_SOUNDS permission when used on the client side.

Parameters.Name: entity
Parameters.Description: The entity to stop the sound on.

Parameters.Name: scriptName
Parameters.Description: The name of the sound script to stop (that was previously started with emitEntity()).

Libraries.Name: starfall
Libraries.Description: Starfall library. Used for introspection of the running instance.

Functions.Name: Error
Functions.Description:
Constructs a new Error object with the given message, capturing the traceback starting at the given level. 
This is akin to a call to error(), but instead of throwing, the message and traceback are captured. 
If the returned error object is thrown with error() and left uncaught, its message and traceback will be shown in the editor, rather than the traceback from the point at which it was thrown.
Functions.Usage:
local function OnError( Err ) 
	-- Capture the error as-is (level 0 won't add the source to the message), and its traceback. 
	return starfall.Error( Err, 0 ) 
end 
-- Assume that ExampleFunction is some external function whose errors should be captured. 
local Success, Err = xpcall( ExampleFunction, OnError ) 
if not Success then 
	-- Err is an Error type, with a recorded message and traceback. 
	print( "Example operation failed:", Err:getMessage() ) 
	print( Err:getTraceback() ) 
	-- It can be thrown again to preserve the original traceback. 
	error( Err ) 
end

Parameters.Name: message
Parameters.Description: The message to store on the error (usually a string, but can be any value).

Parameters.Name: level
Parameters.Description: The stack level at which to start capturing the traceback (default = 1).

ReturnValues.Description: An Error object with the given message, capturing the traceback at the given level.

Functions.Name: getAuthor
Functions.Description: Provides the author of the given entity's instance.

Parameters.Name: starfallEntity
Parameters.Description: (Optional) The entity to get the author of. If not provided, the entity executing this function will be used.

ReturnValues.Description: The entity's Starfall author.

Functions.Name: getInstanceOwner
Functions.Description:
Returns the player that owns the given entity's instance. 
This may differ from the entity's prop protection owner if another player uploaded code to the entity.

Parameters.Name: starfallEntity
Parameters.Description: (Optional) The entity to get the instance owner of. If not provided, the entity executing this function will be used.

ReturnValues.Description: The player that last uploaded code to the given Starfall entity, or nil if the given entity has no active instance.

Functions.Name: getLibraries
Functions.Description: Gets a list of all library names.

ReturnValues.Description: A table containing the names of all available libraries.

Functions.Name: getMainFileName
Functions.Description: Provides the name of the main file used to load the running script.

ReturnValues.Description: The main file's name.

Functions.Name: getName
Functions.Description: Provides the current name of the given entity's instance.

Parameters.Name: starfallEntity
Parameters.Description: (Optional) The entity to get the name of. If not provided, the entity executing this function will be used.

ReturnValues.Description: The entity's Starfall name.

Functions.Name: getScripts
Functions.Description:
Returns every included script file in a table of filepath -> function pairs. 
Call a function to run the file in the same way as dofile(). 
Note that this does not contain scripts that were included asynchronously and have not yet been downloaded.
Functions.Usage:
-- This snippet loads all script files. 
local Scripts = starfall.getScripts() 
for FilePath, CompiledScript in pairs( Scripts ) do 
	-- If you want to only load those from a certain folder, check FilePath starts with the folder path. 
	-- Calling the CompiledScript function loads the script file. 
	CompiledScript() 
end

ReturnValues.Description: Table of filepath -> function pairs.

Functions.Name: getSuperTypeOf
Functions.Description: Returns the name of the type the given type inherits from, if any.

Parameters.Name: typeName
Parameters.Description: The name of the type to get the super type of.

Functions.Name: getTypeDef
Functions.Description:
Returns a table which you can add new methods for the given type to, if the type is extendable. 
The type's own methods are accessible through this table, but cannot be overwritten. 
Note that native types (Angle, Color, Vector) cannot be extended.
Functions.Usage:
-- Get the "Player" type. 
local Player = starfall.getTypeDef( "Player" ) 
 
-- Add a new method to check if the player's an ally. 
function Player:isFactionAlly() 
	return faction.isAlly( self ) 
end 
 
-- The new method's ready and can be used on any player object now! 
print( ents.owner():isFactionAlly() ) -- True if you're in a faction.

Parameters.Name: name
Parameters.Description: The name of the type you want to extend, e.g "Entity".

ReturnValues.Description: If the type exists, then a table which you can add methods to. Otherwise nil.

Functions.Name: isInstanceOf
Functions.Description:
Returns whether the given object is an instance of the given type. 
That is, if the given object either is the given type, or inherits from it.

Parameters.Name: object
Parameters.Description: The object to check.

Parameters.Name: type
Parameters.Description: The name of the type to check for.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the given object is, or inherits from the given type.

Functions.Name: isSpawnedFromDuplicator

ReturnValues.Description: True if the current instance's entity was spawned from a duplicator.

Functions.Name: listenForRingTeleport
Functions.Description: Adds a listener function for entities teleporting through a ring base.
Functions.Usage:
stargate.listenForRingTeleport( function( SourceRings, DestinationRings, Entities ) 
	print( SourceRings, "teleported the following entities to", DestinationRings, ":" ) 
	for i = 1, #Entities do 
		print( Entities[ i ] ) 
	end 
end, RingBase )

Parameters.Name: callback

Libraries.Name: string
Libraries.Description: Lua's (not glua's) string library

Functions.Name: byte
Functions.Description: Returns the byte(s) in the given string between the given start and optional end index (inclusive).

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to get the bytes from.

Parameters.Name: startIndex
Parameters.Description: The index within the string to get the first byte from (default = 1).

Parameters.Name: endIndex
Parameters.Description: The index within the string to get the last byte from (default = startIndex).

ReturnValues.Description: A variable number of bytes, depending on the size of the given index range.

Functions.Name: char
Functions.Description: Returns a string constructed from the given bytes.

Parameters.Description: The bytes to construct the string from.

Functions.Name: explode
Functions.Description: Splits the given string based on the given delimiter.
Functions.Usage:
-- This will print "comma" as it's the second value when splitting by ",". 
print( string.explode( "some,comma,separated,values", "," )[ 2 ] )

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to split.

Parameters.Name: delimiter
Parameters.Description: The delimiter to split the string by.

Parameters.Name: withPattern
Parameters.Description: Whether the delimiter should be interpreted as a Lua pattern or not (default = false).

ReturnValues.Description: An array containing the segments of the string split by the given delimiter.

Functions.Name: find
Functions.Description: Searches the given string for the given pattern, optionally from the given starting index. See the Lua manual for details on the pattern syntax.

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to search.

Parameters.Name: pattern
Parameters.Description: The pattern to search for.

Parameters.Name: init
Parameters.Description: An optional start index within the given string to begin the pattern search from (default = 1).

Parameters.Name: plainText
Parameters.Description: An optional flag indicating whether to search for an exact value (true), or a pattern (false) (default = false).

ReturnValues.Description: The index within the string at which the given pattern was found, or nil if the pattern was not found.
The index within the string at which the given pattern match ended, or nil if the pattern was not found.
A variable number of matched values, determined by the capture groups within the pattern.

Functions.Name: format
Functions.Description: Returns a string built from the given format with the given values substituted into it.

Parameters.Name: format
Parameters.Description: The format definition to use to construct the output string.
Values to be substituted based on the given format definition.

Functions.Name: gmatch
Functions.Description: Returns an iterator over all matches of the given pattern within the given string. The number of values in the iteration depend on the number of capture groups. See the Lua manual for details on the pattern syntax.

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to iterate over.

Parameters.Name: pattern
Parameters.Description: The pattern to search for in the given string.

ReturnValues.Description: An iterator function that can be used in a for-loop to iterate over every match.

Functions.Name: gsub
Functions.Description: Substitutes all matches of the given pattern within the given string with the given replacement value, or calls a function for each match if the replacement is a function. See the Lua manual for details on the pattern syntax.
Functions.Usage:
-- Replace all matches with a fixed string (this prints "word word", 2). 
print( string.gsub( "test value", "%w+", "word" ) ) 
-- Replace all matches based on a table of values (this prints "first second", 2). 
print( string.gsub( "test value", "%w+", { test = "first", value = "second" } ) ) 
-- Replace all matches by upper casing using a function (this prints "TEST VALUE", 2). 
print( string.gsub( "test value", "%w+", string.upper ) )

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to replace values within.

Parameters.Name: pattern
Parameters.Description: The pattern to find and replace.

Parameters.Name: replacement
Parameters.Description: If a string, all matches will be replaced with this value. If a table, any matches that have a key in the given table will be replaced with the value under that key. If a function, will be called for each match, passing the capture groups from the pattern as var-args, with a non-nil return value used as the replacement (returning nil will leave the value as-is).

Parameters.Name: max
Parameters.Description: An optional limit on the number of replacements to perform before stopping (default = unlimited).

ReturnValues.Description: A copy of the given string with all matches of the given pattern (up to the given maximum) replaced.
The number of substitutions that were made.

Functions.Name: len
Functions.Description: Returns the length of the given string, in bytes (equivalent to #string).

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to get the length of.

ReturnValues.Description: The length of the given string in bytes.

Functions.Name: lower
Functions.Description: Returns a copy of the given string with all ASCII letters lower-cased.

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to get a lower-case copy of.

Functions.Name: match
Functions.Description: Matches the given string against the given pattern, optionally from the given starting index. See the Lua manual for details on the pattern syntax.

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to match against.

Parameters.Name: pattern
Parameters.Description: The pattern to match.

Parameters.Name: init
Parameters.Description: An optional start index within the given string to begin the pattern match from (default = 1).

ReturnValues.Description: A variable number of matched values if the pattern was matched, determined by the capture groups within the pattern. Otherwise nil if there was no match.

Functions.Name: rep
Functions.Description: Returns the given string repeated the given number of times, optionally separated by the given separator.

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to repeat.

Parameters.Name: repetitions
Parameters.Description: The number of times to repeat the given string.

Parameters.Name: separator
Parameters.Description: An optional separator to add between each repetition (default = "").

Functions.Name: reverse
Functions.Description: Returns a copy of the given string with all bytes in reverse order.

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to get a reversed copy of.

Functions.Name: sub
Functions.Description: Returns a substring of the given string between the given start and optional end index (inclusive).

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to extract a substring from.

Parameters.Name: startIndex
Parameters.Description: The start index of the substring (where 1 is the first byte). May be negative to start from the end of the string (e.g. -1 is the last byte).

Parameters.Name: endIndex
Parameters.Description: The end index of the substring (default = #string).

Functions.Name: upper
Functions.Description: Returns a copy of the given string with all ASCII letters upper-cased.

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to get a upper-case copy of.
