Libraries.Name: table
Libraries.Description: Lua's (not glua's) table library

Functions.Name: concat
Functions.Description: Returns the concatenation of all values in the given array as a string.

Parameters.Name: array
Parameters.Description: The array to concatenate.

Parameters.Name: separator
Parameters.Description: An optional separator to insert between each array value (default = "").

Parameters.Name: startIndex
Parameters.Description: An optional start index within the array to concatenate values from (inclusive, default = 1).

Parameters.Name: endIndex
Parameters.Description: An optional end index within the array to stop concatenating values at (inclusive, default = #array).

Functions.Name: foreach
Functions.Description: Executes the given function for every key-value in the given table.

Parameters.Name: table
Parameters.Description: The table to iterate.

Parameters.Name: callback
Parameters.Description: The function that will be called for each key-value pair, passed the key and value.

Functions.Name: foreachi
Functions.Description: Executes the given function for every array value in the given table.
Functions.Deprecated: Use the ipairs() iterator or a numeric for-loop instead.

Parameters.Name: array
Parameters.Description: The array to iterate.

Parameters.Name: callback
Parameters.Description: The function that will be called for each array value, passed the index and value.

Functions.Name: getn
Functions.Description: Returns the length of the array part of the given table.

Parameters.Name: table
Parameters.Description: The table to get the length of the array part from.

Functions.Name: insert
Functions.Description: Inserts the given value into the given table.
Functions.Usage:
local Array = {} 
-- Insert a new value at the end of the array. 
table.insert( Array, "example" ) 
-- Insert a new value at the start of the array. 
table.insert( Array, 1, "this is an" )

Parameters.Name: table
Parameters.Description: Table to insert the given value into.

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: An optional index to insert the value into (default = #table + 1).

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: The value to insert.

Functions.Name: maxn
Functions.Description:
Returns the maximum numeric key in the given table. 
Unlike '#', this does not require a sequence of values.

Parameters.Name: table
Parameters.Description: The table to get the highest numeric key from.

Functions.Name: pack
Functions.Description:
Returns a new table whose array part is populated with the given argumnents. 
An additional 'n' key is also added which specifies the number of arguments the table was constructed from (including nils).

Parameters.Description: The values to pack into a new table's array part.

Functions.Name: remove
Functions.Description: Removes the value held at the given table key, shifting later array values down.

Parameters.Name: table
Parameters.Description: The table to remove the given key from.

Parameters.Name: key
Parameters.Description: The key to remove.

ReturnValues.Description: The value that was removed, or nil if no such key existed.

Functions.Name: sort
Functions.Description: Sorts the given table in-place, optionally using the given comparator function.

Parameters.Name: table
Parameters.Description: The table to sort.

Parameters.Name: comparator
Parameters.Description: An optional comparator function to determine the sorting order. Called with two values, and should return true if the first value is before the second, or false otherwise.

Functions.Name: unpack
Functions.Description:
Unpacks the values in the given table into individual return values. 
This is the same as the global unpack() function.

Parameters.Name: table
Parameters.Description: Table to unpack into a vararg.

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: The index to start unpacking from (default = 1).

Parameters.Name: endIndex
Parameters.Description: The index to finish unpacking at (default = #table).

ReturnValues.Description: A value per entry in the table unpacked.

Functions.Description:
Returns the intersection point for the given ray against the given box, in world space. 
If no intersection occurs, nil will be returned.

Parameters.Name: rayOrigin
Parameters.Description: The origin of the ray.

Parameters.Name: rayDelta
Parameters.Description: A vector whose direction determines the ray's direction, and whose length determines how far the ray travels.

Parameters.Name: boxOrigin
Parameters.Description: The origin of the box.

Parameters.Name: boxAngles
Parameters.Description: The angles of the box.

Parameters.Name: boxMins
Parameters.Description: The minimum extents of the box.

Parameters.Name: boxMaxs
Parameters.Description: The maximum extents of the box.

ReturnValues.Description: The position of the intersection point, or nil if no intersection occurs.
The hit normal of the intersection, or nil if no intersection occurs.
The fraction along the ray at which the intersection occurred (between 0 and 1), or nil if no intersection occurs.

Functions.Name: intersectRayWithPlane
Functions.Description:
Returns the intersection point for the given ray on the given plane, in world space. 
If no intersection occurs, nil will be returned.

Parameters.Name: rayOrigin
Parameters.Description: The origin of the ray.

Parameters.Name: rayDir
Parameters.Description: The normalised direction of the ray.

Parameters.Name: planePos
Parameters.Description: A position on the plane.

Parameters.Name: planeNormal
Parameters.Description: The plane's normal.

Functions.Name: intersectRayWithSphere
Functions.Description:
Returns the two intersection points for the given ray against the given sphere, in world space. 
Note that the first fraction is always behind the second, and may be negative if the intersection point is behind the origin. 
If no intersection occurs, nil will be returned.

Parameters.Name: rayOrigin
Parameters.Description: The origin of the ray.

Parameters.Name: rayDelta
Parameters.Description: A vector whose direction determines the ray's direction, and whose length determines how far the ray travels.

Parameters.Name: sphereOrigin
Parameters.Description: The origin of the sphere.

Parameters.Name: sphereRadius
Parameters.Description: The radius of the sphere.

ReturnValues.Description: The fraction along the ray at which the first intersection point occurred, or nil if no intersection was found.
The fraction along the ray at which the second intersection point occurred, or nil if no intersection was found.

Functions.Name: isBoxIntersectingSphere
Functions.Description: Returns true if the given box and sphere are intersecting, false otherwise.

Parameters.Name: boxMins
Parameters.Description: The minimum extents of the box.

Parameters.Name: boxMaxs
Parameters.Description: The maximum extents of the box.

Parameters.Name: sphereOrigin
Parameters.Description: The origin of the sphere.

Parameters.Name: sphereRadius
Parameters.Description: The radius of the sphere.

Functions.Name: isOBBIntersectingOBB
Functions.Description: Returns true if the given boxes are intersecting, false otherwise.

Parameters.Name: box1Origin
Parameters.Description: The origin of the first box.

Parameters.Name: box1Angles
Parameters.Description: The angles of the first box.

Parameters.Name: box1Mins
Parameters.Description: The minimum extents of the first box.

Parameters.Name: box1Maxs
Parameters.Description: The maximum extents of the first box.

Parameters.Name: box2Origin
Parameters.Description: The origin of the second box.

Parameters.Name: box2Angles
Parameters.Description: The angles of the second box.

Parameters.Name: box2Mins
Parameters.Description: The minimum extents of the second box.

Parameters.Name: box2Maxs
Parameters.Description: The maximum extents of the second box.

Parameters.Name: tolerance
Parameters.Description: The tolerance to accept when calculating the intersection. Set to 0 for a precise test.

Functions.Name: isPointInCone
Functions.Description: Returns true if the given point is contained within the given cone, false otherwise.

Parameters.Name: point
Parameters.Description: The point to test.

Parameters.Name: coneOrigin
Parameters.Description: The origin of the cone.

Parameters.Name: coneDir
Parameters.Description: The direction the cone is pointing towards.

Parameters.Name: coneSine
Parameters.Description: The sine (calculated with math.sin) of the cone's angle.

Parameters.Name: coneLength
Parameters.Description: The height of the cone.

Functions.Name: isRayIntersectingRay
Functions.Description: Returns true and the intersection point if the given rays intersect, false otherwise.

Parameters.Name: ray1Start
Parameters.Description: The start point of the first ray.

Parameters.Name: ray1End
Parameters.Description: The end point of the first ray.

Parameters.Name: ray2Start
Parameters.Description: The start point of the second ray.

Parameters.Name: ray2End
Parameters.Description: The end point of the second ray.

Parameters.Name: tolerance
Parameters.Description: A tolerance value to use to determine if the rays intersect. If the rays come within this many units of each other along each axis, they will be considered to intersect. The default value is 0.0001 units.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the given rays intersect, false otherwise.
If the rays intersect, the distance from the start of the first ray to the intersection point.
If the rays intersect, the distance from the start of the second ray to the intersection point.

Functions.Name: isSphereIntersectingCone
Functions.Description: Returns true if the given sphere and cone are intersecting, false otherwise.

Parameters.Name: sphereOrigin
Parameters.Description: The origin of the sphere.

Parameters.Name: sphereRadius
Parameters.Description: The radius of the sphere.

Parameters.Name: coneOrigin
Parameters.Description: The origin of the cone.

Parameters.Name: coneDir
Parameters.Description: The direction the cone is pointing towards.

Parameters.Name: coneSine
Parameters.Description: The sine (calculated with math.sin) of the cone's angle.

Parameters.Name: coneCosine
Parameters.Description: The consine (calculated with math.cos) of the cone's angle.

Functions.Name: isSphereIntersectingSphere
Functions.Description: Returns true if the given spheres are intersecting, false otherwise.

Parameters.Name: sphere1Origin
Parameters.Description: The origin of the first sphere.

Parameters.Name: sphere1Radius
Parameters.Description: The radius of the first sphere.

Parameters.Name: sphere2Origin
Parameters.Description: The origin of the second sphere.

Parameters.Name: sphere2Radius
Parameters.Description: The radius of the second sphere.

Functions.Name: traceEntity

Parameters.Name: filter

Libraries.Name: utf8
Libraries.Description: GLua's utf8 library.

Functions.Name: char
Functions.Description: Builds a UTF-8 string with the given codepoints.

Parameters.Description: One or more codepoints.

ReturnValues.Description: The UTF-8 character for each of the given codepoints concatenated into a single string.

Functions.Name: codepoint
Functions.Description: Returns the codepoints contained in the given UTF-8 string between a given start and end position.

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to retrieve the codepoint(s) from.

Parameters.Name: startPos
Parameters.Description: The starting byte position in the string to read from (inclusive, default = 1).

Parameters.Name: endPos
Parameters.Description: The ending byte position in the string to read up to (inclusive, default = 1).

ReturnValues.Description: Each of the codepoints contained between the two byte positions in the string.

Functions.Name: codes
Functions.Description:
Returns an iterator over the codepoints in the given string. 
The loop values are the byte position, and the codepoint respectively.
Functions.Usage:
for BytePos, CodePoint in utf8.codes( "test" ) do 
    print( BytePos, utf8.char( CodePoint ) ) 
end

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to iterate over.

ReturnValues.Description: An iterator function that iterates over the given string's UTF-8 codepoints.

Functions.Name: force
Functions.Description: Returns the given string with any invalid UTF-8 byte sequences replaced by the replacement character (U+FFFD).

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to force into valid UTF-8.

ReturnValues.Description: The string as a valid UTF-8 byte sequence.

Functions.Name: getChar
Functions.Description: Returns the UTF-8 character at the given character index in the given string.

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to get the character from.

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: The index of the character to retrieve.

ReturnValues.Description: The character at the given index.

Functions.Name: len
Functions.Description: Returns the length of the given string in UTF-8 codepoints rather than bytes.

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to get the length of.

Parameters.Name: startPos
Parameters.Description: The starting byte position in the string to count from (default = 1).

Parameters.Name: endPos
Parameters.Description: The ending byte position in the string to count up to (default = #string).

ReturnValues.Description: The string's length in UTF-8 codepoints, or false if the string is not valid UTF-8.
If the string is not valid UTF-8, the byte position of the first invalid byte sequence.

Functions.Name: offset
Functions.Description: Returns the byte offset of the n-th UTF-8 codepoint in the given string.

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to search.

Parameters.Name: n
Parameters.Description: The index of the codepoint to retrieve the byte offset of (negative values start from the end of the string, e.g. -1 is the last character).

Parameters.Name: startPos
Parameters.Description: The byte position to start the search from (default = 1 if N >= 0, #string otherwise).

ReturnValues.Description: The byte offset of the given character.

Functions.Name: sub
Functions.Description: Returns the UTF-8 characters between the given start and end character positions.

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: The string to get the characters from.

Parameters.Name: startPos
Parameters.Description: The starting character position (inclusive).

Parameters.Name: endPos
Parameters.Description: The ending character position (inclusive, default = utf8.len( string )).

ReturnValues.Description: A string containing the characters between the given start and end positions.

Fields.Name: charpattern
Fields.Description: A pattern that matches a single UTF-8 character (for use in find/match/gmatch etc.)

Libraries.Name: util
Libraries.Description:
Utility library. 
Contains functions that don't belong anywhere else.

Functions.Name: isFirstTimePredicted
Functions.Description:
Returns whether the current event is being called for the first time. 
Only has meaning inside predicted events, such as "playerbuttondown".

ReturnValues.Description: True if the current event call is the first prediction.

Functions.Name: isInWorld
Functions.Description: Returns true if the given position is inside the world boundaries.

Parameters.Name: pos
Parameters.Description: The position vector to check.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the given position is inside the world, false otherwise.

Libraries.Name: vgui
Libraries.Description: VGUI library. Used to create basic VGUI controls. Only available to HUDs.

Functions.Name: create
Functions.Description:
Creates and returns a VGUI control. Available classes are: 
* AvatarImage 
* DAdjustableModelPanel 
* DAlphaBar 
* DButton 
* DCollapsibleCategory 
* DCategoryList 
* DCheckBox 
* DCheckBoxLabel 
* DColorButton 
* DColorCombo 
* DColorCube 
* DColorMixer 
* DColumnSheet 
* DComboBox 
* DDrawer 
* DFileBrowser 
* DForm 
* DFrame 
* DGrid 
* DHorizontalDivider 
* DHorizontalScroller 
* DIconBrowser 
* DIconLayout 
* DImage 
* DImageButton 
* DLabel 
* DLabelEditable 
* DListView 
* DMenu 
* DMenuBar 
* DModelPanel 
* DNumberScratch 
* DNumberWang 
* DNumPad 
* DNumSlider 
* DPanel 
* DProgress 
* DProperties 
* DPropertySheet 
* DRGBPicker 
* DScrollPanel 
* DTextEntry 
* DTree 
* DVerticalDivider 
* DVScrollBar

Parameters.Name: class
Parameters.Description: The class name of the control you want to create.

Parameters.Name: parent
Parameters.Description: Optional parent object.

ReturnValues.Description: The created object, or nil if it could not be created (i.e the local player isn't linked or the object doesn't exist).

Functions.Name: isCursorVisible
Functions.Description: Returns whether the mouse cursor is currently visible or not.

Functions.Name: isSupportedControl
Functions.Description:
Indicates whether the given class is supported and valid to pass to vgui.create(). 
Note that this does not check any additional required permissions, it only indicates whether the class is globally whitelisted for use.

Parameters.Name: class
Parameters.Description: The class name of the control to check.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the given class name is supported, false otherwise.

Libraries.Name: von
Libraries.Description: VON library.

Functions.Name: deserialize
Functions.Description: Deserialises a vON encoded string. Returns the deserialised table.

Parameters.Name: string
Parameters.Description: A string of vON encoded data.

ReturnValues.Description: The vON data as a table, or nil if it was not valid vON.

Functions.Name: serialize
Functions.Description: Serialises a table using vON. Returns the serialised string.

Parameters.Name: table
Parameters.Description: The table to be serialised.

ReturnValues.Description: A vON string representing the table.

Libraries.Name: wire
Libraries.Description: Wire library. Handles wire inputs/outputs, wirelinks, etc.

Functions.Name: create
Functions.Description: Wires the input of Entity1 to the output of Entity2.

Parameters.Name: entity1
Parameters.Description: The entity to wire from.

Parameters.Name: entity2
Parameters.Description: The entity to wire to.

Parameters.Name: input
Parameters.Description: The name of the input on self to wire.

Parameters.Name: output
Parameters.Description: The name of the output on Ent to wire to.

ReturnValues.Description: True if successful, false, error if not.

Functions.Name: createInputs
Functions.Description:
Creates/modifies wire inputs. All wire ports must begin with an uppercase 
letter and contain only alphanumeric characters.
Functions.Usage:
-- Creates 2 wire inputs, a string and a number. 
wire.createInputs( { "MyStringInput", "MyNumberInput" }, { "string", "normal" }, { "A string input.", "A number input." } )

Parameters.Name: names
Parameters.Description: An array of input names. May be modified by the function.

Parameters.Name: types
Parameters.Description:
An array of input types. May be modified by the function. See wire.PORT_TYPE_*. 
Available types: 
- "normal"/"number" 
- "string" 
- "vector" 
- "vector2" 
- "vector4" 
- "angle" 
- "table" 
- "array" 
- "wirelink" 
- "entity"

Parameters.Name: descriptions
Parameters.Description: An optional array of input descriptions. May be modified by the function.

Functions.Name: createOutputs
Functions.Description:
Creates/modifies wire outputs. All wire ports must begin with an uppercase 
letter and contain only alphanumeric characters.
Functions.Usage:
-- Creates 2 wire outputs, a string and a number. 
wire.createOutputs( { "MyStringOutput", "MyNumberOutput" }, { "string", "normal" }, { "A string output.", "A number output." } )

Parameters.Name: names
Parameters.Description: An array of output names. May be modified by the function.

Parameters.Name: types
Parameters.Description:
An array of output types. May be modified by the function. See wire.PORT_TYPE_*. 
Available types: 
- "normal"/"number" 
- "string" 
- "vector" 
- "vector2" 
- "vector4" 
- "angle" 
- "table" 
- "array" 
- "entity"

Parameters.Name: descriptions
Parameters.Description: An optional array of output descriptions. May be modified by the function.

Functions.Name: delete
Functions.Description: Deletes the wire leading to the given entity's input.

Parameters.Name: entity
Parameters.Description: The entity to delete the wire from.

Parameters.Name: input
Parameters.Description: The name of the input to unwire.

ReturnValues.Description: True if successful, false, error if not.

Functions.Name: getInputs
Functions.Description: Returns a table of entity's inputs

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: Entity with input(s)

ReturnValues.Description: Table of entity's inputs, if it has any. Otherwise nil.

Functions.Name: getOutputs
Functions.Description: Returns a table of entity's outputs

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: Entity with output(s)

ReturnValues.Description: Table of entity's outputs, if it has any. Otherwise nil.

Functions.Name: self
Functions.Description: Returns the wirelink representing the current instance's entity.

ReturnValues.Description: The wirelink for the current instance's entity.

Functions.Name: setName
Functions.Description: Sets the wire name of the entity (i.e. the name used by the wire namer tool).

Parameters.Name: entity
Parameters.Description: The entity to set the name for.

Parameters.Name: name
Parameters.Description: The name to assign to the entity.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the name was set, false otherwise.
If the name could not be set, the reason why.

Tables.Name: ports
Tables.Description:
Ports table. Reads from this table will read from the wire input 
of the same name. Writes will write to the wire output of the same name.

Tables.Name: AttachmentData
Tables.Description: Attachment data.

Tables.Name: CamData
Tables.Description: A table describing a view to render from. Used by the "calcview" hook.

Fields.Name: angles
Fields.Description: The camera's angles (the forward direction determines the direction of the camera).

Fields.Name: fov
Fields.Description: The field of view of the camera in degrees.

Fields.Name: znear
Fields.Description: The distance to the near-Z clipping plane (anything closer to the origin than this will not render).

Fields.Name: zfar
Fields.Description: The distance to the far-Z clipping plane (anything further away from the origin than this will not render).

Fields.Name: drawviewer
Fields.Description: Whether to draw the player that this hook is executing for. If false, the player's view model will be visible if looking at the player. If true, their world model will be visible instead.

Tables.Name: CameraParameters3D
Tables.Description: Parameters used to start a 3D camera context. All parameters are optional and inherit from the current camera context if omitted (e.g. the player's view).

Fields.Name: x
Fields.Description: The X position on the 2D surface to draw the 3D world.

Fields.Name: y
Fields.Description: The Y position on the 2D surface to draw the 3D world.

Fields.Name: w
Fields.Description: The width to take up on the 2D surface.

Fields.Name: h
Fields.Description: The height to take up on the 2D surface.

Fields.Name: origin
Fields.Description: The origin from which the camera will render in 3D space.

Fields.Name: angles
Fields.Description: The angles determining the direction the camera points in 3D space.

Fields.Name: fov
Fields.Description: The field of view of the camera.

Fields.Name: aspect
Fields.Description: The aspect ratio of the camera. If omitted and a width and height are provided, this will be set to width / height.

Fields.Name: znear
Fields.Description: The distance from the camera to start the near clipping plane.

Fields.Name: zfar
Fields.Description: The distance from the camera to start the far clipping plane.

Fields.Name: ortho
Fields.Description: If an orthographic projection is desired, the rectangle that describes it. If omitted, a normal perspective projection camera is used.

Tables.Name: CameraRect
Tables.Description: A box describing a rectangle relative to a point.

Fields.Name: left
Fields.Description: The left offset of the rectangle.

Fields.Name: right
Fields.Description: The right offset of the rectangle.

Fields.Name: bottom
Fields.Description: The bottom offset of the rectangle.

Fields.Name: top
Fields.Description: The top offset of the rectangle.

Tables.Name: ClippingPlane
Tables.Description: Clipping plane.

Fields.Name: Normal
Fields.Description: The plane's normal vector (local to the entity).

Fields.Name: Origin
Fields.Description: The plane's origin (local to the entity).

Tables.Name: Date
Tables.Description: Represents a date.

Fields.Name: day
Fields.Description: The day of the month (between 1 and 31).

Fields.Name: yday
Fields.Description: The day of the year (between 1 and 365).

Fields.Name: isdst
Fields.Description: Whether the date is expressed using daylight-saving time.

Tables.Name: Environment
Tables.Description: Environment information

Fields.Name: name
Fields.Description: Name of the environment, if it has one.

Fields.Name: air
Fields.Description: A table containing percentage levels of various gasses, summing to 100.

Fields.Name: Entity
Fields.Description: The entity that is responsible for the environment.

Fields.Name: radius
Fields.Description: The radius that the environment encompasses, if the environment is a planet.

Tables.Name: Faction
Tables.Description: Faction details.

Fields.Name: TagColour
Fields.Description: The colour of the faction's tag.

Fields.Name: LeaderID
Fields.Description: The SteamID of the faction leader.

Fields.Name: Public
Fields.Description: Whether the faction is public or not.

Tables.Name: Font
Tables.Description: Font parameters used when creating fonts.

Fields.Name: font
Fields.Description: The name of the font to use (e.g. "Arial", "Tahoma", default is "Arial").

Fields.Name: size
Fields.Description: The size of the font in pixels (between 4 and 255, default is 13).

Fields.Name: weight
Fields.Description: The weight of the font (how thick it is, minimum of 100, default is 500).

Fields.Name: blursize
Fields.Description: The strength of blurring to apply (between 0 and 10, default is 0).

Fields.Name: antialias
Fields.Description: Whether to anti-alias the text, which smooths/blurs the edges (default is true).

Fields.Name: underline
Fields.Description: Whether to add an underline to the font (default is false).

Fields.Name: italic
Fields.Description: Whether the font is italic or not (note that fonts that are already italic do not need this, default is false).

Fields.Name: strikeout
Fields.Description: Whether to strikeout the font (default is false).

Fields.Name: symbol
Fields.Description: Required when using a symbol font such as Wingdings (default is false).

Fields.Name: shadow
Fields.Description: Whether to add a shadow to the font (default is false).

Fields.Name: additive
Fields.Description: Whether the font should render additively or not (default is false).

Fields.Name: outline
Fields.Description: Whether to add an outline to the font (default is false).

Fields.Name: extended
Fields.Description: Whether the font can be used to display characters outside the Latin-1 range. Code points past 0xFFFF are not supported (default is true).

Tables.Name: HTTPRequest
Tables.Description: A table describing an HTTP request.

Fields.Name: url
Fields.Description: The URL to send the request to.

Fields.Name: method
Fields.Description: The HTTP method to use (case-insensitive). Valid values: GET, HEAD, OPTIONS, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE.

Fields.Name: headers
Fields.Description: (Optional) Headers to send in the request. When sending a body, specifying a "content-type" header is recommended.

Fields.Name: parameters
Fields.Description: (Optional) The parameters to encode as a form-encoded body or query parameters. Valid only for GET/HEAD (query parameters) and POST (body) requests. Ignored for POST requests if "body" is provided.

Fields.Name: body
Fields.Description: (Optional) The request body to send. If set, will override "parameters" on POST requests. Ignored for GET and HEAD requests.

Fields.Name: timeout
Fields.Description: (Optional) The number of seconds to wait for a response before giving up (default: 60).

Fields.Name: onSuccess
Fields.Description: The function to call on a successful response, will be invoked with the response body, response headers, and response code.

Fields.Name: onFailed
Fields.Description: The function to call if the request fails, will be invoked with an error message.

Tables.Name: Halo
Tables.Description: Halo definition.

Fields.Name: BlurX
Fields.Description: How much to blur along the x-axis.

Fields.Name: BlurY
Fields.Description: How much to blur along the y-axis.

Fields.Name: DrawPasses
Fields.Description: The number of iterations to draw the halo.

Fields.Name: Additive
Fields.Description: Whether the halo should be drawn additively or not.

Fields.Name: IgnoreZ
Fields.Description: Whether the halo should be visible through other objects or not.

Tables.Name: HologramClip
Tables.Description: Clipping plane structure used by holograms.

Fields.Name: Normal
Fields.Description: The normal direction of the clipping plane.

Fields.Name: Origin
Fields.Description: The origin of the clipping plane (local or world depending on IsLocal/LocalToEntity).

Fields.Name: IsLocal
Fields.Description: Flag indicating whether the clipping plane is local to the hologram. If false, the clipping plane may be in world space or local to another entity (see the LocalToEntity field).

Fields.Name: LocalToEntity
Fields.Description: The entity the clipping plane is local to, if any.

Tables.Name: LocalLight
Tables.Description: A local light definition to be used with render.setLocalModelLights().

Fields.Name: type
Fields.Description: The type of light to apply (one of render.MATERIAL_LIGHT_*, default = render.MATERIAL_LIGHT_POINT).

Fields.Name: color
Fields.Description: The colour of the light (x = red, y = green, z = blue). Larger values increase the brightness.

Fields.Name: pos
Fields.Description: The position of the light in world space.

Fields.Name: dir
Fields.Description: The direction the light is pointing (applicable to directional and spot lights only).

Fields.Name: range
Fields.Description: The maximum range of the light source. This does not control attenuation, it merely sets a cutoff point. The default value of 0 is infinite range.

Fields.Name: angularFalloff
Fields.Description: The angular falloff exponent for spot lights. Higher values result in a sharper transition between the inner and outer cone (default = 5).

Fields.Name: innerAngle
Fields.Description: The inner cone angle for spot lights, in degrees (default = 45).

Fields.Name: outerAngle
Fields.Description: The outer cone angle for spot lights, in degrees (default = 45).

Fields.Name: fiftyPercentDistance
Fields.Description: The distance from the light's position at which the light will be at 50% brightness.

Fields.Name: zeroPercentDistance
Fields.Description: The distance from the light's position at which the light will fade out entirely.

Fields.Name: quadraticFalloff
Fields.Description: If the distance values are not supplied, this provides the quadratic term of the falloff (default = 0).

Fields.Name: linearFalloff
Fields.Description: If the distance values are not supplied, this provides the linear term of the falloff (default = 0).

Fields.Name: constantFalloff
Fields.Description: If the distance values are not supplied, this provides the constant term of the falloff (default = 1).

Tables.Name: MeshVertex
Tables.Description: Mesh vertex structure used by IMesh:buildFromTriangles().

Fields.Name: normal
Fields.Description: (Optional) The normal of the vertex, used to calculate lighting.

Fields.Name: color
Fields.Description: (Optional) The colour to assign to the vertex (for $vertexcolor/$vertexalpha materials).

Fields.Name: userdata
Fields.Description: (Optional) Tangent data for VertexLitGeneric materials (x, y, z, handedness).

Fields.Name: u
Fields.Description: The u co-ordinate of the vertex.

Fields.Name: v
Fields.Description: The v co-ordinate of the vertex.

Fields.Name: tangent
Fields.Description: (Optional) The tangent of the vertex (not used by VertexLitGeneric materials).

Fields.Name: binormal
Fields.Description: (Optional) The binormal of the vertex (not used by VertexLitGeneric materials).

Tables.Name: PhysEnvSettings
Tables.Description: Physics environment settings.

Fields.Name: LookAheadTimeObjectsVsObject
Fields.Description: Maximum amount of seconds to precalculate collisions with objects.

Fields.Name: LookAheadTimeObjectsVsWorld
Fields.Description: Maximum amount of seconds to precalculate collisions with world.

Fields.Name: MaxCollisionsPerObjectPerTimestep
Fields.Description: Maximum collision per object per tick.

Fields.Name: MaxFrictionMass
Fields.Description: Maximum mass of an object to be affected by friction.

Fields.Name: MinFrictionMass
Fields.Description: Minimum mass of an object to be affected by friction.

Fields.Name: DefaultClippingPlanes
Fields.Description: A list of clipping planes to apply by default on unclipped entities.

Fields.Description: Whether the clipping planes should be applied visually only.

Tables.Description: Position and angle data.

Tables.Name: PropResizerOptions
Tables.Description: Prop resizer options. All fields are optional.

Fields.Name: Mode
Fields.Description: The resizer mode to use (one of "ENTITY", "RING" or "TORUS").

Fields.Name: UseSize
Fields.Description: Whether to enable size-based resizing (if Mode is "ENTITY").

Fields.Name: Scale
Fields.Description: The scale to configure the tool with, if UseSize is false (if Mode is "ENTITY").

Fields.Name: Size
Fields.Description: The size to use, if UseSize is true (if Mode is "ENTITY").

Fields.Name: YX
Fields.Description: The YX shearing factor (if Mode is "ENTITY").

Fields.Name: ZX
Fields.Description: The ZX shearing factor (if Mode is "ENTITY").

Fields.Name: XY
Fields.Description: The XY shearing factor (if Mode is "ENTITY").

Fields.Name: ZY
Fields.Description: The ZY shearing factor (if Mode is "ENTITY").

Fields.Name: XZ
Fields.Description: The XZ shearing factor (if Mode is "ENTITY").

Fields.Name: YZ
Fields.Description: The YZ shearing factor (if Mode is "ENTITY").

Fields.Name: RingAdvancedMode
Fields.Description: Whether to enable advanced ring-resizing options (if Mode is "RING").

Fields.Name: RingInnerRadius
Fields.Description: The inner radius of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is false).

Fields.Name: RingOuterRadius
Fields.Description: The outer radius of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is false).

Fields.Name: RingHeight
Fields.Description: The height of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is false).

Fields.Name: RingInnerLowerHeight
Fields.Description: The inner-lower height of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is true).

Fields.Name: RingInnerUpperHeight
Fields.Description: The inner-upper height of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is true).

Fields.Name: RingOuterLowerHeight
Fields.Description: The outer-lower height of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is true).

Fields.Name: RingOuterUpperHeight
Fields.Description: The outer-upper height of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is true).

Fields.Name: RingInnerLowerRadius
Fields.Description: The inner-lower radius of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is true).

Fields.Name: RingOuterLowerRadius
Fields.Description: The outer-lower radius of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is true).

Fields.Name: RingInnerUpperRadius
Fields.Description: The inner-upper radius of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is true).

Fields.Name: RingOuterUpperRadius
Fields.Description: The outer-upper radius of the ring to use (if Mode is "RING" and RingAdvancedMode is true).

Fields.Name: TorusRingRadius
Fields.Description: The radius of the torus ring to use (if Mode is "TORUS").

Fields.Name: TorusTubeRadius
Fields.Description: The radius of the torus tube to use (if Mode is "TORUS").

Tables.Name: RenderOverlayData
Tables.Description: Provides metadata for overlay rendering.

Fields.Name: DefaultText
Fields.Description: The default overlay text that would have been rendered.

Fields.Name: ServerCPUTime
Fields.Description: The CPU time used on the server in the last tick (in microseconds), if a server instance is running.

Fields.Name: ServerAverageCPUTime
Fields.Description: The average CPU time used on the server (in microseconds), if a server instance is running.

Tables.Name: ScreenInfo
Tables.Description: Screen information

Fields.Name: RatioX
Fields.Description: Inverted Aspect ratio (height divided by width)

Fields.Name: x1
Fields.Description: Corner of screen in local co-ordinates (relative to offset)

Fields.Name: x2
Fields.Description: Corner of screen in local co-ordinates (relative to offset)

Fields.Name: y1
Fields.Description: Corner of screen in local co-ordinates (relative to offset)

Fields.Name: y2
Fields.Description: Corner of screen in local co-ordinates (relative to offset)

Fields.Name: z
Fields.Description: Screen plane offset in local co-ordinates (relative to offset)

Fields.Name: translucent
Fields.Description: Whether the screen is set to render without a background.

Tables.Name: ScreenParameters
Tables.Description:
Defines how a screen is rendered in the world, relative to the screen entity. 
All fields are optional, those that are not specified will use their default value.

Fields.Name: WidthMultiplier
Fields.Description: The multiplier to apply to the screen's width, relative to its height. For example, a value of 2 would mean the screen's width is twice its height.

Fields.Name: Offset
Fields.Description: The entity-local offset of the screen's centre. This must be within the entity's bounding box.

Fields.Name: Angles
Fields.Description: The entity-local angles that define how the screen is oriented.

Fields.Name: Scale
Fields.Description: A scaling factor to apply when drawing the screen, controlling its size. This must not cause the screen to exceed the size of the entity.

Fields.Name: Translucent
Fields.Description: Whether the screen is translucent or not (if false or nil, a black background is always rendered).

Tables.Name: ToScreenData
Tables.Description: Data indicating screen position of a world position.

Fields.Name: x
Fields.Description: The x position on the screen.

Fields.Name: y
Fields.Description: The y position on the screen.

Fields.Name: visible
Fields.Description: Whether the position is visible or not.

Tables.Name: TraceResult
Tables.Description: Trace result structure.

Fields.Name: Entity
Fields.Description: The entity that the trace hit.

Fields.Name: Fraction
Fields.Description: How far along the trace ray the hit occurred, from 0-1.

Fields.Name: FractionLeftSolid
Fields.Description: Given the trace started inside a solid object, this is how far along the ray (0-1) the trace left the solid object.

Fields.Name: Hit
Fields.Description: Whether the trace hit an obstacle.

Fields.Name: HitBox
Fields.Description: The hit box ID the trace hit.

Fields.Name: HitGroup
Fields.Description: The hit group the trace hit.

Fields.Name: HitNoDraw
Fields.Description: Whether the trace hit a no-draw brush.

Fields.Name: HitNonWorld
Fields.Description: Whether the trace hit something that is not part of the world.

Fields.Name: HitNormal
Fields.Description: The normal to the trace's impact position.

Fields.Name: HitSky
Fields.Description: Whether the trace hit the skybox.

Fields.Name: HitTexture
Fields.Description: The name of the texture the trace hit.

Fields.Name: HitWorld
Fields.Description: Whether the trace hit the world.

Fields.Name: MatType
Fields.Description: The type of material the trace hit.

Fields.Name: Normal
Fields.Description: The direction vector of the trace as a normalised vector.

Fields.Name: PhysicsBone
Fields.Description: The physics bone ID that was hit.

Fields.Name: StartPos
Fields.Description: The starting position of the trace.

Fields.Name: StartSolid
Fields.Description: Whether the trace started inside a solid object.

Fields.Name: AllSolid
Fields.Description: Whether the trace is entirely inside a solid object.

Fields.Name: SurfaceFlags
Fields.Description: The surface flags of the hit surface (see trace.SURF_*).

Fields.Name: DispFlags
Fields.Description: The displacement flags of the this surface (see trace.DISPSURF_*).

Fields.Name: Contents
Fields.Description: The contents flags of the hit surface (see trace.CONTENTS_*).

Tables.Name: Vertex
Tables.Description: Vertex format

Tables.Name: WireOutputConnection
Tables.Description: Represents a wire output connection to another entity's input.

Fields.Name: Name
Fields.Description: The name of the input on the connected entity that the output is connected to.

Fields.Name: p
Fields.Description: The pitch (rotation around the y-axis) of the angle (also available at index 1).

Fields.Name: y
Fields.Description: The yaw (rotation around the z-axis) of the angle (also available at index 2).

Fields.Name: r
Fields.Description: The roll (rotation around the x-axis) of the angle (also available at index 3).

Methods.Name: Add
Methods.Description: Adds the given argument angle's component values to the given angle, modifying in-place.

Parameters.Name: angle
Parameters.Description: The angle to add.

ReturnValues.Description: The modified angle.

Methods.Name: Div
Methods.Description: Divides the given angle's component values by the given number, modifying in-place.

Parameters.Name: divisor
Parameters.Description: The number value to divide the angle's components by.

ReturnValues.Description: The modified angle.

Methods.Name: Forward
Methods.Description: Returns the forward direction for the given angles.

Methods.Name: IsZero
Methods.Description: Returns true if the angle has all components equal to 0.

Methods.Name: Mul
Methods.Description: Multiplies the given angle's component values by the given number, modifying in-place.

Parameters.Name: multiplier
Parameters.Description: The number value to multiply the angle's components by.

ReturnValues.Description: The modified angle.

Methods.Name: Normalize
Methods.Description: Normalises the given angle's components into the (-180, 180] range, modifying in-place.

ReturnValues.Description: The modified angle.

Methods.Name: Right
Methods.Description: Returns the right direction for the given angles.

Methods.Name: RotateAroundAxis
Methods.Description: Rotates the given angles around the given axis by the given amount in degrees, modifying in-place.

Parameters.Name: axis
Parameters.Description: The axis vector to rotate the angles around.

Parameters.Name: amount
Parameters.Description: The amount in degrees to rotate by.

ReturnValues.Description: The modified angle.

Methods.Name: Set
Methods.Description: Sets the angle's component values to equal the given angles, modifying in-place.

Parameters.Name: angle
Parameters.Description: The angle to take the component values from.

ReturnValues.Description: The modified angle.

Methods.Name: SetUnpacked
Methods.Description: Sets the angle's component values, modifying in-place.

Parameters.Name: pitch
Parameters.Description: The pitch to set.

Parameters.Name: yaw
Parameters.Description: The yaw to set.

Parameters.Name: roll
Parameters.Description: The roll to set.

ReturnValues.Description: The modified angle.

Methods.Name: SnapTo
Methods.Description: Rounds a component of the given angles to the given amount of degrees.

Parameters.Name: component
Parameters.Description: The component to be snapped (e.g. "p", "y", "r").

Parameters.Name: targetDegrees
Parameters.Description: The amount of degrees to round to (e.g. 90 would round to the nearest multiple of 90 degrees).

ReturnValues.Description: The modified angle.

Methods.Name: Sub
Methods.Description: Subtracts the given argument angles from the given angles, modifying in-place.

Parameters.Name: angle
Parameters.Description: The angle to subtract.

ReturnValues.Description: The modified angle.

Methods.Name: ToTable
Methods.Description: Returns the angles as an array of 3 elements.

Methods.Name: Unpack
Methods.Description: Returns the component values of the angle.

ReturnValues.Description: The pitch value.
The yaw value.
The roll value.

Methods.Name: Up
Methods.Description: Returns the up direction for the given angles.

Methods.Name: Zero
Methods.Description: Sets all component values to 0, modifying in-place..

ReturnValues.Description: The modified angle.

Operators.Name: mul_Angle_number
Operators.Description: Multiplies an angle by a scalar value.

ReturnValues.Description: A new angle whose values are equal to those in the original angle multiplied by the given number.

Operators.Name: div_Angle_number
Operators.Description: Divides an angle by a scalar value.

ReturnValues.Description: A new angle whose values are equal to those in the original angle divided by the given number.

Operators.Name: add_Angle_Angle
Operators.Description: Adds two angles.

ReturnValues.Description: A new angle whose values are the sum of the values in the given vectors.

Operators.Name: sub_Angle_Angle
Operators.Description: Subtracts one angle's values from another.

ReturnValues.Description: A new angle whose values are the values in the first angle minus those in the second.

Operators.Name: unm_Angle_nil
Operators.Description: Returns the negation of the given angle.

ReturnValues.Description: A new angle whose values are the negation of the given angle's.

Operators.Name: eq_Angle_Angle
Operators.Description: Determines if the given angles are equal by checking each component.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the 2 angles have the same component values, false otherwise.

Classes.Name: ChairGhost
Classes.Description: Chair ghost, representing a TSCM chair tool seating position.

Methods.Name: cameraDistance
Methods.Description: Returns the current camera distance for the vehicle associated with this seating position, or 0 if no player is sat down.

Methods.Name: driver
Methods.Description: Returns the player that is currently sat in this seating position, or nil if no player is sat down.

Methods.Name: getExitPos
Methods.Description: Returns the currently configured exit position for the chair in world space.

Methods.Name: passenger
Methods.Description:
Returns the passenger of this seating position. 
Note that the only valid passenger index is 0, for the player sat in the seat. 
This method is provided to match the Vehicle interface.

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: The 0-based passenger index to retrieve.

Methods.Name: thirdPersonMode
Methods.Description: Returns whether the vehicle associated with this seating position is in third person mode or not.

Methods.Name: drawModel
Methods.Description:
Draws the model at the clientside model's position and angles. 
Allowed render contexts: 3D camera.

Methods.Name: getModelScale
Methods.Description: Returns the current model scale of the clientside model (as set by setModelScale()).

Methods.Name: getRenderMatrix
Methods.Description: Returns the current rendering matrix for the given clientside model, if it has one.

Methods.Name: getScaleVector
Methods.Description: Returns the current scaling vector applied to the clientside model.

Methods.Name: remove
Methods.Description:
Removes the clientside model. Call this if you don't need it anymore. 
Attempting to use it again will fail.

Methods.Name: setAng
Methods.Description: Sets the clientside model's angles for rendering.

Parameters.Name: ang
Parameters.Description: Angles in the current 3D world to render with.

Methods.Name: setAnim
Methods.Description: Animates the given clientside model.

Parameters.Name: anim
Parameters.Description: Animation enum/sequence number, or nil to stop animating.

Parameters.Name: cycle
Parameters.Description: (Optional) How far through the animation to start (between 0 and 1, default = 0).

Parameters.Name: rate
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Playback rate for the animation (default = 1).

Methods.Name: setBodygroup
Methods.Description: Sets the given bodygroup.

Parameters.Name: id
Parameters.Description: Body group ID.

Parameters.Name: subID
Parameters.Description: Sub ID to use.

Methods.Name: setLOD
Methods.Description: Sets the level of detail for the clientside model.

Parameters.Name: level
Parameters.Description: A number between 0 and 8 for fixed quality (0 being highest quality), or -1 for automatic based on distance.

Methods.Name: setMaterial
Methods.Description: Sets the material of the clientside model.

Parameters.Name: mat
Parameters.Description: Material path to set.

Methods.Name: setModel
Methods.Description: Sets the clientside model's model path.

Parameters.Name: model
Parameters.Description: Model path to use.

Methods.Name: setModelScale
Methods.Description: Sets the model scale of the given clientside model.

Parameters.Name: scale
Parameters.Description: The scale value.

Parameters.Name: deltaTime
Parameters.Description: The time to take when scaling.

Methods.Name: setPos
Methods.Description: Sets the clientside model's position for rendering.

Parameters.Name: vec
Parameters.Description: Position in the current 3D world to render at.

Methods.Name: setPoseParameter
Methods.Description: Sets the pose parameter with the given key to the given value.

Parameters.Name: key
Parameters.Description: The pose parameter ID or name to set.

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: The value to set on the given pose parameter.

Methods.Name: setRenderMatrix
Methods.Description: Sets the rendering matrix for the given clientside model.

Parameters.Name: renderMatrix
Parameters.Description: The matrix to apply to this model when rendering, or nil to reset it.

Methods.Name: setRenderMode
Methods.Description: Sets the render mode of the clientside model.

Parameters.Name: renderMode
Parameters.Description: Rendering mode, from ents.RENDERMODE_*

Methods.Name: setScaleVector
Methods.Description: Sets the scale vector of the given clientside model.

Parameters.Name: scaleVec
Parameters.Description: The vector scale to apply.

Methods.Name: setSkin
Methods.Description: Sets the skin to use on the clientside model.

Parameters.Name: skin
Parameters.Description: Skin index to use.

Methods.Name: setSubMaterial
Methods.Description: Sets the sub-material at the given index on the clientside model.

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: Sub-material index.

Parameters.Name: mat
Parameters.Description: Material path.

Classes.Name: Color
Classes.Description:
Colours in RGB space. 
This is a direct binding to the GLua colour type.

Methods.Name: SetUnpacked
Methods.Description: Sets the RGBA components of the colour.

Parameters.Name: r
Parameters.Description: The red value to set.

Parameters.Name: g
Parameters.Description: The green value to set.

Parameters.Name: b
Parameters.Description: The blue value to set.

Parameters.Name: a
Parameters.Description: The alpha value to set.

Methods.Name: ToHSL
Methods.Description: Returns the colour in HSL space.

ReturnValues.Description: The hue of the colour in degrees between 0 and 360.
The saturation of the colour between 0 and 1.
The lightness of the colour between 0 and 1.

Methods.Name: ToHSV
Methods.Description: Returns the colour in HSV space.

ReturnValues.Description: The hue of the colour in degrees between 0 and 360.
The saturation of the colour between 0 and 1.
The value of the colour between 0 and 1.

Methods.Name: ToNormalizedHSV
Methods.Description: Returns the colour in HSV space, normalising the hue to between 0 and 1.

ReturnValues.Description: The hue of the colour between 0 and 1.
The saturation of the colour between 0 and 1.
The value of the colour between 0 and 1.

Methods.Name: ToTable
Methods.Description: Returns the colour as an (r, g, b, a) array.

Methods.Name: ToVector
Methods.Description: Returns the RGB values as a Vector, with each component normalised to be between 0 and 1.

Methods.Name: Unpack
Methods.Description: Returns the components of the colour.

ReturnValues.Description: The red value.
The green value.
The blue value.
The alpha value.

Operators.Name: add_Color_Color
Operators.Description: Adds the given colour values field-by-field (including alpha).

ReturnValues.Description: A new colour whose values are the sum of those in the given colours.

Operators.Name: sub_Color_Color
Operators.Description: Subtracts the second colour's values from the first field-by-field (including alpha).

ReturnValues.Description: A new colour whose values are the second colour's values subtracted from the first.

Operators.Name: mul_Color_number
Operators.Description: Multiplies the given colour's RGBA values by the given number.

ReturnValues.Description: A new colour whose values are multiplied by the given number.

Operators.Name: div_Color_number
Operators.Description: Divides the given colour's RGBA values by the given number.

ReturnValues.Description: A new colour whose values are divided by the given number.

Methods.Name: setAngles

Parameters.Description: Entity to set.

Parameters.Name: num
Parameters.Description: Number magnitude.

Methods.Name: setOrigin

Methods.Name: addCallback
Methods.Description: Adds a callback to an entity for a specific event. Valid events are: - PhysicsCollide

Parameters.Name: name
Parameters.Description: The name of the event to add a callback for.

Parameters.Name: identifier
Parameters.Description: The identifier for the event.

Parameters.Name: func
Parameters.Description: The function to call when the event is fired.

Methods.Name: ang
Methods.Description: Returns the angle of the entity

ReturnValues.Description: The angle

Methods.Name: attachments
Methods.Description: Returns a table of all attachments the entity's model has, or nil if it has none.

Methods.Name: boundingRadius
Methods.Description:
Returns the distance to the furthest corner on the entity's bounding box. 
Note that this will return 0 on clientside models as it is derived from collision data.

Methods.Name: callOnRemove
Methods.Description: Registers a callback to run on removal of this entity.

Parameters.Name: identifier
Parameters.Description: A unique string identifier for this callback. Used if you want to remove it later.

Parameters.Name: callback
Parameters.Description: The function to run when the entity is removed. Receives the entity, then the arguments passed to this.
Arguments to pass to the callback.

Methods.Name: class
Methods.Description: Returns the class of the entity

ReturnValues.Description: The string class name

Methods.Name: color
Methods.Description: Returns the entity's colour.

Methods.Name: coreHealth
Methods.Description: Returns the health of the ship core the entity is assigned to.

Methods.Name: coreMaxHealth
Methods.Description: Returns the maximum health of the ship core the entity is assigned to.

Methods.Name: createWire
Methods.Description: Wires the input of self to the output of Ent.

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: The entity to wire to.

Parameters.Name: input
Parameters.Description: The name of the input on self to wire.

Parameters.Name: output
Parameters.Description: The name of the output on Ent to wire to.

ReturnValues.Description: True if successful, false, error if not.

Methods.Name: deleteWire
Methods.Description: Deletes the wire leading to self's input.

Parameters.Name: input
Parameters.Description: The name of the input to unwire.

ReturnValues.Description: True if successful, false, error if not.

Methods.Name: entHealth
Methods.Description: Returns the entity's current health.

Methods.Name: entMaxHealth
Methods.Description: Returns the entity's maximum health.

Methods.Name: eyeAngles
Methods.Description: Gets the entitiy's eye angles

Methods.Name: eyePos
Methods.Description: Gets the entity's eye position

Methods.Name: followBone
Methods.Description: Parents the entity to the given parent's bone.

Parameters.Name: parent
Parameters.Description: The parent entity to attach this entity to.

Parameters.Name: boneID
Parameters.Description: The ID of the bone on the parent to attach to.

Methods.Name: forward
Methods.Description: Gets the entity's forward direction vector.

Methods.Name: getAttachment
Methods.Description: Returns a table containing the position and angles of the given attachment, if it exists.

Parameters.Name: attachment
Parameters.Description: The attachment name to get the position/angle for.

ReturnValues.Description: A table with fields "Pos" and "Ang", or nil if no such attachment exists.

Methods.Name: getBodygroup
Methods.Description: Gets the bodygroup the current ID is using.

Parameters.Name: id
Parameters.Description: Bodygroup ID.

Methods.Name: getBodygroupCount
Methods.Description: Gets the bodygroup count.

Parameters.Name: id
Parameters.Description: Bodygroup ID.

Methods.Name: getBoneCount
Methods.Description: Gets the number of bones the entity's model has defined.

ReturnValues.Description: The number of bones on the entity, or -1 if the number is not known.

Methods.Name: getBoneName
Methods.Description: Gets the nice name of the bone with the given ID on the entity.

Parameters.Name: boneID
Parameters.Description: The ID of the bone to get the name of.

ReturnValues.Description: The name of the bone, or nil if no such bone exists.

Methods.Name: getBoneParent
Methods.Description: Gets the ID of the bone that is the parent bone for the given bone ID on the entity.

Parameters.Name: boneID
Parameters.Description: The ID of the bone to get the parent bone ID for.

ReturnValues.Description: The ID of the parent bone, or -1 if no parent bone exists.

Methods.Name: getBonePosition
Methods.Description: Gets the position and angles of the bone with the given ID on the entity.

Parameters.Name: boneID
Parameters.Description: The ID of the bone to get the position of.

ReturnValues.Description: The world position of the bone, or nil if no such bone exists.
The world angles of the bone.

Methods.Name: getChildBones
Methods.Description: Gets the IDs of all child bones for the given bone ID on the entity.

Parameters.Name: boneID
Parameters.Description: The ID of the bone to get child bones for.

ReturnValues.Description: A table containing all child bone IDs, or nil if no such bone exists.

Methods.Name: getChildren
Methods.Description: Returns a list of all child entities parented to this entity.

ReturnValues.Description: A table containing all child entities.

Methods.Name: getClippingPlanes
Methods.Description: Returns the clipping planes applied to the entity using the physical clipping tool.

ReturnValues.Description: A table of clipping planes if the entity has been clipped, otherwise nil.

Methods.Name: getCollisionGroup
Methods.Description: Gets the collision group of an entity.

ReturnValues.Description: The collision group (one of ents.COLLISION_GROUP_*)

Methods.Name: getConstraints
Methods.Description: Gets a table of all constrained entities.

Parameters.Name: filter
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Filter function, return true to keep the entity, false to discard it.

Methods.Name: getCore
Methods.Description: Returns the ship core that the entity is assigned to, or nil if it does not have one.

Methods.Name: getCreationID
Methods.Description: Returns the creation ID of the entity.

Methods.Name: getCycle
Methods.Description: Returns the current cycle (frame) of the playing sequence (animation), as a value between 0 and 1.

Methods.Name: getDoorState
Methods.Description: Returns the state of an SBEP door.

Methods.Name: getDoors
Methods.Description: Returns the door entities an SBEP door controller is controlling.

Methods.Name: getEFlags
Methods.Description: Returns a mask indicating the EFL_* flags the entity has currently set.

Methods.Name: getEnvironmentData
Methods.Description: Gets the environment data table of an entity.

Methods.Name: getFlags
Methods.Description: Returns a mask indicating the FL_* flags the entity has currently set.

Methods.Name: getGroundEntity
Methods.Description: Returns the entity that this entity is currently standing on.

ReturnValues.Description: The entity below this entity.

Methods.Name: getMapCreationID
Methods.Description: Returns the map creation ID of the entity (or -1 if the entity is not part of the map).

Methods.Name: getMaterials
Methods.Description:
Returns all materials on the given entity. 
Use the index value - 1 in Entity:setSubMaterial() to replace it. 
Always returns the original materials, is not affected by Entity:setSubMaterial().

Methods.Name: getModelBounds
Methods.Description:
Returns the min and max bounds for the entity's model, ignoring its current scale. 
This works on normal entities and clientside models.

ReturnValues.Description: The position of the local minimum corner of the model's bounding box.
The position of the local maximum corner of the model's bounding box.

Methods.Name: getNode
Methods.Description: Returns the resource node the entity is connected to, or nil.

ReturnValues.Description: Resource node entity, potentially two of them for a resource valve.

Methods.Name: getNumPoseParameters
Methods.Description: Returns the number of pose parameters assigned to the entity's current model.

Methods.Name: getParent
Methods.Description:
Returns the parent of the given entity. 
On the server, the instance owner must have toolgun permission on the entity to see its parent. 
On the client, the entity owner must be known and match the instance owner to see its parent.

Methods.Name: getPhysicsObject
Methods.Description: Gets the physics object associated with the entity.

ReturnValues.Description: The physics object, or nil if it does not have one.

Methods.Name: getPhysicsObjectCount
Methods.Description: Gets the number of physics objects the entity has.

ReturnValues.Description: The number of physics objects present.

Methods.Name: getPhysicsObjectNum
Methods.Description:
Gets the physics object at the given index. 
Note: indices are 0 based.

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: The index of the physics object, starting from 0.

ReturnValues.Description: The physics object, or nil if no such physics object exists.

Methods.Name: getPlaybackRate
Methods.Description: Returns the current playback rate of the playing sequence (animation).

Methods.Name: getPoseParameter
Methods.Description:
Gets the given pose parameter's current value. 
Note that on the client, this value is between 0 and 1, whereas on the server this is between the minimum and maximum value in the range.

Parameters.Name: id
Parameters.Description: The ID of the pose parameter to look up (0-based).

Methods.Name: getPoseParameterName
Methods.Description: Returns the name of the pose parameter with the given ID.

Parameters.Name: id
Parameters.Description: The ID of the pose parameter to look up (0-based).

Methods.Name: getPoseParameterRange
Methods.Description: Returns the valid range of values for the pose parameter with the given ID.

Parameters.Name: id
Parameters.Description: The ID of the pose parameter to get the range for (0-based).

ReturnValues.Description: The minimum value of the given pose parameter, if the ID is valid. Otherwise nil.
The maximum value of the given pose parameter, if the ID is valid. Otherwise nil.

Methods.Name: getPrintName
Methods.Description: Returns the print name of the entity if it has one, otherwise nil.

Methods.Name: getPropScale
Methods.Description: Returns the scaling used to resize the given entity, or 1 if no scaling is applied.

ReturnValues.Description: Vector or number scale value, depending on the settings used to resize the entity.

Methods.Name: getRenderMode
Methods.Description: Returns the entity's current render mode. See the ents.RENDERMODE_* enums for possible values.

ReturnValues.Description: The entity's current render mode.

Methods.Name: getResourceAmount
Methods.Description: Retrieves the current supply of the given resource.

Parameters.Name: resource
Parameters.Description: String resource name, e.g "energy".

Methods.Name: getRotatedAABB
Methods.Description:
Returns world-space extents for the entity given local-space mins and maxs. 
Unlike using localToWorld, this creates a true AABB that encompasses the given space regardless of rotation.

Parameters.Name: mins
Parameters.Description: The OBB mins vector to start the box at.

Parameters.Name: maxs
Parameters.Description: The OBB maxs vector to end the box at.

ReturnValues.Description: Mins, Maxs vectors which represent a world-space AABB containing the entire box given (and often more).

Methods.Name: getScreen
Methods.Description: Returns the screen a repeater is linked to.

ReturnValues.Description: Linked Starfall screen, or nil if no screen is linked.

Methods.Name: getSequence
Methods.Description: Returns the current sequence (animation) playing on the given entity.

Methods.Name: getSequenceList
Methods.Description: Returns a list of all sequences (animations) on the model.

Methods.Name: getSequenceName
Methods.Description: Returns the name of the given sequence (animation) index.

Parameters.Name: sequence
Parameters.Description: Sequence index.

Methods.Name: getSingleConstrainedEnts
Methods.Description: Gets all entities constrained to self, non-recursive.

Methods.Name: getSubMaterial
Methods.Description: Returns the currently set sub-material at the given index.

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: The index from Entity:getMaterials(), minus 1.

Methods.Name: getSubMaterialColor
Methods.Description: Returns the currently set sub-material colour at the given index.

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: The index from Entity:getMaterials(), minus 1.

Methods.Name: getSubsystemHealth
Methods.Description: Returns the entity's subsystem health, if it has any. Otherwise nil.

ReturnValues.Description: Subsystem health (between 0 and 100), or nil if the entity is not a subsystem.

Methods.Name: getUnitCapacity
Methods.Description: Retrieves the maximum supply of the given resource.

Parameters.Name: resource
Parameters.Description: String resource name, e.g "energy".

Methods.Name: getWireName
Methods.Description: Gets the name set with the wire namer tool.

Methods.Name: getWirelink
Methods.Description: Gets the wirelink associated with the given entity (if the entity is wireable).

ReturnValues.Description: A wirelink for the given entity, if it has inputs/outputs. Otherwise nil.

Methods.Name: getWorldTransformMatrix
Methods.Description:
Returns the entity's world transform matrix. 
The first 3 columns are a rotation matrix representing the entity's angles, and the last column is its position.

ReturnValues.Description: The entity's world transform as a matrix.

Methods.Name: index
Methods.Description: Returns the EntIndex of the entity

ReturnValues.Description: The numerical index of the entity

Methods.Name: isConstrained
Methods.Description: Returns true if the entity is constrained to something.

Methods.Name: isDormant
Methods.Description: Returns true if the entity is dormant, that is, outside the current PVS of the local player.

Methods.Name: isFlagSet
Methods.Description: Returns true if the given FL_* flag is set on the entity.

Parameters.Description: flag

Methods.Name: isPlayer
Methods.Description: Returns whether the given entity is a player.

Methods.Name: isValid
Methods.Description: Checks if an entity is valid.

ReturnValues.Description: True if valid, false if not

Methods.Name: isVehicle
Methods.Description: Returns whether the given entity is a vehicle.

Methods.Name: isWeapon
Methods.Description: Returns whether the given entity is a weapon.

Methods.Name: isWeldedTo
Methods.Description: Returns the first entity that is welded to the given entity.

Methods.Name: link
Methods.Description: Links two entities. One should be a resource node.

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: Second entity to link to.

ReturnValues.Description: True if succeeded, false and a failure reason otherwise.

Methods.Name: lookupBone
Methods.Description: Looks up the ID for an entity's bone using its nice name.

Parameters.Name: boneName
Parameters.Description: The name of the bone to lookup.

ReturnValues.Description: The ID of the bone, or nil if no such bone exists.

Methods.Name: lookupSequence
Methods.Description: Looks up the index of the given animation name.

Parameters.Name: name
Parameters.Description: Animation name.

Methods.Name: material
Methods.Description: Returns the entity's material.

Methods.Name: model
Methods.Description: Gets the model of an entity

Methods.Name: nocollide
Methods.Description: Creates a nocollide constraint between two entities.

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: Second entity.

Parameters.Name: bone1
Parameters.Description: Bone on the first entity, defaults to 0.

Parameters.Name: bone2
Parameters.Description: Bone on the second entity, defaults to 0.

Methods.Name: obbCenter
Methods.Description:
Returns the local position of the entity's outer bounding box 
Note that this will return a zero-vector on clientside models as it is derived from collision data.

ReturnValues.Description: The position vector of the outer bounding box center

Methods.Name: obbCenterW
Methods.Description:
Returns the world position of the entity's outer bounding box. 
Note that this will return the entity's position on clientside models as it is derived from collision data.

ReturnValues.Description: The position vector of the outer bounding box center in world space.

Methods.Name: obbMaxs
Methods.Description:
Returns the max of the entity's outer bounding box (local to the entity) 
Note that this will return a zero-vector on clientside models as it is derived from collision data.

ReturnValues.Description: The outer bounding box maximum

Methods.Name: obbMins
Methods.Description:
Returns the min of the entity's outer bounding box (local to the entity) 
Note that this will return a zero-vector on clientside models as it is derived from collision data.

ReturnValues.Description: The outer bounding box minimum

Methods.Name: obbSize
Methods.Description:
Returns the x, y, z size of the entity's outer bounding box (local to the entity) 
Note that this will return a zero-vector on clientside models as it is derived from collision data.

ReturnValues.Description: The outer bounding box size

Methods.Name: owner
Methods.Description: Gets the owner of the entity

Methods.Name: pos
Methods.Description: Returns the position of the entity

ReturnValues.Description: The position vector

Methods.Name: pullResource
Methods.Description: Pulls the given amount of resource from node 2 to node 1 instantly.

Parameters.Description: String resource name, e.g "energy", "oxygen" etc.

Parameters.Name: amount
Parameters.Description: Amount to transfer.

Methods.Name: pushResource
Methods.Description: Pushes the given amount of resource from node 1 to node 2 instantly.

Parameters.Name: resource
Parameters.Description: String resource name, e.g "energy", "oxygen" etc.

Parameters.Name: amount
Parameters.Description: Amount to transfer.

Methods.Name: remove
Methods.Description:
Removes the given entity. 
This requires the REMOVE_ENTITY permission when running in restricted mode (e.g. executed from the shared area).

Methods.Name: removeCallOnRemove
Methods.Description: Removes a registered removal callback.

Parameters.Name: identifier
Parameters.Description: The unique string identifier given to Entity:callOnRemove().

Methods.Name: removeCallback
Methods.Description: Removes a callback added by Entity:addCallback().

Parameters.Name: name
Parameters.Description: The name of the event to remove a callback for.

Parameters.Name: identifier
Parameters.Description: The identifier given when the callback was added.

Methods.Name: removeTrail
Methods.Description: Removes an entity's sprite trail.

Methods.Name: right
Methods.Description: Gets the entity's right direction vector.

Methods.Name: sequenceDuration
Methods.Description: Returns the duration of the current playing sequence (animation).

Methods.Name: setAlpha
Methods.Description:
Sets the alpha of the given entity without changing the colour. 
Note that this will automatically change the rendermode from ents.RENDERMODE_NORMAL to ents.RENDERMODE_TRANSCOLOR if alpha < 255.

Parameters.Name: alpha
Parameters.Description: The alpha value to set from 0 - 255.

Methods.Name: setAng
Methods.Description: Sets the entity's angles

Parameters.Name: ang
Parameters.Description: New angles

Methods.Name: setBodygroup
Methods.Description: Sets the sub model to be rendered under the given bodygroup of the given entity.

Parameters.Name: id
Parameters.Description: The ID of the body group to update (0-based).

Parameters.Name: subModelID
Parameters.Description: The ID of the sub model to use for the given body group (0-based).

Methods.Name: setCollisionGroup
Methods.Description: Sets the entity's collision group, which determines what it collides with.

Parameters.Name: group
Parameters.Description: COLLISION_GROUP_* enum.

Methods.Name: setColor
Methods.Description:
Sets the entity's colour. 
If you're changing alpha < 255, then you also need to update the rendermode to something like ents.RENDERMODE_TRANSCOLOR.

Parameters.Name: r
Parameters.Description: Either a colour object, or the red number value (default = 255).

Parameters.Name: g
Parameters.Description: Green value (default = 255, if R is a number).

Parameters.Name: b
Parameters.Description: Blue value (default = 255, if R is a number).

Parameters.Name: a
Parameters.Description: Alpha value (default = 255, if R is a number).

Methods.Name: setLocalAng
Methods.Description: Sets the entity's angles locally.

Parameters.Name: ang
Parameters.Description: Local angles.

Methods.Name: setMaterial
Methods.Description: Sets an entity's material.

Parameters.Name: material
Parameters.Description: The string path to the material.

Methods.Name: setModel
Methods.Description: Sets the model of the given entity (does not change physics!)

Parameters.Name: model
Parameters.Description: The path to the model to use.

Methods.Name: setNotSolid
Methods.Description: Sets the entity solid state

Parameters.Name: notsolid
Parameters.Description: Should the entity be not solid?

Methods.Name: setParent
Methods.Description: Parents the first entity to the second.

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: Entity to parent to. Pass nil to remove the entity's current parent.

Methods.Name: setPos
Methods.Description:
Sets the entitiy's position. 
When called on the instance owner and running in restricted mode (e.g. executed from the shared area), or when called on a player other than the instance owner, this requires the SET_PLAYER_POSITION permission.

Parameters.Name: pos
Parameters.Description: New position

Parameters.Name: flags
Parameters.Description: Optional bitflags that determine how the entity is moved (see ents.SET_POS_*).

ReturnValues.Description: True if moving the entity succeeded, false otherwise.
If moving the entity failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setPullRate
Methods.Description: Sets the pull rate (node 2 to node 1) on the given resource valve.

Parameters.Name: resource
Parameters.Description: String resource name, e.g "energy", "oxygen" etc.

Parameters.Name: rate
Parameters.Description: Rate to transfer resources.

Methods.Name: setPushRate
Methods.Description: Sets the push rate (node 1 to node 2) on the given resource valve.

Parameters.Name: resource
Parameters.Description: String resource name, e.g "energy", "oxygen" etc.

Parameters.Name: rate
Parameters.Description: Rate to transfer resources.

Methods.Name: setRenderMode
Methods.Description: Sets the render mode of the given entity.

Parameters.Name: mode
Parameters.Description: ents.RENDERMODE_* enum.

Methods.Name: setSkin
Methods.Description: Sets the skin of an entity.

Parameters.Name: skin
Parameters.Description: The skin index to use.

Methods.Name: setSubMaterial
Methods.Description: Sets an entity's sub-material.

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: The index of the sub-material, get it from getMaterials().

Parameters.Name: material
Parameters.Description: The material to replace the sub-material with.

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: The index of the sub-material, get it from getMaterials().

Parameters.Name: colour
Parameters.Description: The colour to set on the sub-material.

Methods.Name: skin

Methods.Name: spriteTrail

Parameters.Description: The colour of the trail.

Parameters.Name: startSize
Parameters.Description: The starting size of the trail.

Parameters.Name: endSize
Parameters.Description: The end size of the trail.

Parameters.Name: length
Parameters.Description: The length of the trail.

Parameters.Name: material
Parameters.Description: The material to use. Invalid materials will have no effect.

Methods.Name: toLocal
Methods.Description: Converts an angle or vector in world space to entity local space.

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: World space angle or vector.

ReturnValues.Description: The angle or vector corresponding to the given world value in local space.

Methods.Name: toWorld
Methods.Description: Converts an angle or vector in entity local space to world space.

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: Local space angle or vector.

ReturnValues.Description: The angle or vector corresponding to the given local value in world space.

Methods.Name: unconstrain
Methods.Description: Removes constraints from the given entity.

Parameters.Name: type
Parameters.Description: Optional type of constraint to remove. No type will remove all constraints.
