Methods.Description: Unlinks the given entity from its resource node.

ReturnValues.Description: True if succeeded, false and a failure reason otherwise.

Methods.Name: up
Methods.Description: Gets the entity's upward direction vector.

Methods.Name: vel
Methods.Description: Returns the velocity of the entity

ReturnValues.Description: The velocity vector

Methods.Name: waterLevel
Methods.Description: Returns the entity's water level.

ReturnValues.Description: 0 is not in water, 1 is slightly inside, 2 is mostly inside and 3 is completely inside.

Methods.Name: weldTo
Methods.Description: Creates a weld constraint between two entities.

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: Second entity.

Parameters.Name: bone1
Parameters.Description: Bone on the first entity, defaults to 0.

Parameters.Name: bone2
Parameters.Description: Bone on the second entity, defaults to 0.

Parameters.Name: noCollide
Parameters.Description: Limit on the weld before it breaks, default is 0 (no limit).

Parameters.Name: deleteEnt1OnBreak
Parameters.Description: Sets whether to delete the first entity when the weld breaks.

Classes.Name: Error
Classes.Description: Error type. Used to capture tracebacks and re-throw them later.

Methods.Name: getMessage
Methods.Description: Returns the message that the error was constructed with (usually a string, but can be any value).

Methods.Name: getTraceback
Methods.Description: Returns the traceback that was recorded at the point the error was constructed.

Methods.Name: close
Methods.Description: Closes the file. You cannot perform any further operations on the file once it is closed.

Methods.Name: flush
Methods.Description: Writes all buffered data to the file and saves it.

Methods.Name: lines
Methods.Description:
Returns an iterator over the file's contents, starting from the current position. 
Pass in read arguments to have them returned in the iteration.

Parameters.Description: Reading arguments to use. Defaults to "*l".

ReturnValues.Description: Iterator.

Methods.Name: read
Methods.Description: Reads the given file using the arguments.

Parameters.Description:
Reading arguments. '*n' reads a number, '*a' reads the entire file, 
'*l' reads a line without the new line character, '*L' reads a line with the new line character 
and a number reads that many bytes.

ReturnValues.Description: All read values.

Methods.Name: seek
Methods.Description: Moves the file cursor position for reading from. If no arguments are provided, it returns the current position. See: http://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#pdf-file:seek

Parameters.Name: whence
Parameters.Description: The position in the file to seek from (one of "set", "cur" or "end").

Parameters.Name: offset
Parameters.Description: If whence is provided, the offset from that given position to seek to.

ReturnValues.Description: The new file cursor position.

Methods.Name: setvbuf
Methods.Description: Sets the buffering mode for the given file. See: http://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html#pdf-file:setvbuf

Parameters.Name: mode
Parameters.Description: The mode to use for buffering (one of "no", "full" or "line").

Parameters.Name: size
Parameters.Description: The size of the buffer. Applicable only if mode = "full".

Methods.Name: write
Methods.Description: Writes every passed in value to the given file.

Parameters.Description: String or number values to write to the given file.

ReturnValues.Description: The file, allowing for chained calls.

Methods.Name: disableShading
Methods.Description: Disables or enables shading on a hologram.

Parameters.Name: disableShading
Parameters.Description: Whether to suppress shading on the hologram. Pass nil to reset to the default behaviour.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the shading was changed, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: drawModel
Methods.Description: Draws the hologram's model, if executing within a render callback.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the hologram was rendered, false otherwise.
If the hologram was not rendered, the reason why.

Methods.Name: followBone
Methods.Description: Parents this hologram to the specified entity's bone.

Parameters.Name: parent
Parameters.Description: The parent entity to attach to.

Parameters.Name: boneID
Parameters.Description: The ID of the bone to attach to.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the parent was changed, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: getBoneAngles
Methods.Description: Returns the angles value for the given bone, if the given bone is valid and this hologram belongs to the instance owner.

Parameters.Name: bone
Parameters.Description: Bone index or string name.

ReturnValues.Description: The angle value for the given bone, or nil if the angles are not available.
If the angles are not available, the reason why.

Methods.Name: getBoneOffset
Methods.Description: Returns the offset value for the given bone, if the given bone is valid and this hologram belongs to the instance owner.

Parameters.Name: bone
Parameters.Description: Bone index or string name.

ReturnValues.Description: The offset value for the given bone, or nil if the offset is not available.
If the offset is not available, the reason why.

Methods.Name: getBoneScale
Methods.Description: Returns the scale value for the given bone, if the given bone is valid and this hologram belongs to the instance owner.

Parameters.Name: bone
Parameters.Description: Bone index or string name.

ReturnValues.Description: The scale value for the given bone, or nil if the scale is not available.
If the scale is not available, the reason why.

Methods.Name: getClip
Methods.Description: Returns the clipping plane that was set on the given index, if it exists.

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: The unique index value, must be a number between 1 and 16.

ReturnValues.Description: The clipping plane for the given index, or nil if no plane has been set or is otherwise inaccessible.
If the clipping plane was nil, the reason why.

Methods.Name: getDTVar
Methods.Description:
Gets the value of the given DTVar, if this hologram belongs to the instance owner. 
For the index: Float, Int and Vector are between 1 and 31, Entity is between 1 and 30, String is between 1 and 4, all others are between 1 and 32.

Parameters.Name: type
Parameters.Description: The type of variable. Valid types are: Angle, Bool, Entity, Int, Float, String, Vector

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: The index of the DTVar.

ReturnValues.Description: The current value of the DTVar.

Methods.Name: getDefaultRenderBounds
Methods.Description: Returns the default render bounds based on the hologram's current model and scale.

ReturnValues.Description: The minimum bounds of the hologram's default render bounds, or nil if the render bounds are not available.
The maximum bounds of the hologram's default render bounds, or the reason why the bounds are not available.

Methods.Name: getFlexes
Methods.Description:
Returns a table of flexname -> flexid pairs for use in flex functions. 
These IDs become invalid when the hologram's model changes.

ReturnValues.Description: The flexes for this hologram, if available. Otherwise nil.

Methods.Name: getParent
Methods.Description: Returns the parent of this hologram, if it has one.

Methods.Name: getRenderBounds
Methods.Description: Returns the hologram's current render bounds, taking into account bounds set by Hologram:SetRenderBounds().

ReturnValues.Description: The minimum bounds of the hologram's current render bounds, or nil if the render bounds are not available.
The maximum bounds of the hologram's current render bounds, or the reason why the bounds are not available.

Methods.Name: getRenderGroup
Methods.Description: Returns the hologram's current render group, if set.

ReturnValues.Description: The render group of the hologram if set, or -1 if the engine is controlling the render group, or nil if the render group could not be retrieved.
If the render group could not be retrieved, the reason why.

Methods.Name: getScale
Methods.Description: Gets the current scale of the hologram, if this hologram belongs to the instance owner.

ReturnValues.Description: The scale assigned to the hologram (may be a number, vector or matrix).

Methods.Name: isBoneJiggle
Methods.Description: Returns the jiggle value for the given bone, if the given bone is valid and this hologram belongs to the instance owner.

Parameters.Name: bone
Parameters.Description: Bone index or string name.

ReturnValues.Description: The jiggle value for the given bone, or nil if the jiggle is not available.
If the jiggle is not available, the reason why.

Methods.Name: isDrawTranslucent
Methods.Description:
Indicates whether the hologram is set to render in the translucent render pass. 
Note that on the server this only represents the state of the networked flag, it does not account for any client-side override.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the hologram is set to render in the translucent render pass, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: isShadingDisabled
Methods.Description:
Indicates whether shading has been disabled for the given hologram. 
Note that on the server this only represents the state of the networked flag, it does not account for any client-side override.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the hologram has disabled shading, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: remove
Methods.Description: Removes the hologram from the world. It cannot be used again after this operation.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the hologram was removed, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: removeHalo
Methods.Description: Removes the halo from the hologram, if it has one.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the hologram had a halo which has been removed, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: setAngVel
Methods.Description: Sets the hologram's angular velocity.

Parameters.Name: angularVelocity
Parameters.Description: Angular velocity.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the angular velocity was changed, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setAnim
Methods.Description: Sets the animation for the given hologram.

Parameters.Name: anim
Parameters.Description: Animation enum/sequence number, or nil to stop animating.

Parameters.Name: cycle
Parameters.Description: (Optional) How far through the animation to start (between 0 and 1, default = 0).

Parameters.Name: rate
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Playback rate for the animation (default = 1).

ReturnValues.Description: True if the animation was changed, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setBoneAngles
Methods.Description: Sets the angles of the given hologram's bone.

Parameters.Name: bone
Parameters.Description: Bone index or string name.

Parameters.Name: angles
Parameters.Description: The angles (relative to the bone) to apply.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the angles were changed, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setBoneJiggle
Methods.Description: Sets whether the given bone has jiggle enabled or not.

Parameters.Name: bone
Parameters.Description: Bone index or string name.

Parameters.Name: enabled
Parameters.Description: True if the bone's jiggle should be enabled, false otherwise.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the bone jiggle was changed, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setBoneOffset
Methods.Description: Sets the position of the given hologram's bone.

Parameters.Name: bone
Parameters.Description: Bone index or string name.

Parameters.Name: offset
Parameters.Description: The offset (relative to the bone) to apply.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the offset was changed, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setBoneScale
Methods.Description: Sets the scale of a bone.

Parameters.Name: bone
Parameters.Description: Bone index or string name.

Parameters.Name: scale
Parameters.Description: Vector scale value.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the scale was changed, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setClip
Methods.Description:
Updates a clip plane, which cuts the hologram's visuals. 
Note that while the index range accepts up to 16 clips, the amount that can be drawn simultaneously depends on the client operating system. Windows can render up to 6 planes, while Linux/macOS can only render 2.

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: The unique index value, must be a number between 1 and 16.

Parameters.Name: enabled
Parameters.Description: Whether to enable or disable the clipping plane.

Parameters.Name: origin
Parameters.Description: The position of the clipping plane (required only if the plane is enabled).

Parameters.Name: normal
Parameters.Description: The normal pointing outwards from the clipping plane (required only if the plane is enabled).

Parameters.Name: localToEntity
Parameters.Description: If passed an entity, the clipping plane will be local to the given entity. If passed true, the clipping plane will be local to the hologram. Otherwise, the clipping plane will be in world space. Required only if the plane is enabled.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the clipping plane was changed, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setDTVar
Methods.Description:
Sets a DataTable variable on the hologram. These are automatically synced between the server and the client. 
For the index: Float, Int and Vector are between 1 and 31, Entity is between 1 and 30, String is between 1 and 4, all others are between 1 and 32. 
For entities, use ents.NULL_ENTITY to reset the value.

Parameters.Name: type
Parameters.Description: The type of variable. Valid types are: Angle, Bool, Entity, Int, Float, String, Vector

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: The index of the DTVar.

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: The value to set.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the DTVar was updated, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setDrawTranslucent
Methods.Description:
Sets whether the hologram should be forced to draw in the translucent render pass. 
Note that this occurs automatically if the hologram's alpha drops below 255. 
Consider using Hologram:setRenderGroup() instead for more granular control over rendering when using a render callback.

Parameters.Name: drawTranslucent
Parameters.Description: Boolean indicating whether to draw in the translucent pass. Pass nil to reset to the default behaviour.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the translucent flag was changed, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setFlexScale
Methods.Description: Sets the scale of all flexes of a hologram

Parameters.Name: scale
Parameters.Description: Scale to apply to flexes.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the flex scale was changed, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setFlexWeight
Methods.Description: Sets the weight (value) of a flex.

Parameters.Name: flexID
Parameters.Description: The index of the flex.

Parameters.Name: weight
Parameters.Description: The weight to set.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the flex weight was changed, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setLOD
Methods.Description: Sets the level of detail for the given hologram.

Parameters.Name: level
Parameters.Description: A number between 0 and 8 for fixed quality (0 being highest quality), or -1 for automatic based on distance.

ReturnValues.Description: True if setting the LOD succeeded, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setMaterialOverride
Methods.Description:
Sets the material to use to override the hologram's networked material name. This can be used to set a render target 
as the material for a hologram, which combined with setRenderMesh can allow for procedurally generated models. 
Note that setting a render target as the material requires the USE_RENDER_TARGET_MATERIALS_WITH_HOLOGRAMS permission. 
If the material being used is translucent or transparent, call Hologram:setDrawTranslucent( true ) and consider changing the render mode (e.g. to RENDERMODE_GLOW).

Parameters.Name: mat
Parameters.Description: The material instance (IMaterial or RenderTarget) to apply. Pass nil to remove the override.

ReturnValues.Description: True if setting the material override succeeded, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setModel
Methods.Description: Sets the model of the hologram.

Parameters.Name: model
Parameters.Description: Model path.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the model was changed, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setNoDraw
Methods.Description: Sets whether the hologram should be flagged as not drawing, which leaves it invisible.

Parameters.Name: noDraw
Parameters.Description: Whether the hologram should be flagged not to draw.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the no draw flag was changed, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setParent
Methods.Description: Parents this hologram to the specified entity.

Parameters.Name: parent
Parameters.Description: The parent entity to attach to.

Parameters.Name: attachment
Parameters.Description: Optional attachment name to attach to.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the parent was changed, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setPoseParameter
Methods.Description: Sets the pose parameter on the given hologram.

Parameters.Name: key
Parameters.Description: The pose parameter ID or name to set.

Parameters.Name: value
Parameters.Description: The value to set on the given pose parameter.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the pose was changed, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setRenderBounds
Methods.Description:
Sets an override on the hologram's render bounds, altering the hologram's visibility and available space to render additional elements in its render callback. 
These bounds will be clamped based on the maximum allowed hologram size. The returned bounds represent the final clamped bounds.

Parameters.Name: mins
Parameters.Description: The minimum bounds to set, or nil to reset to the default. Components of this vector must be less-equal the provided maximum bounds.

Parameters.Name: maxs
Parameters.Description: The maximum bounds to set, or nil to reset to the default. Components of this vector must be greater-equal the provided minimum bounds.

ReturnValues.Description: The new minimum bounds of the hologram's render bounds, or nil if the render bounds were not changed.
The new maximum bounds of the hologram's render bounds, or the reason why the render bounds were not changed.

Methods.Name: setRenderCallback
Methods.Description:
Sets or removes the render callback for the hologram. 
The provided callback function will be invoked with the hologram, and a bitflag indicating the type of rendering being performed (see render.STUDIO_*). 
Note that the hologram's model will not render unless the callback invokes Hologram:drawModel(). 
All clipping, lighting and material overrides will be applied to whatever is rendered in the callback. 
This callback may be invoked multiple times per frame, depending on the render group of the hologram, and whether water reflections are being drawn.

Parameters.Name: callback
Parameters.Description: The callback to set as the render callback, or nil to reset rendering back to the default behaviour.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the render callback was updated, false otherwise.
If the render callback was not updated, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setRenderFX
Methods.Description: Sets the render FX for the hologram.

Parameters.Name: effect
Parameters.Description: Effect index.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the render FX was changed, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setRenderGroup
Methods.Description:
Sets the render group in which the hologram will be drawn. 
This can be used to customise at what point in the rendering process the hologram's render callback is invoked. 
Note that Hologram:setDrawTranslucent() overrides this value.

Parameters.Name: renderGroup
Parameters.Description: The render group to set, or nil to reset to the default render group. If not nil, must be one of render.RENDERGROUP_OPAQUE, render.RENDERGROUP_TRANSLUCENT or render.RENDERGROUP_BOTH.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the render group was updated, false otherwise.
If the render group was not updated, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setRenderMesh
Methods.Description:
Sets a mesh to be used as the hologram's model. 
This will override the hologram's original model. Note that a material must be set, either on the server or by using setMaterialOverride(), otherwise the mesh will render as a wireframe. 
Make sure to set the original model to one with an idle sequence that has no rotation, otherwise the mesh will not render correctly. One such model is "models/error.mdl". 
Note that this requires the DRAW_MESH_HOLOGRAMS permission.

Parameters.Name: mesh
Parameters.Description: The IMesh to use as the hologram's model. Pass nil to remove the mesh.

ReturnValues.Description: True if setting the mesh succeeded, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setScale
Methods.Description: Sets the hologram scale, either a number, vector or matrix. Matrix scales use only the first 3x3 components.

Parameters.Name: scale
Parameters.Description: Either a number scale, vector scale or matrix scale.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the scale was changed, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setVel
Methods.Description: Sets the hologram's linear velocity.

Parameters.Name: velocity
Parameters.Description: Velocity to apply.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the velocity was changed, false otherwise.
If the operation failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: enableLooping
Methods.Description: Enables/disables looping. Only works on noblock channels.

Parameters.Name: enable
Parameters.Description: Should looping be enabled?

Methods.Name: fft
Methods.Description: Outputs FFT data to the Output table.

Parameters.Name: output
Parameters.Description: The table to place the FFT data into.

Parameters.Name: type
Parameters.Description: The type of FFT data to put into it. One of the sounds.FFT_* enums.

ReturnValues.Description: The number of levels put into the table.

Methods.Name: get3DCone
Methods.Description: Gets the 3D cone paramters for the sound.

ReturnValues.Description: Inner angle, outer angle and outer volume.

Methods.Name: get3DFadeDistance
Methods.Description: Gets the 3D fade distance for the sound.

ReturnValues.Description: Min distance, max distance.

Methods.Name: getAverageBitRate
Methods.Description: Gets the current average bit rate of the sound.

ReturnValues.Description: Bit rate.

Methods.Name: getBitsPerSample
Methods.Description: Gets the bits per sample for the file. Does not work for mp3 or ogg files.

ReturnValues.Description: Bits per sample.

Methods.Name: getFileName
Methods.Description: Gets the file name of the sound.

ReturnValues.Description: File name.

Methods.Name: getLength
Methods.Description: Gets the length of the sound.

ReturnValues.Description: Length of the sound.

Methods.Name: getLevel
Methods.Description: Gets the level of the sound for the left and right channels. Values are between 0 and 1.

ReturnValues.Description: Left sound level.
Right sound level.

Methods.Name: getPan
Methods.Description: Gets the current panning of the channel.

ReturnValues.Description: The panning of the channel, with -1 being left, 0 centre, and 1 right.

Methods.Name: getPlaybackRate
Methods.Description: Gets the playback rate of the sound.

ReturnValues.Description: Playback rate, 1 is normal speed.

Methods.Name: getPos
Methods.Description: Gets the position of the sound.

ReturnValues.Description: Position vector of the sound.

Methods.Name: getSamplingRate
Methods.Description: Gets the sampling rate of the sound in MHz. Should always be 44100.

ReturnValues.Description: Sampling rate.

Methods.Name: getState
Methods.Description: Gets the state of the sound as an enum.

ReturnValues.Description: State enum (one of sounds.GMOD_CHANNEL_*).

Methods.Name: getTime
Methods.Description: Gets the current time the sound is at.

ReturnValues.Description: Current time of the sound.

Methods.Name: getVolume
Methods.Description: Gets the current volume of the sound. Value is between 0 and 1.

ReturnValues.Description: Volume.

Methods.Name: is3D
Methods.Description: Returns whether the sound is 3D.

ReturnValues.Description: True if 3D, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: isBlockStreamed
Methods.Description: Returns whether the sound is block streamed.

ReturnValues.Description: True if block streamed, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: isLooping
Methods.Description: Returns whether the sound is looping.

ReturnValues.Description: True if looping, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: isValid
Methods.Description: Returns whether the sound channel is valid for use.

ReturnValues.Description: True if valid, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: pause
Methods.Description: Pauses the sound playback.

Methods.Name: play
Methods.Description: Resumes or starts the sound playback depending on if it was paused or stopped.

Methods.Name: set3DCone
Methods.Description: Sets the 3D cone for the sound. For all parameters, -1 leaves it at its current value.

Parameters.Name: innerAng
Parameters.Description: The inner angle. Range is 0 (no cone) to 360 (sphere).

Parameters.Name: outerAng
Parameters.Description: The outer angle. Range is 0 (no cone) to 360 (sphere).

Parameters.Name: outerVolume
Parameters.Description: The volume outside the projection cone. Range is 0 (silent) to 1 (same as inside cone).

Methods.Name: set3DEnabled
Methods.Description: Sets whether 3D playback is enabled.

Parameters.Name: enabled
Parameters.Description: Whether 3D playback should be enabled or not.

Methods.Name: set3DFadeDistance
Methods.Description: Sets the 3D fade distance for the sound.

Parameters.Name: min
Parameters.Description: Min distance from the sound to start fading.

Parameters.Name: max
Parameters.Description: Past this distance the sound stops fading.

Methods.Name: setPan
Methods.Description: Sets the panning of the channel.

Parameters.Name: panning
Parameters.Description: The panning to use as a number, with -1 being left, 0 centre, and 1 right.

Methods.Name: setPlaybackRate
Methods.Description: Sets the playback rate for the sound.

Parameters.Name: rate
Parameters.Description: Playback rate, 1 is normal speed, 0.5 is half speed etc.

Methods.Name: setPos
Methods.Description: Sets the position of the sound.

Parameters.Name: pos
Parameters.Description: The position in the world to place the sound.

Parameters.Name: dir
Parameters.Description: Optional direction to play the sound towards. Leave nil to have no direction.

Methods.Name: setTime
Methods.Description: Sets the time of the sound. You must have set 'noblock' in the flags for the sound.

Parameters.Name: time
Parameters.Description: The time in the sound to jump to.

Methods.Name: setVolume
Methods.Description: Sets the volume of the sound.

Parameters.Name: volume
Parameters.Description: The volume to set, value is a multiplier (0 = muted, 1 = 100%, 2 = 200% etc.)

Methods.Name: stop
Methods.Description: Stops the sound from playing, next time you call :play() it will restart from the beginning.

Methods.Name: getColor
Methods.Description:
Gets the colour of the given pixel co-ordinates on the texture. 
Only works for PNG files.

Parameters.Name: x
Parameters.Description: The x co-ordinate.

Parameters.Name: y
Parameters.Description: The y co-ordinate.

ReturnValues.Description: Pixel colour object.

Methods.Name: getFloat
Methods.Description: Gets the given float material parameter.

Parameters.Name: param
Parameters.Description: The string key to read.

ReturnValues.Description: Float value at the given key.

Methods.Name: getInt

Methods.Name: getMatrix

Parameters.Name: param
Parameters.Description: The string key to read.

ReturnValues.Description: VMatrix value at the given key.

Methods.Name: getName
Methods.Description: Gets the path of the material.

ReturnValues.Description: String path of the material.

Methods.Name: getShader
Methods.Description: Gets the shader the material is using.

ReturnValues.Description: String shader name, e.g "UnlitGeneric".

Methods.Name: getString
Methods.Description: Gets the given string material parameter.

Parameters.Name: param
Parameters.Description: The string key to read.

ReturnValues.Description: String value at the given key.

Methods.Name: getVector
Methods.Description: Gets the given vector material parameter.

Parameters.Name: param
Parameters.Description: The string key to read.

ReturnValues.Description: Vector value at the given key.

Methods.Name: getVectorLinear
Methods.Description: Gets the given linear vector material parameter.

Parameters.Name: param
Parameters.Description: The string key to read.

ReturnValues.Description: Vector value at the given key.

Methods.Name: height
Methods.Description: Returns the height of the material.

ReturnValues.Description: Height value.

Methods.Name: isError
Methods.Description: Returns whether the material has not loaded correctly (i.e doesn't exist).

ReturnValues.Description: True if the material invalid.

Methods.Name: width
Methods.Description: Returns the width of the material.

ReturnValues.Description: Width value.

Classes.Name: IMesh
Classes.Description: IMesh object, holds static mesh data.

Methods.Name: buildFromTriangles
Methods.Description: Builds the mesh from the given table of triangles.

The bounding box maximum.

Methods.Name: isValid

Methods.Name: facePos

The priority associated with the disposition type.

Methods.Name: getTarget

Classes.Name: Panel
Classes.Description:
Panel 
Note that most methods are from the panel object itself, so you'll need to look up GLua's documentation on panels to find them. 
There are a few methods that are not exposed, or are modified slightly. See the panel-specific types for modified methods.

Methods.Name: isValid
Methods.Description:
Returns whether the given panel is valid. 
This method allows the use of the global IsValid() helper on panels, as VGUI uses upper-case "IsValid" which is not checked by IsValid().

Methods.Name: replaceMethod
Methods.Description: Replaces the given method with your function.

Parameters.Name: name
Parameters.Description: The name of the method to replace.

Parameters.Name: replacementFunc
Parameters.Description: The function that should replace this method.

Methods.Name: addAngleVelocity
Methods.Description:
Adds the given angular velocity value to the current angular velocity. Each component applies the given magnitude 
to the entity's corresponding local axis, e.g. a value of Vector( 0, 0, 50 ) applies an angular velocity to rotate 
the entity around its up direction by 50 units/s.

Parameters.Name: angularVelocity
Parameters.Description: The angular velocity to add.

Methods.Name: applyForceCenter
Methods.Description: Applies force to the centre of the physics object.

Parameters.Name: force
Parameters.Description: Force vector to apply.

Methods.Name: applyForceOffset
Methods.Description: Applies force to the physics object from the given offset position.

Parameters.Name: force
Parameters.Description: Force vector to apply.

Parameters.Name: offset
Parameters.Description: Position in world space to apply from.

Methods.Name: applyTorqueCenter
Methods.Description: Applies torque to the centre of the physics object.

Parameters.Name: torque
Parameters.Description: The torque vector (where the direction is in world space and the magnitude's units are kg * degrees / s).

Methods.Name: enableCollisions
Methods.Description: Enables or disables collisions on the physics object.

Parameters.Name: enable
Parameters.Description: Boolean flag whether to enable.

Methods.Name: enableDrag
Methods.Description: Enables or disables drag on the physics object.

Parameters.Name: enable
Parameters.Description: Boolean flag whether to enable.

Methods.Name: enableGravity
Methods.Description: Enables or disables gravity on the physics object.

Parameters.Name: enable
Parameters.Description: Boolean flag whether to enable.

Methods.Name: enableMotion
Methods.Description: Enables or disables motion on the physics object.

Parameters.Name: enable
Parameters.Description: Boolean flag whether to enable.

Methods.Name: getAngleVelocity
Methods.Description: Returns the angular velocity of the physics object.

ReturnValues.Description: Angular velocity *VECTOR*.

Methods.Name: getAngles
Methods.Description: Returns the angles of the physics object.

ReturnValues.Description: Angle object.

Methods.Name: getContents
Methods.Description: Returns the contents of the physics object.

ReturnValues.Description: The contents flags applied to this physics object (see trace.CONTENTS_*).

Methods.Name: getDamping
Methods.Description: Returns damping values on the physics object.

ReturnValues.Description: Linear damping, angular damping.

Methods.Name: getEnergy
Methods.Description: Returns the kinetic energy of the physics object.

ReturnValues.Description: Kinetic energy value.

Methods.Name: getEntity
Methods.Description: Returns the entity this physics object belongs to.

ReturnValues.Description: The entity this physics object belongs to.

Methods.Name: getInertia
Methods.Description: Returns the inertia of the physics object.

ReturnValues.Description: Vector inertia.

Methods.Name: getInvInertia
Methods.Description: Returns 1 / the inertia of the physics object.

ReturnValues.Description: Inverse inertia vector.

Methods.Name: getInvMass
Methods.Description: Returns 1 / the physics object's mass.

ReturnValues.Description: Inverse mass.

Methods.Name: getMass
Methods.Description: Returns the mass of the physics object.

ReturnValues.Description: Mass value.

Methods.Name: getMassCenter
Methods.Description: Returns the mass centre of the physics object in local space.

ReturnValues.Description: Vector mass centre position in local space.

Methods.Name: getMaterial
Methods.Description: Returns the type of material the physics object represents, e.g "wood", "metal".

ReturnValues.Description: String material type. NOT the same as Entity:getMaterial().

Methods.Name: getPos
Methods.Description: Returns the position of the physics object.

ReturnValues.Description: Position vector.

Methods.Name: getPositionMatrix
Methods.Description:
Returns the physics object's world transform matrix. 
The first 3 columns are a rotation matrix representing the physics object's angles, and the last column is its position.

ReturnValues.Description: The physics object's world transform as a matrix.

Methods.Name: getRotDamping
Methods.Description: Returns the rotation damping value of the physics object.

ReturnValues.Description: Rotation damping value.

Methods.Name: getSpeedDamping
Methods.Description: Returns the speed damping value of the physics object.

ReturnValues.Description: Speed damping value.

Methods.Name: getStress
Methods.Description: Returns the stress being exerted on the physics object as a number value.

ReturnValues.Description: Stress value.

Methods.Name: getSurfaceArea
Methods.Description: Returns the total surface area of the physics object in units^2.

ReturnValues.Description: Surface area value.

Methods.Name: getVelocity
Methods.Description: Returns the velocity of the physics object.

ReturnValues.Description: Velocity vector.

Methods.Name: getVelocityAtPoint
Methods.Description: Returns the velocity in the object's local space at a specific point.

Parameters.Name: point
Parameters.Description: The point (in world space) to get the velocity at.

ReturnValues.Description: The velocity at the given point.

Methods.Name: getVolume
Methods.Description: Returns the total volume of the physics object in units^3.

ReturnValues.Description: Volume value.

Methods.Name: isAsleep
Methods.Description: Returns whether the physics object is "sleeping", that is, not being simulated.

ReturnValues.Description: True if sleeping, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: isCollisionEnabled
Methods.Description: Returns whether the physics object has collisions enabled.

ReturnValues.Description: True if collisions are enabled, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: isDragEnabled
Methods.Description: Returns whether the physics object has drag enabled.

ReturnValues.Description: True if drag is enabled, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: isGravityEnabled
Methods.Description: Returns whether the physics object has gravity enabled.

ReturnValues.Description: True if gravity is enabled, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: isMotionEnabled
Methods.Description: Returns whether the physics object has motion enabled, i.e whether it is not frozen.

ReturnValues.Description: True if motion is enabled, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: isMoveable
Methods.Description: Returns whether the physics object is moveable or not.

ReturnValues.Description: True if moveable, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: isPenetrating
Methods.Description: Returns whether the physics object is intersecting another or not.

ReturnValues.Description: True if intersecting, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: isValid
Methods.Description:
Returns whether the physics object is valid. 
Note that trying to use an invalid physics object will throw an error.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the physics object is valid, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: localToWorld
Methods.Description: Returns the input vector transformed from local space to world space.

Parameters.Description: vec

ReturnValues.Description: Translated and rotated vector.

Methods.Name: setMass
Methods.Description: Sets the mass of the physics object. Mass cannot exceed 50,000.

Parameters.Name: mass
Parameters.Description: The new mass to give the physics object, in kilograms.

Methods.Name: setVelocity
Methods.Description: Sets the linear velocity of the physics object.

Parameters.Name: velocity
Parameters.Description: Velocity vector in world space.

Methods.Name: worldToLocal
Methods.Description: Returns the input vector transformed from world space to local space.

Parameters.Description: vec

ReturnValues.Description: Translated and rotated vector.

Methods.Name: accountID
Methods.Description: Returns the player's account ID (SteamID3) as an integer.

Methods.Name: activeWeapon
Methods.Description: Returns the player's active weapon.

Methods.Name: aimEntity
Methods.Description: Returns the entity the player is looking at.

Parameters.Name: followCursor
Parameters.Description: Whether to follow the cursor for aiming in the context menu.

Methods.Name: aimPos
Methods.Description: Returns the position the player is looking at.

Parameters.Name: followCursor
Parameters.Description: Whether to follow the cursor for aiming in the context menu.

Methods.Name: aimVector
Methods.Description: Returns the player's aim vector.

Methods.Name: alive
Methods.Description: Returns whether the player is alive.

Methods.Name: armor
Methods.Description: Returns the player's armour.

Methods.Name: crouching
Methods.Description: Returns whether the player is crouching.

Methods.Name: deaths
Methods.Description: Returns the player's deaths count.

Methods.Name: exitVehicle
Methods.Description:
Forces a player to exit the vehicle they are currently sat in. 
This applies only to engine vehicles (e.g. chairs), not scripted vehicles, and can only be triggered for vehicles belonging to the instance owner. This also requires the CONTROL_VEHICLE_INPUT permission to have been granted by the player. 
Note that the "canexitvehicle" hook will be called by this method.

ReturnValues.Description: True if exiting the vehicle succeded, false otherwise.
If exiting failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: eyeTrace
Methods.Description: Returns an eye trace from the player ignoring the cursor position.

Parameters.Name: followCursor
Parameters.Description: Whether to follow the cursor for aiming in the context menu.

Methods.Name: flashlightIsOn
Methods.Description: Returns whether the player's flashlight is on.

Methods.Name: fov
Methods.Description: Returns the player's FOV.

Methods.Name: frags
Methods.Description: Returns the player's frag count.

Methods.Name: getAmmoCount
Methods.Description: Returns the amount of ammo the player currently has of the given type.

Parameters.Name: ammoType
Parameters.Description: The type of ammo, get this from Weapon:getPrimaryAmmoType()/Weapon:getSecondaryAmmoType().

Methods.Name: getEntityInUse
Methods.Description: Returns the entity the player is currently using (e.g. a picked up prop).

ReturnValues.Description: The entity currently being used by the given player.

Methods.Name: getFriendStatus
Methods.Description: Returns the friend status code for the client running this function.

Methods.Name: getShuttle
Methods.Description: Returns the shuttle the player is currently seated in.

Methods.Name: getTeamColor
Methods.Description: Returns the player's team colour.

Methods.Name: getTool
Methods.Description: Returns the tool object belonging to the player for the given tool mode, or nil if the player has no such tool.

Parameters.Name: toolMode
Parameters.Description: The name of the tool mode to retrieve a tool object for.

Methods.Name: getUserGroup
Methods.Description: Returns the player's user group.

Methods.Name: getViewEntity
Methods.Description: Returns the current view entity of the given player.

Methods.Name: hasDamageEnabled
Methods.Description: Returns whether the given player is opted into prop damage.

Methods.Name: hasPlayerDamageEnabled
Methods.Description: Returns whether the given player is opted into player damage.

Methods.Name: health
Methods.Description: Returns the player's health.

Methods.Name: inShuttle
Methods.Description: Returns true if the player is seated in a shuttle, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: inVehicle
Methods.Description: Returns true if the player is in a vehicle.

Methods.Name: isAdmin
Methods.Description: Returns true if the player is an admin.

Methods.Name: isBot
Methods.Description: Returns true if the player is a bot.

Methods.Name: isConnected
Methods.Description: Returns true if the player is connected.

Methods.Name: isFrozen
Methods.Description: Returns true if the player is frozen.

Methods.Name: isMuted
Methods.Description: Returns whether the player is muted for the client running this function.

Methods.Name: isSprinting
Methods.Description: Returns whether the player is sprinting.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the player is sprinting, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: isSuperAdmin
Methods.Description: Returns true if the player is a super admin.

Methods.Name: isUserGroup
Methods.Description: Returns true if the player is in the given user group.

Parameters.Description: group

Methods.Name: jumpPower
Methods.Description: Returns the player's jump power.

Methods.Name: keyDown
Methods.Description: Returns whether the player is holding the given key down.

Parameters.Name: key
Parameters.Description: input.IN_ enum.

Methods.Name: keyReleased
Methods.Description: Returns whether the player has released the given key.

Parameters.Name: key
Parameters.Description: input.IN_ enum.

Methods.Name: maxArmor
Methods.Description: Returns the player's maximum armour.

Methods.Name: maxHealth
Methods.Description: Returns the player's max health.

Methods.Name: maxSpeed
Methods.Description: Returns the player's max speed.

Methods.Name: name
Methods.Description: Returns the player's name.

Methods.Name: nick
Methods.Description: Returns the player's name.

Methods.Name: ping
Methods.Description: Returns the player's ping.

Methods.Name: runSpeed
Methods.Description: Returns the player's run speed.

Methods.Name: setVelocity
Methods.Description: Sets a player's velocity.

Parameters.Name: velocity
Parameters.Description: The new (world) velocity vector.

Methods.Name: shootPos
Methods.Description: Returns the player's shoot position.

Methods.Name: steamID
Methods.Description: Returns the player's Steam ID.

Methods.Name: steamID64
Methods.Description: Returns the player's 64 bit Steam ID.

Methods.Name: team
Methods.Description: Returns the player's team ID.

Methods.Name: teamName
Methods.Description: Returns the player's team name.

Methods.Name: uniqueID
Methods.Description: Returns the player's unique ID.

Methods.Name: userID
Methods.Description: Returns the player's user ID.

Methods.Name: vehicle
Methods.Description: Returns the vehicle the player is in if applicable.

Classes.Name: Quaternion
Classes.Description:
Quaternion 
Fields are stored as r, i, j, k components respectively and can be indexed with 1-4 or the field's name.

Fields.Name: r
Fields.Description: The real part of the quaternion.

Fields.Name: i
Fields.Description: The first imaginary part of the quaternion.

Fields.Name: j
Fields.Description: The second imaginary part of the quaternion.

Fields.Name: k
Fields.Description: The third imaginary part of the quaternion.

Fields.Name: w
Fields.Description: The real part of the quaternion (alias of 'r').

Fields.Name: x
Fields.Description: The first imaginary part of the quaternion (alias of 'i').

Fields.Name: y
Fields.Description: The second imaginary part of the quaternion (alias of 'j').

Fields.Name: z
Fields.Description: The third imaginary part of the quaternion (alias of 'k').

Methods.Name: abs
Methods.Description: Returns the absolute value of the quaternion.

Methods.Name: conj
Methods.Description: Returns the conjugate of the quaternion.

ReturnValues.Description: The conjugate of this quaternion as a new quaternion.

Methods.Name: exp
Methods.Description: Returns Euler's constant e raised to the power of the quaternion.

ReturnValues.Description: e raised to the power of the given quaternion.

Methods.Name: forward
Methods.Description: Returns a vector pointing forward, relative to the rotation represented by the quaternion.

ReturnValues.Description: Forward vector.

Methods.Name: inv
Methods.Description: Returns the inverse of the quaternion.

ReturnValues.Description: The inverse of this quaternion as a new quaternion.

Methods.Name: mod
Methods.Description: Changes the quaternion so that the represented rotation is by an angle between 0 and 180 degrees.

ReturnValues.Description: A new quaternion with a normalised angle.

Methods.Name: right
Methods.Description: Returns a vector pointing right, relative to the rotation represented by the quaternion.

ReturnValues.Description: Right vector.

Methods.Name: setUnpacked
Methods.Description: Sets the values of the quaternion.

Parameters.Name: real
Parameters.Description: The real part to set.

Parameters.Name: i
Parameters.Description: The i component value to set.

Parameters.Name: j
Parameters.Description: The j component value to set.

Parameters.Name: k
Parameters.Description: The k component value to set.

ReturnValues.Description: The quaternion.

Methods.Name: unpack
Methods.Description: Returns the 4 components of the quaternion.

Methods.Name: up
Methods.Description: Returns a vector pointing up, relative to the rotation represented by the quaternion.

ReturnValues.Description: Up vector.

Operators.Name: unm_Quaternion_nil
Operators.Description: Negates the given quaternion.

ReturnValues.Description: A new quaternion whose values are all the negation of the original's.

Operators.Name: add_Quaternion_Quaternion
Operators.Description: Adds the given quaternions.

ReturnValues.Description: A new quaternion whose values are the sum of those in the given quaternions.

Operators.Name: add_Quaternion_number
Operators.Description: Adds the given number to the real part of the given quaternion.

ReturnValues.Description: A new quaternion whose real part is the original's plus the number. The other components are unchanged.

Operators.Name: sub_Quaternion_number
Operators.Description: Subtracts the given number from the real part of the given quaternion.

ReturnValues.Description: A new quaternion whose real part is the original's minus the number. The other components are unchanged.

Operators.Name: sub_number_Quaternion
Operators.Description: Takes the given quaternion's values away from the given number.

ReturnValues.Description: A new quaternion whose real part is the number minus the quaternions real part, and the rest of the components negated.

Operators.Name: mul_Quaternion_number
Operators.Description: Multiplies the given quaternion's components by the given number.

ReturnValues.Description: A new quaternion whose components are the original's multiplied by the given number.

Operators.Name: mul_Quaternion_Quaternion
Operators.Description: Multiplies two quaternions, this operation is NOT commutative.

ReturnValues.Description: A new quaternion whose components are the Hamilton product of the originals.

Operators.Name: mul_Quaternion_Vector
Operators.Description: Multiplies a quaternion with a vector, this operation is NOT commutative.

ReturnValues.Description:
A new quaternion whose components are the Hamilton product of the original quaternion and the vector, 
where the vector is taken to be a quaternion with real part 0 and imaginary parts equal to the vector's values.

Operators.Name: div_Quaternion_number
Operators.Description: Divides the components of the given quaternion by the given number.

ReturnValues.Description: A new quaternion whose components are the original's divided by the given number.

Operators.Name: div_number_Quaternion
Operators.Description: Multiplies the inverse of the quaternion by the given number.

ReturnValues.Description: A new quaternion that is the result of multiplying the inverse of the right hand quaternion by the given number.

Operators.Name: div_Quaternion_Quaternion
Operators.Description: Multiplies the left quaternion by the inverse of the right quaternion.

ReturnValues.Description: A new quaternion that is the result of multiplying the left quaternion by the inverse of the right.

Operators.Name: pow_Quaternion_number
Operators.Description: Exponentiates the left quaternion by the given value.

ReturnValues.Description: A new quaternion that is the result of raising the left quaternion to the given power.

Operators.Name: pow_number_Quaternion
Operators.Description: Exponentiates the number by the given quaternion.

ReturnValues.Description: A new quaternion that is the result of raising the number to the power of the quaternion.

Operators.Name: eq_Quaternion_Quaternion
Operators.Description: Equality is considered to be the component-wise differences all being less than a small delta value (1e-7).

ReturnValues.Description: True if the given quaternions are equal accounting for minute floating point error.

Methods.Name: addMaterialFlags
Methods.Description: Adds the given material flags to the render target.

Parameters.Name: materialFlags
Parameters.Description: The flags to add (see rendertarget.MATERIAL_FLAG_*).

ReturnValues.Description: The material flags set on the render target after adding the given flags.

Methods.Name: draw
Methods.Description:
Draws the texture itself at the given position and with given width/height. 
Note that width/height values different from the texture size will stretch the texture, not resize it. 
Also, note that if the shader is not rendertarget.SHADER_UNLIT_GENERIC, nothing will be drawn.

Parameters.Name: x
Parameters.Description: X position to draw.

Parameters.Name: y
Parameters.Description: Y position to draw.

Parameters.Name: w
Parameters.Description: Width.

Parameters.Name: h
Parameters.Description: Height.

Methods.Name: drawToTexture
Methods.Description: Runs the given function asynchronously in a separate drawing context. Anything drawn in the function is rendered to the RT texture.

Parameters.Name: func
Parameters.Description: A function to be executed to draw to the texture.

Methods.Name: freeRT
Methods.Description:
Frees the given RT for use by another entity, allowing you to allocate a new one. 
Do not attempt to draw/update it after freeing or your chip will error.

Methods.Name: getMemoryUsageMiB
Methods.Description: Returns the amount of graphics memory being consumed by this render target, in MiB.

Methods.Name: getShader
Methods.Description: Returns the name of the shader being used to render this render target (one of the rendertarget.SHADER_* enums).

Methods.Name: hasMaterialFlags
Methods.Description:
Returns true if the render target has all the given material flags enabled. 
Multiple flags can be tested at once using bit.bor().

Parameters.Name: materialFlags
Parameters.Description: The material flag(s) to check for.

Methods.Name: height
Methods.Description: Returns the height of the render target in pixels.

Methods.Name: isMaterialLinearWrite
Methods.Description: Returns whether the render target's material is set to use linear writes.

Methods.Name: isValid
Methods.Description:
Returns true if the render target is still valid for drawing. 
Note that render targets may be forcibly freed if the graphics memory quota is reduced.

Methods.Name: removeMaterialFlags
Methods.Description: Removes the given material flags from the render target.

Parameters.Name: materialFlags
Parameters.Description: The flags to remove (see rendertarget.MATERIAL_FLAG_*).

ReturnValues.Description: The material flags set on the render target after removing the given flags.

Methods.Name: setMaterialLinearWrite
Methods.Description:
Sets whether the render target's material should be drawn using linear pixel writes (i.e. without gamma correction). 
This applies to drawing the render target as a material and applying a colour modulation in a 2D surface context, such as on a screen or within another render target. This does **not** affect render operations applied within the render target itself. 
The render target must be using the rendertarget.SHADER_UNLIT_GENERIC shader to apply this setting.

Parameters.Name: linearWrite
Parameters.Description: Whether the render target's material should use linear writes.

Methods.Name: setShader
Methods.Description:
Sets the shader to use to render this render target in future rendering operations. 
Note that 2D rendering can only use rendertarget.SHADER_UNLIT_GENERIC. Attempting to render in 2D with a different shader will fail. 
Also, note that material flags are separate between shaders, so they should be configured after calling this method.

Parameters.Name: shaderType
Parameters.Description: The shader to use (see rendertarget.SHADER_*).

Methods.Name: width
Methods.Description: Returns the width of the render target in pixels.

Methods.Name: divertPowerTo
Methods.Description: Diverts power to the given entity from this node based on a given multiplier value.

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: The entity to divert power to.

Parameters.Name: multiplier
Parameters.Description: The multiplier to use to divert power (within the limits returned by getPowerDivertMultiplierLimit()).

ReturnValues.Description: True if diverting power succeeded, false otherwise.
If diverting failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: getDivertedPower
Methods.Description: Returns the current power divert multiplier from this node to the given entity.

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: The entity to get the power divert multiplier for.

ReturnValues.Description: The current power divert multiplier for the given entity, or nil if no power has been diverted.

Methods.Name: getLinkedEntities
Methods.Description: Returns a list of all entities that are currently linked to this node.

Methods.Name: getMaximumPowerToDivert
Methods.Description:
Returns the maximum amount of power that can be diverted from this node in addition to its baseline load. 
This may be less than the value returned by getRemainingPowerToDivert() if entities have had their power reduced.

Methods.Name: getPowerDivertMultiplierLimit
Methods.Description: Returns the minimum and maximum multiplier values that can be used to divert power from this node.

ReturnValues.Description: The minimum multiplier value permitted.
The maximum multiplier value permitted.

Methods.Name: getRemainingPowerToDivert
Methods.Description:
Returns the remaining amount of power that can be diverted to entities on this node. 
Each entity that exceeds a multiplier of 1 will reduce this value, while every entity that has a multiplier less than 1 will increase it. 
For example, an entity with a multiplier of 1.25 will reduce this value by 0.25.

Methods.Name: isLinkAllowed
Methods.Description: Returns whether the given entity can be linked to this node.

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: The entity to check.

Methods.Name: isLinked
Methods.Description: Returns whether the given entity has been linked to this node.

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: The entity to check.

Classes.Name: SensorArray
Classes.Description: Interface into a TSCM sensor array.

Methods.Name: deepScanTarget
Methods.Description:
Deep scans the given target entity (if it is scannable), and returns a table describing its current state. 
The exact format of the scan output depends on the entity being scanned. This operation is separate to the sensor's current target, which is continuously updated. Deep scans return more information, such as type information on weapons. 
Each successful deep scan performed costs energy (see SensorArray:getDeepScanEnergyCost()) and has a maximum range (see SensorArray:getMaxDeepScanRange()).

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: The entity to be scanned.

ReturnValues.Description: A table of information describing the entity, if it could be scanned, or nil if the entity was not scannable.
If the entity could not be scanned, the reason why.

Methods.Name: getDeepScanEnergyCost
Methods.Description: Returns the energy cost to perform a deep scan.

Methods.Name: getMaxDeepScanRange
Methods.Description: Returns the maximum range at which an entity can be deep-scanned by this sensor array.

Methods.Name: getTarget
Methods.Description: Returns the sensor array's current target entity.

Methods.Name: getTargetHealth
Methods.Description: Returns the health of the sensor array's current target.

Methods.Name: getTargetMaxHealth
Methods.Description: Returns the maximum health of the sensor array's current target.

Methods.Name: setTarget
Methods.Description: Sets the current scan target on the sensor array.

Parameters.Name: ent
Parameters.Description: The entity to set as the current target.

ReturnValues.Description: True if setting the target succeeded, false otherwise.
If setting the target failed, the reason why.

Methods.Name: addPlayersToWhitelist
Methods.Description: Adds the given list of players to the shield's whitelist.

Parameters.Name: players
Parameters.Description: A list of players to be added.

Methods.Name: divertPower
Methods.Description: Sets the face to divert power to.

Parameters.Name: face
Parameters.Description: -1 distributes equally, 0 disables diversion, 1-6 match front, back, left, right, up, down.

Methods.Name: getDivertedFace
Methods.Description: Gets the face index that the shield is currently diverting power to.

ReturnValues.Description: The face index that is currently being diverted to, if the shield has faces and access is allowed. -1 indicates that power is being distributed evenly, 0 indicates no diversion, and 1-6 correspond to front, back, left, right, up, down respectively.
If the face index is nil, the reason why.

Methods.Name: getFaceCharge
Methods.Description: Returns the current charge of the given face.

Parameters.Name: face
Parameters.Description: The face to check, 1-6 match front, back, left, right, up, down.

Methods.Name: getMaxCharge
Methods.Description: Returns the maximum possible charge of the shield emitter.

Methods.Name: getRealTransferRate
Methods.Description: Returns the current rate energy can be transferred between faces per transfer tick.

Methods.Name: getSizeMultiplier
Methods.Description: Returns the current size multiplier, if one is set. A zero-vector indicates no multiplier has been applied.

Methods.Name: getTotalCharge
Methods.Description: Returns the current total charge of the shield emitter.

Methods.Name: getTransferRate
Methods.Description: Returns the maximum base rate energy can be transferred between faces per transfer tick.

Methods.Name: getTransferTickInterval
Methods.Description: Returns the time (in seconds) between shield facing power transfers (the transfer ticks).

Methods.Name: getType
Methods.Description: Returns the type (number identifier) of the shield emitter.

Methods.Name: hasFaces
Methods.Description: Returns whether the shield has faces or not.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the shield has faces.

Methods.Name: hasWhitelist
Methods.Description: Indicates whether the shield can whitelist certain players or not.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the shield can whitelist players, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: isActive
Methods.Description: Returns whether the shield is currently active.

Methods.Name: removePlayersFromWhitelist
Methods.Description: Removes the given list of players from the shield's whitelist.

Parameters.Name: players
Parameters.Description: A list of players to be removed.

Methods.Name: setActive
Methods.Description: Sets whether the shield should be active.

Parameters.Name: active
Parameters.Description: Boolean to indicate whether to activate or deactivate the shield.

Methods.Name: setSizeMultiplier
Methods.Description: Sets the size multiplier to use on the shield emitter.

Parameters.Name: sizeMultiplier
Parameters.Description: The size multiplier to use. Each component is clamped to be between 0.75 and 1. A nil value resets the multiplier.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the size multiplier was updated, false otherwise.
If the size multiplier was not updated, the reason why.

Methods.Name: addTarget
Methods.Description: Adds a target player/Steam ID to the given ship core.

Parameters.Name: plyOrSteamID
Parameters.Description: The player/Steam ID to add. If a Steam ID, the corresponding player must be connected.

Methods.Name: enableEmitters
Methods.Description: Enables or disables any pulsar emitters under control of the given core.

Parameters.Name: bool
Parameters.Description: Boolean enable.

Methods.Name: fireRepairBeam
Methods.Description: Sets the repair beam to fire or not.

Parameters.Name: fire
Parameters.Description: Should the repair beam fire?

Methods.Name: getShield
Methods.Description: Gets the shield assigned to the ship core.

ReturnValues.Description: The starship, Asgard or Atlantis shield assigned to the ship core.

Methods.Name: getShipName
Methods.Description:
Returns the name assigned to the ship core, if any. 
The name is taken from the life support core associated with this ship core.

Methods.Name: getShipSize
Methods.Description:
Gets the mins and maxs of the ship in co-ordinates local to the core's shield emitter. 
Make sure the core has a starship or Atlantis shield, or it will return 0,0,0.

ReturnValues.Description: Mins vector (local to the shield emitter).
Maxs vector (local to the shield emitter).

Methods.Name: getTargets
Methods.Description: Returns a list of all selected target Steam IDs on the ship core.

ReturnValues.Description: The list of selected target Steam IDs on the ship core if they are available, or nil otherwise.
If the list is nil, the reason why.

Methods.Name: isTarget
Methods.Description: Returns whether the given player/Steam ID is a target.

Parameters.Name: plyOrSteamID
Parameters.Description: The player/Steam ID to check.

Methods.Name: platingStrength
Methods.Description: Returns the current strength of the ship core's plating.

Methods.Name: removeTarget
Methods.Description: Removes a target player/Steam ID from the given ship core.

Parameters.Name: plyOrSteamID
Parameters.Description: The player/Steam ID to remove. If a Steam ID, the corresponding player must be connected.

Methods.Name: selfDestruct
Methods.Description: Triggers the ship core's self destruct. There's no going back.

Methods.Name: setEnvironmentSound
Methods.Description: Sets a sound that will play to the LS core environment assigned to the ship core.

Parameters.Name: soundPath
Parameters.Description: The path to the sound to play.

Parameters.Name: volume
Parameters.Description: (Optional) The volume of the sound, between 0 and 1. Defaults to 1.

Parameters.Name: pitch
Parameters.Description: (Optional) The pitch of the sound, between 0 and 255. Defaults to 100.

Methods.Name: setRepairTarget
Methods.Description: Sets the repair beam target.

Parameters.Name: target
Parameters.Description: Entity to repair.

Methods.Name: stopEnvironmentSound
Methods.Description: Stops any environment sound that was playing.

Methods.Name: getMaxShieldCharge
Methods.Description: Returns the maximum charge for the shuttle's shields.

Methods.Name: getName
Methods.Description: Returns the name assigned to the shuttle, if any.

Methods.Name: getPassenger
Methods.Description: Returns the passenger at the given index in the shuttle.

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: The index of the passenger to return (1-based).

Methods.Name: getPassengerCount
Methods.Description: Returns the number of passenger seats available in the shuttle.

Methods.Name: getPassengerSeatPoint
Methods.Description: Returns the position and angles of the passenger seat position at the given index.

Parameters.Name: index
Parameters.Description: The index of the passenger seat position and angles to return (1-based).

ReturnValues.Description: The position of the seat position in world space.
The angles of the seat position in world space.

Methods.Name: getPilot
Methods.Description: Returns the pilot of the shuttle.

Methods.Name: getRegistryNumber
Methods.Description: Returns the registry number of the ship the shuttle is associated with, if any.

Methods.Name: getShipName
Methods.Description: Returns the name of the ship the shuttle is associated with, if any.

Methods.Name: getTotalShieldCharge
Methods.Description: Returns the current state of charge for the shuttle's shields.

Methods.Name: isShieldActive
Methods.Description: Returns true if the shuttle's shield is active, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: isPlaying
Methods.Description: Checks if the sound is playing

ReturnValues.Description: True if the sound is playing, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: isValid
Methods.Description: Checks if the given sound is valid.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the sound is valid, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: play
Methods.Description: Plays the sound.

Methods.Name: setLevel
Methods.Description:
Sets the sound level. This determines the sound attenuation. Only works when the sound is not playing. 
See https://developer.valvesoftware.com/wiki/Soundscripts#SoundLevel for values and more info 
(use decibel value from the 'code' column, not the actual enum or 'value' column).

Parameters.Name: level
Parameters.Description: The sound level to set (between 10 and 511).

Methods.Name: setPitch
Methods.Description: Sets the sound pitch

Parameters.Name: pitch
Parameters.Description: The sound pitch as a percent from 0 to 255

Parameters.Name: delta
Parameters.Description: (Optional) The transition time between the current pitch and the new one (in seconds).

Methods.Name: setVolume
Methods.Description: Sets the sound volume

Parameters.Name: vol
Parameters.Description: Volume as a percent between 0 and 1

Parameters.Name: delta
Parameters.Description: (Optional) The transition time between the current volume and the new one (in seconds).

Methods.Name: stop
Methods.Description: Stops or fades out the sound.

Parameters.Name: fade
Parameters.Description: (Optional) Time, in seconds, to fade out the sound. Not given = stop immediately

Methods.Name: accessList
Methods.Description: Restricts access to this gate to players/Steam IDs in the given list.

Parameters.Name: list
Parameters.Description: A table of players and/or Steam IDs to allow. Anyone else is blocked.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the access list is applied.
If the access list is not applied, the reason why.

Methods.Name: active
Methods.Description: Gets whether this gate is active.

ReturnValues.Description: True if this gate is currently active, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: address
Methods.Description: Gets this gate's address if it is visible.

ReturnValues.Description: The address of this gate, if it is visible. Otherwise an empty string.

Methods.Name: addressList
Methods.Description: Gets a list of diallable addresses from this gate.

ReturnValues.Description: A table containing a list of gate addresses that are available to dial.

Methods.Name: asuranBeam
Methods.Description: Gets whether an Asuran weapon is firing through this gate.

ReturnValues.Description: True if an Asuran weapon is being fired through this gate, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: blocked

ReturnValues.Description: True if this gate is set to be blocked, false otherwise.

Parameters.Description: The gate being dialled.

ReturnValues.Description: True if it can be dialled, false otherwise.
If the target cannot be dialled, the reason why.

Methods.Name: chevron

Methods.Name: chevrons

Methods.Name: dhd

If the gate was not dialled, the reason why.

Methods.Name: gateRing

ReturnValues.Description: The entity that represents the ring on this gate, if it exists.

Methods.Name: getSignal

Methods.Name: group

Methods.Name: iris
Methods.Description: Gets the iris on this gate, if it has one.

Methods.Name: name

Methods.Name: setBlocked

Parameters.Name: blocked

ReturnValues.Description: True if the blocked state was set, false otherwise.
If the blocked state was not set, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setGroup
Methods.Description: Sets the group of this Stargate.

Parameters.Name: group

ReturnValues.Description: True if the group was set, false otherwise.
If the group was not set, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setLocal
Methods.Description: Sets the local state of this Stargate.

Parameters.Name: local
Parameters.Description: Boolean local.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the local state was set, false otherwise.
If the local state was not set, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setName
Methods.Description: Sets the name of this Stargate.

Parameters.Name: name
Parameters.Description: Name to set.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the name was set, false otherwise.
If the name was not set, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setPrivate
Methods.Description: Sets the private state of this Stargate.

Parameters.Name: private
Parameters.Description: Boolean private.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the private state was set, false otherwise.
If the private state was not set, the reason why.

Methods.Name: setSGC
Methods.Description: Sets the SGC mode of this Stargate.

Parameters.Name: sgc
Parameters.Description: Boolean SGC mode.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the SGC mode was set, false otherwise.
If the SGC mode was not set, the reason why.

Methods.Name: sgc
Methods.Description: Gets this gate's SGC mode state (applicable to milky-way gates only).

ReturnValues.Description: True if this gate is set to use SGC dialling mode, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: stop
Methods.Description: Removes a gate network listener from this gate.

Parameters.Name: function
Parameters.Description: Function to remove.

Methods.Name: stopCodeListen
Methods.Description: Removes a listener for iris codes from this gate.

Parameters.Name: function
Parameters.Description: Function to remove.

Methods.Name: stopTeleListen
Methods.Description: Removes a listener for teleporting entities from this gate.

Parameters.Name: function
Parameters.Description: Function to remove.

Methods.Name: target
Methods.Description: Gets the gate connected to this gate.

ReturnValues.Description: The gate that is connected to this gate (either inbound or outbound).

Methods.Name: transmit

Methods.Name: getOptions
Methods.Description:
Returns the current options for the tool. 
The exact format of the options depends on the tool mode. 
Note that the returned data may not immediately reflect changes made by setOptions() due to the use of networked convar values.

ReturnValues.Description: The current options for the tool, or nil if they cannot be retrieved.
If the tool options are nil, the reason why.

Methods.Name: getPlayer
Methods.Description: Returns the player that owns the tool object.

Methods.Name: isConfigurable
Methods.Description: Returns true if the tool can be confgiured using the setOptions() method.

Methods.Name: isValid
Methods.Description: Returns true if this tool object is valid for use, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: setOptions
Methods.Description:
Passes the given options table to the tool. 
The exact format of the options depends on the tool mode.

Parameters.Name: options
Parameters.Description: The options to pass to the tool.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the tool accepted the options and applied them, false if the options were rejected.
If the options were rejected, the reason why.

Methods.Name: add
Methods.Description: Adds the given matrix component-wise to this matrix.

Parameters.Name: matrix
Parameters.Description: The matrix to add to this matrix.

ReturnValues.Description: This matrix.

Methods.Name: getAngles
Methods.Description: Returns the matrix's angles

Methods.Name: getField
Methods.Description: Returns the value in the given row and column. There are 4 rows and 4 columns.

Parameters.Name: row
Parameters.Description: The row to retrieve the value from.

Parameters.Name: column
Parameters.Description: The column to retrieve the value from.

ReturnValues.Description: The number value at the given row and column.

Methods.Name: getForward
Methods.Description:
Returns the forward direction of the matrix. 
This is the first column of the matrix excluding the 4th component.

ReturnValues.Description: A vector representing the forward direction.

Methods.Name: getInverse
Methods.Description:
Returns the inverse matrix of the given matrix. 
This operation will fail for matrices with determinant close to or equal to 0.

ReturnValues.Description: The matrix representing the inverse of the given matrix, or nil if the inverse could not be computed.

Methods.Name: getInverseTR
Methods.Description:
Returns the inverse matrix of the given matrix. 
This function will not fail, but only works correctly on matrices that contain only translation and/or rotation.

ReturnValues.Description: The matrix representing the inverse of the given matrix, assumming the matrix was not scaled.

Methods.Name: getRight
Methods.Description:
Returns the right direction of the matrix. 
This is the second column of the matrix excluding the 4th component.

ReturnValues.Description: A vector representing the right direction.

Methods.Name: getScale
Methods.Description: Returns the matrix's scale

Methods.Name: getTranslation
Methods.Description: Returns the matrix's translation

Methods.Name: getTransposed
Methods.Description: Returns a copy of the matrix with its rows tranposed (every row becomes a column).

Methods.Name: getUp
Methods.Description:
Returns the up direction of the matrix. 
This is the third column of the matrix excluding the 4th component.

ReturnValues.Description: A vector representing the up direction.

Methods.Name: identity
Methods.Description:
Resets the matrix's values to the identity matrix. 
Modifies the given matrix directly.

ReturnValues.Description: This matrix.

Methods.Name: invert
Methods.Description:
Inverts the given matrix, modifying in-place. 
This operation will fail for matrices with determinant close to or equal to 0. If it does fail, the matrix is not modified.

ReturnValues.Description: This matrix.

Methods.Name: invertTR
Methods.Description:
Inverts the given matrix, modifying in-place. 
This function will not fail, but only works correctly on matrices that contain only translation and/or rotation.

ReturnValues.Description: This matrix.

Methods.Name: isIdentity
Methods.Description: Returns whether the given matrix is the identity matrix.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the matrix is the identity matrix, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: isRotationMatrix
Methods.Description:
Returns whether the given matrix is a "rotation matrix". 
This checks whether the forward, right and up directions are orthogonal and normalised to 1.

Methods.Name: mul
Methods.Description: Post-multiplies this matrix by the given matrix (i.e. self * matrix), setting this matrix's fields to the result.

Parameters.Name: matrix
Parameters.Description: The matrix to post-multiply by.

ReturnValues.Description: This matrix.

Methods.Name: rotate
Methods.Description: Rotates the matrix by the given angle. This is equivalent to post-multiplying by a rotation matrix, but without creating a new matrix object.

Parameters.Description: Angle to rotate by.

ReturnValues.Description: This matrix.

Methods.Name: scale

Parameters.Description: Scale vector.

ReturnValues.Description: This matrix.

Methods.Name: scaleTranslation
Methods.Description: Scales the absolute translation of the matrix by the given scale.

Parameters.Name: scale
Parameters.Description: Amount to scale by.

ReturnValues.Description: This matrix.

Methods.Name: set

Parameters.Description: The matrix to copy values from.

ReturnValues.Description: This matrix.

Parameters.Name: ang

ReturnValues.Description: This matrix.
