Operators.Name: mul_VMatrix_VMatrix
Operators.Description: Multiplies a matrix by another matrix.

ReturnValues.Description: A new matrix that is the result of the matrix product.

Operators.Name: mul_VMatrix_Vector
Operators.Description: Multiplies a vector by a matrix.

ReturnValues.Description: A new vector that is the result of the matrix by column vector multiplication.

Operators.Name: add_VMatrix_VMatrix
Operators.Description: Adds the values of 2 matrices component-wise.

ReturnValues.Description: A new matrix whose components are the sum of the components of the 2 matrices.

Operators.Name: sub_VMatrix_VMatrix
Operators.Description: Subtracts component values of the right matrix from those in the left matrix.

ReturnValues.Description: A new matrix whose components are the left matrix minus the right matrix.

Operators.Name: eq_VMatrix_VMatrix
Operators.Description: Determines if the given matrices are equal by checking each component.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the 2 matrices have the same component values, false otherwise.

Fields.Name: x
Fields.Description: The x co-ordinate of the vector (also available at index 1).

Fields.Name: y
Fields.Description: The y co-ordinate of the vector (also available at index 2).

Fields.Name: z
Fields.Description: The z co-ordinate of the vector (also available at index 3).

Methods.Name: Add
Methods.Description: Adds the given argument vector's component values to the given vector, modifying in-place.

Parameters.Name: vector
Parameters.Description: The vector to add.

ReturnValues.Description: The modified vector.

Methods.Name: Angle
Methods.Description: Returns an Angle object representing the vector as a forward direction, using Vector( 0, 0, 1 ) as up.

Methods.Name: AngleEx
Methods.Description: Returns an Angle object representing the vector as a forward direction, using the given up direction.

Parameters.Name: up
Parameters.Description: The up direction to use.

Methods.Name: Cross
Methods.Description: Returns the cross product of the two vectors as a new vector.

Parameters.Name: vector
Parameters.Description: The vector to take the cross product with.

Methods.Name: DistToSqr
Methods.Description: Returns the squared distance between the two vectors. This avoids the cost of a square-root.

Parameters.Name: vector
Parameters.Description: The vector to get the squared distance from.

Methods.Name: Distance
Methods.Description: Returns the distance between the two vectors.

Parameters.Name: vector
Parameters.Description: The vector to get the distance from.

Methods.Name: Div
Methods.Description: Divides the vector by the given argument value, modifying in-place.

Parameters.Name: divisor
Parameters.Description: The number or vector to divide the vector's components by.

ReturnValues.Description: The modified vector.

Methods.Name: Dot
Methods.Description: Returns the dot product of the two vectors.

Parameters.Name: vector
Parameters.Description: The vector to get the dot product with.

Methods.Name: GetNormalized
Methods.Description: Returns a new vector that has the same direction as the given vector, but with length equal to 1.

Methods.Name: IsEqualTol
Methods.Description: Returns whether the given vectors are equal, using the given tolerance value.

Parameters.Name: vector
Parameters.Description: The vector to compare against.

Parameters.Name: tolerance
Parameters.Description: The tolerance value to use to consider the vectors equal.

Methods.Name: IsZero
Methods.Description: Returns true if the given vector has all components equal to 0.

Methods.Name: Length
Methods.Description: Returns the length of the vector.

Methods.Name: Length2D
Methods.Description: Returns the 2-dimensional length of the vector (ignoring the z component).

Methods.Name: Length2DSqr
Methods.Description: Returns the squared 2-dimensional length of the vector (ignoring the z component).

Methods.Name: LengthSqr
Methods.Description: Returns the squared length of the vector.

Methods.Name: Mul
Methods.Description: Multiplies the given vector by the given number, vector (component-wise) or VMatrix, modifying in-place.

Parameters.Name: multiplier
Parameters.Description: The value to multiply the vector's components with.

ReturnValues.Description: The modified vector.

Methods.Name: Normalize
Methods.Description: Normalises the given vector's length to equal 1, modifying in-place.

ReturnValues.Description: The normalised vector.

Methods.Name: Rotate
Methods.Description: Rotates the given vector by the given angles, modifying in-place.

Parameters.Name: angles
Parameters.Description: The angles to rotate the vector with.

Methods.Name: Set
Methods.Description: Sets the vector's component values to equal the given vector, modifying in-place.

Parameters.Name: vector
Parameters.Description: The vector to take the component values from.

ReturnValues.Description: The modified vector.

Methods.Name: SetUnpacked
Methods.Description: Sets the vector's component values, modifying in-place.

Parameters.Name: x
Parameters.Description: The x-component value.

Parameters.Name: y
Parameters.Description: The y-component value.

Parameters.Name: z
Parameters.Description: The z-component value.

ReturnValues.Description: The modified vector.

Methods.Name: Sub
Methods.Description: Subtracts the given argument vector, modifying in-place.

Parameters.Name: vector
Parameters.Description: The vector to subtract.

ReturnValues.Description: The modified vector.

Methods.Name: ToColor
Methods.Description: Converts the vector into a colour by multiplying each component by 255.

ReturnValues.Description: A colour representing the vector.

Methods.Name: ToScreen
Methods.Description: Returns the position on the local player's screen that corresponds to this vector as a world position.

Methods.Name: ToTable
Methods.Description: Returns the vector as an array of 3 elements.

Methods.Name: Unpack
Methods.Description: Returns the component values of the vector.

ReturnValues.Description: The x-component.
The y-component.
The z-component.

Methods.Name: WithinAABox
Methods.Description: Returns true if the given vector is within the axis-aligned bounding box defined by the given vector arguments.

Parameters.Name: mins
Parameters.Description: The minimum corner of the box.

Parameters.Name: maxs
Parameters.Description: The maximum corner of the box.

Methods.Name: Zero
Methods.Description: Sets all component values to 0, modifying in-place..

ReturnValues.Description: The modified vector.

Operators.Name: mul_Vector_number
Operators.Description: Multiplies a vector by a scalar value.

ReturnValues.Description: A new vector whose values are equal to those in the original vector multiplied by the given number.

Operators.Description: Multiplies a vector component-wise by the given other vector.

ReturnValues.Description: A new vector whose values are equal to those in the original vector multiplied by the respective component of the other vector.

Operators.Name: div_Vector_number
Operators.Description: Divides a vector by a scalar value.

ReturnValues.Description: A new vector whose values are equal to those in the original vector divided by the given number.

Operators.Name: div_Vector_Vector
Operators.Description: Divides a vector component-wise by another vector.

ReturnValues.Description: A new vector whose values are equal to those in the original vector divided by the respective component of the other vector.

Operators.Name: add_Vector_Vector
Operators.Description: Adds two vectors.

ReturnValues.Description: A new vector whose values are the sum of the values in the given vectors.

Operators.Name: sub_Vector_Vector
Operators.Description: Subtracts one vector's values from another.

ReturnValues.Description: A new vector whose values are the values in the first vector minus those in the second.

Operators.Name: unm_Vector_nil
Operators.Description: Returns the negation of the given vector.

ReturnValues.Description: A new vector whose values are the negation of the given vector's.

Operators.Name: eq_Vector_Vector
Operators.Description: Determines if the given vectors are equal by checking each component.

ReturnValues.Description: True if the 2 vectors have the same component values, false otherwise.

Methods.Name: addOutputCallback

Adds an output change callback to the wirelink. This is called whenever an output changes. 
It is passed the wirelink, output name and new output value.

Parameters.Name: id

Parameters.Name: callback
Parameters.Description: The function to run when an output changes.

Methods.Name: entity
Methods.Description: Returns the entity that the wirelink represents.

Methods.Name: getOutputConnections
Methods.Description: Returns the connections that have been made to the given wire output, or nil if no connections have been made.

Parameters.Name: outputName
Parameters.Description: The name of the output to get the connections for.

Methods.Name: getWiredTo

Parameters.Name: inputName
Parameters.Description: The name of the input to get the entity for.

ReturnValues.Description: The entity wired to the given input, if the input exists and is wired. Otherwise nil.

Methods.Description: Returns the name of the output the given input is wired to.

ReturnValues.Description: The name of the output the given input is wired to, if the input exists and is wired. Otherwise nil.

Parameters.Description: The name of the output to get the type of.

Parameters.Description: The ID you used to register it.

Directives.Name: autoupdate
Directives.Description:
Marks the entity to automatically update its files. 
If --@updater is set, the newest version in the shared area will be used both after spawning normally and from the duplicator if it is available (with read access). 
Otherwise, the newest version stored in the owner's local files will be used.

Parameters.Name: path
Parameters.Description: An optional path to the file in the shared area that this file should be updated with on upload. This can be used to create a local alias for a shared file at a different path.

Directives.Name: class
Directives.Description:
Sets what type of entity is allowed to run this script. 
Attempting to spawn the script on an entity that is not in the list will result in an error message. 
Valid classes are: processor, screen, hud, emitter. 
In addition, if the value is set to "none", the script will be blocked from spawning with any type. This can be useful for library files.

Parameters.Name: classes
Parameters.Description: A list of comma separated classes to allow to run this script.

Directives.Name: dependency
Directives.Description:
Add a dependency on a shared file or folder. 
Files will be pulled in from the shared area belonging to the specified Steam ID, or your own if no Steam ID is given. 
Note that if you do not have permission to read a file, you'll get an error. 
When requesting folders, only 'async' and 'sv' are applicable to configure how the files are networked, all files must be manually executed. Also note that folders are not included recursively.
Directives.Usage:
--@dependency lib/myServerLibrary.txt sv 
--@dependency STEAM_0:0:1234 lib/theirUsefulLib.txt sh 
--@dependency coolstuff/requiredlater.txt 
--@dependency morestuff/in/a/folder sv 
--@dependency secret/example.txt async dev 
 
-- lib/myServerLibrary.txt is loaded on the server 
-- lib/theirUsefulLib.txt is loaded shared from STEAM_0:0:1234's shared folder. 
-- coolstuff/requiredlater.txt is included but not loaded yet. 
-- All files directly inside 'morestuff/in/a/folder' are included on the server only, but not loaded. 
-- secret/example.txt is included but not downloaded by clients until required, and will be uploaded from local 
-- files if available. 
if SERVER then 
	require( "coolstuff/requiredlater.txt" ) 
elseif ents.owner() == ents.player() then 
	require( "secret/example.txt" ) 
end

Parameters.Name: steamID
Parameters.Description: Steam ID whose shared folder the file/folder should be pulled from.

Parameters.Name: path
Parameters.Description: The path to the file/folder you want to include.

Parameters.Name: modifier
Parameters.Description: Optional modifier to determine how a file is loaded or a folder's files are networked. One of 'sv', 'sh', 'cl' or 'async' for server auto-require, shared auto-require, client auto-require and asynchronous include respectively.

Parameters.Name: mode
Parameters.Description: Optional mode to resolve the dependency with. Specify 'dev' to first attempt to load a local file with the given name. By default, local files are ignored.

Directives.Name: highres
Directives.Description:
Sets whether the screen should be high resolution. 
High resolution screens are 1024 x 1024, while normal resolution screens are 512 x 512.

Directives.Name: include
Directives.Description:
Mark a file to be included in the upload. 
This is required to use the file in require() and dofile()

Parameters.Name: path
Parameters.Description: Path to the file.

Directives.Name: includeasync
Directives.Description: Mark a file to be included in the upload but not downloaded to clients until executed by require() or dofile().
Directives.Usage:
--@includeasync example.txt 
 
if ents.player() == ents.owner() then 
	print( time.curTime(), "Loading example file asynchronously..." ) 
 
	-- This will yield until the file has been downloaded. 
	require( "example.txt" ) 
 
	print( time.curTime(), "Example file loaded." ) 
end 
-- CODE

Parameters.Name: path
Parameters.Description: Path to the file.

Directives.Name: includedir
Directives.Description: Mark a folder to be included in the upload. This is not recursive.

Parameters.Name: path
Parameters.Description: Path to the folder.

Directives.Name: includedirasync
Directives.Description: Mark a folder to be included in the upload but not downloaded to clients until each file is executed by require() or dofile(). This is not recursive.
Directives.Usage:
--@includedirasync lib 
 
if ents.player() == ents.owner() then 
	require( "lib/someLibrary.txt" ) 
	require( "lib/someOtherLibrary.txt" ) 
end 
-- CODE

Parameters.Name: path
Parameters.Description: Path to the folder.

Directives.Name: includesv
Directives.Description: Mark a file to be included in the upload, but not sent to clients for download. Use this for files that will only be used on the server.
Directives.Usage:
--@includesv lib/someLibrary.txt 
if SERVER then 
	require( "lib/someLibrary.txt" ) 
end 
-- CODE

Parameters.Name: path
Parameters.Description: Path to the file.

Directives.Name: mainfile
Directives.Description:
Marks the script as only runnable as the main file. 
Any instance attempting to load with this script as an include rather than the main script will error before running.
Directives.Usage:
--@mainfile 
-- This can only be executing if the file is the mainfile. 
assert( _MAINFILE == starfall.getMainFileName() )

Directives.Name: model
Directives.Description: Sets the model of a processor entity only.

Parameters.Name: path
Parameters.Description: Path of the model to use.

Directives.Name: name
Directives.Description:
Set the name of the script. 
This will become the name of the tab and will show on the overlay of the processor

Parameters.Name: name
Parameters.Description: Name of the script.

Directives.Name: precache
Directives.Description:
Precaches a sound or model file. 
This ensures that playing the sound or creating a prop with the model does not use a large amount of CPU time.

Parameters.Name: paths
Parameters.Description: A comma-separated list of file paths (.wav, .ogg, .mp3, .mdl) to precache.

Directives.Name: require
Directives.Description: Mark a file to be included in the upload and then required on both the server and the client.
Directives.Usage:
--@require lib/someLibrary.txt 
-- Your file is already required and ready to use here.

Parameters.Name: path
Parameters.Description: Path to the file.

Directives.Name: requirecl
Directives.Description: Mark a file to be included in the upload and then required on the client only.
Directives.Usage:
--@requirecl lib/someLibrary.txt 
if CLIENT then 
-- Your file is already required and ready to use here. 
end

Parameters.Name: path
Parameters.Description: Path to the file.

Directives.Name: requiresv
Directives.Description: Mark a file to be included in the upload and then required on the server only. This file will not be sent to clients.
Directives.Usage:
--@requiresv lib/someLibrary.txt 
if SERVER then 
-- Your file is already required and ready to use here. 
end

Parameters.Name: path
Parameters.Description: Path to the file.

Directives.Name: resolution
Directives.Description:
Sets the screen resolution in pixels. 
Valid values are 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024 or 2048 for each dimension. The default resolution is 512 x 512. 
Higher resolutions can be more costly to render, especially for lower-end systems.

Parameters.Name: width
Parameters.Description: The width of the screen in pixels.

Parameters.Name: height
Parameters.Description: The height of the screen in pixels.

Directives.Name: shared
Directives.Description:
For screens, HUDs and emitters, make the script run on the server alongside the client. 
You can use "if SERVER then" and "if CLIENT then" to determine if the script is currently being run on the server or the client, respectively.
Directives.Usage:
--@shared 
 
if SERVER then 
	-- Do important calculations 
	-- Send net message 
else 
	-- Display result of important calculations 
end

Directives.Name: updater
Directives.Description:
Sets the Steam ID of the shared area folder to check for updates against. 
This allows users of your script to right click the file in the editor and choose "Update". 
They will then download the latest version of this file and all included files from your shared folder.

Parameters.Name: steamID
Parameters.Description: The SteamID of the player responsible for updating this script.

Directives.Name: widescreen
Directives.Description:
Sets whether the screen should be widescreen. 
Widescreen screens are 1024 x 512, while normal resolution screens are 512 x 512.
